prs.title: feat(docs): DW Operations Hub dashboard (cso-toolkit hosted, GitHub Pages)
prs.body:
## Summary

Adds a single-page sector progress dashboard for the DW-Production / cso-toolkit ecosystem, served via GitHub Pages from `docs/dashboard/` and refreshed nightly by `.github/workflows/dashboard.yml` (06:00 UTC cron).

## Why this lives on cso-toolkit

- **Free hosting**: public repo gets unlimited GitHub Actions minutes and free GitHub Pages.
- **Cross-cutting**: the dashboard reports on toolkit + DW-Production + Teams snapshots together. cso-toolkit is the neutral cross-cutting home — DW-Production is sector-pipeline-only.
- **Operator-visible secrets stay off DW-Production**: only a read-scoped PAT (`DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN`) crosses the boundary, in one direction.

## Required org-admin action: cross-repo PAT secret

A new repository secret on `cso-toolkit` must be set before the nightly cron will succeed:

| Secret name | Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN` | `repo:read` on `unicef-drp/DW-Production` only | Lets `collect.R` pull PRs / branches / issues / milestones from the private DW-Production repo |

Org admin (@karavan88) must create a PAT with read-only scope on DW-Production and set it as a repository secret on cso-toolkit. Until then, the `dw_production` block in `state.json` is recorded as `reachable: false` (graceful degrade — dashboard still renders).

## Eight tabs

1. **Landing** — KPIs, pipeline-phase distribution, activity feed, watch list of stalled sectors.
2. **Sectors** — per-sector cards (status, rows, indicators, wall-time, fixes, toolkit findings, blockers) + five summary charts.
3. **Pipeline phases** — kanban: Production / Review / Publishing / Live.
4. **Branches** — cso-toolkit branches table (name, link, SHA, protected flag).
5. **Issues** — cso-toolkit issues grouped by milestone (v0.4.6 / v0.5.0 / unlabelled), severity badge.
6. **DBM actions** — TODO / IN-PROGRESS / DONE kanban sourced from `docs/dashboard/data/actions/<id>.yml`.
7. **History** — line charts of state over time (placeholder day-1; grows nightly).
8. **cso-toolkit** — toolkit version per branch, open v0.4.6 issues, cycle burndown.

## Nightly cron

`.github/workflows/dashboard.yml` runs at 06:00 UTC daily plus on push-to-`develop` / `main` and `workflow_dispatch`:

1. `Rscript docs/dashboard/collect.R` — assembles `state.json` from SDMX (rsdmx + cache fallback), cso-toolkit `gh api`, DW-Production `gh api`, and operator snapshots.
2. `Rscript docs/dashboard/render.R` — inlines five SVG charts + data-flow diagram, writes self-contained `index.html`.
3. Commits refreshed artifacts back to the branch with `[skip ci]`.
4. Publishes `docs/` to GitHub Pages.

## Coexistence with pkgdown

Per Phase 6 discovery (2026-05-30): cso-toolkit currently has no pkgdown workflow (only `r-check.yml`), so `dashboard.yml` owns Pages deployment and publishes the full `docs/` tree.

If pkgdown is added later, the conflict resolution is documented inline at the top of `dashboard.yml`:

- pkgdown takes over the Pages deploy (it rebuilds the whole site).
- `dashboard.yml` drops its `deploy` job and only commits refreshed files back to the branch.
- The pkgdown workflow must use `clean: false` so the `docs/dashboard/` subtree survives pkgdown rebuilds.

## Open v0.4.6 issues surfaced by the dashboard

The Issues tab and the DBM-actions kanban both surface the current open v0.4.6 blockers, with one DBM action YAML per issue under `docs/dashboard/data/actions/`:

- [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) — standalone-source `%>%` gate should use `.cso_require()` envelope
- [#52](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/52) — manifest sha256 normalisation (Windows CRLF / LF cross-platform drift)
- [#53](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/53) — re-export `dw_root()` public wrapper around `.dw_root_for()`
- [#54](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/54) — `dw_save` reviewer-mode mode-lock missed OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path

## Companion PR on DW-Production

The operator-side Teams snapshot script feeds this dashboard. Lives on DW-Production because it reads a Teams path only the operator can see:

- [unicef-drp/DW-Production — feat(snapshots): operator-side Teams snapshot script](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/new/docs/operator-snapshot) (linked after merge)

## Test plan

- [ ] Org admin sets `DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN` repository secret
- [ ] `gh workflow run dashboard.yml` succeeds (or wait for next 06:00 UTC tick)
- [ ] GitHub Pages serves the dashboard at the published URL
- [ ] All 8 tabs render; the DW-Production block in `state.json` is non-empty
- [ ] Operator runs `Rscript snapshots/snapshot-teams.R` (DW-Production companion PR) and commits the resulting snapshots to cso-toolkit; dashboard re-renders with fresh data on the next cron

prs.title: release: cut v0.4.6 (#51 + #52 + #53 + #54)
prs.body:
## Summary

Cuts cso-toolkit **v0.4.6** — a corrective release driven by the 2026-05-30 HVA + ED audit incident, plus three smaller correctness fixes batched into the same cycle.

The headline is **#54 (HIGH severity)**: `dw_is_canonical()` failed to recognise the OneDrive-mounted Teams `Documents` path on Windows, so reviewer-mode writes silently fell through and *overwrote canonical Teams artefacts* during back-to-back audit runs. Mode-lock is now tight.

Closes:
- [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) — `.cso_require` envelope on `%>%` standalone-source gate
- [#52](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/52) — `.gitattributes` force LF on R/Rd/YAML/MD/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION
- [#53](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/53) — re-export `dw_root()` public wrapper around `.dw_root_for()`
- [#54](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/54) — `dw_is_canonical` recognises OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path **(HIGH — mode-lock regression)**

## Why #54 is the headline

Empirical evidence from the **2026-05-30 HVA + ED audit runs**: two consecutive reviewer-mode audits *did not trigger* the mode-lock hard-stop, and instead wrote to the producer-canonical Teams path on disk. Root cause: on this Windows + OneDrive layout the canonical Teams root resolves through `…/OneDrive - UNICEF/Documents/…` rather than the bare UNC mount, and the prior `dw_is_canonical()` predicate only matched the latter. The producer/reviewer contract memo ([feedback_reviewer_mode_read_write_contract](https://github.com/jpazvd/...)) was correct; the predicate was the leak.

After this PR the OneDrive-mounted path is recognised as canonical, so reviewer-mode writes correctly hit the hard-stop with the explanatory warning.

## Migration notes

- **Consumers**: `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.6")` brings in the new `dw_root()` public wrapper *and* the LF-normalised file set.
- **One-time hash drift**: any consumer repo with cached CRLF manifests will see a one-time hash drift after upgrade (R/Rd/YAML/MD/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION re-normalised to LF via `.gitattributes`). This is expected; re-pin the manifest after first pull on v0.4.6.
- No public-API removals. `dw_root()` is additive.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check passes on all 4 OS/R combos (ubuntu/macos × R-release/R-devel)
- [ ] New `test-dw-is-canonical.R` passes (covers OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path recognition)
- [ ] Existing `test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` + `test-dw_publish.R` adjustments are green
- [ ] No new lintr / styler diffs in the changed `R/*.R` files
- [ ] Reviewer sector lead (DW-Production MNCH) signs off before merge

## Do not merge

This PR is **gated on sector lead review** (per `feedback_explicit_ack_before_dev_merge`). Do not squash-merge on the strength of green CI alone.

---

Generated as part of the v0.4.6 release cycle. Parent tracker: v0.4.6 milestone.

prs.title: docs: rename third role INGESTOR -> PUBLISHER (DBM/DBR/DBP)
prs.body:
## Summary

Renames the third data-warehouse role **INGESTOR → PUBLISHER** across the docs, and spells out the role acronyms **DBM / DBR / DBP**. **Docs-only** — no code, no exported-API change, and no mode-contract value changed (the contract still exposes `producer` / `reviewer`; a `publisher` mode remains future scope, #15).

### Why

The code already commits to the verb: there is a `dw_publish()` helper (and `[cso_toolkit.dw_publish]` error envelopes), but **no `dw_ingest()`**. The role's own gloss was already "warehouse publisher", so "INGESTOR" contradicted both the code and its own definition. `PUBLISHER` aligns the label with the verb, and **DBM / DBR / DBP** is a clean acronym set (the README role table already labelled PRODUCER as "DBM").

### Mapping

- **DBM** = Database Manager → PRODUCER
- **DBR** = Database Reviewer → REVIEWER
- **DBP** = Database Publisher → PUBLISHER (was INGESTOR)

### Files (7)

- `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` — rename across prose, both mermaid diagrams, the folder-layout map, and the role / helper / boundary tables; adds a DBM/DBR/DBP acronym legend + acronyms in the role matrix
- `README.md` — mermaid node label + edge verb (`ingest` → `publish`); role-table rows gain `(DBR)` / `(DBP)`; See-also reference
- `r/DESCRIPTION`, `docs/git_workflow.md`, `docs/toolkit_strategy.md`, `templates/dbm_submission_template.md` — role-triplet references
- `NEWS.md` — `## Unreleased` entry (historical entries left intact)

### Out of scope (kept surgical)

- No `DESCRIPTION` version bump — change logged under `## Unreleased`.
- Wiring a `publisher` `dw_mode` value into the contract — future scope (#15).
- Internal mermaid node IDs (`IN_PUB` / `IN_RAW`) keep their names (invisible identifiers).

## Test plan

- [ ] `grep -ri "ingestor"` over `docs/`, `README.md`, `r/DESCRIPTION`, `templates/` returns only the two historical `NEWS.md` entries
- [ ] mermaid diagrams in `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` + `README.md` still render (node `I` now reads PUBLISHER; edge verb `publish`)
- [ ] no `.R` / `.py` / `.ado` files touched (docs-only)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

prs.title: release: v0.4.5 → main
prs.body:
## Summary

Promotes v0.4.5 from `develop` to `main`. All v0.4.5 milestone work merged:

  - PR #47 → #46 (standalone-source `%>%` binding + magrittr declared in Imports)
  - PR #48 → #42 (8 `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the remaining un-prefixed exports)
  - PR #49 → release stamp (Version 0.4.5; dw_io.R header + .Rd regen; NEWS reorganisation)

No new code lands in this PR — it is the standard develop→main release-promotion PR.

## What ships in v0.4.5

### Public-surface additions (non-breaking)

8 new `dw_*` aliases via roxygen `@rdname`, sharing the same man page as their un-prefixed counterparts:

  - `dw_aggregate_data`
  - `dw_generate_agg_footnote`
  - `dw_apply_time_window`
  - `dw_generate_markdown_report`
  - `dw_process_all_csv_files`
  - `dw_create_profile`
  - `dw_review_profile`
  - `dw_test_scripts`

### Behavioural changes

  - `aggregate_data.R` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` now safe to `source()` standalone — neither `library(dplyr)` nor `library(magrittr)` required first.
  - `magrittr` declared as a first-class Import; NAMESPACE carries `importFrom(magrittr,"%>%")`. R CMD check no longer relies on transitive resolution through dplyr.

### Version-stamp consistency

All five live version-stamped sites read `0.4.5`:

  - `r/DESCRIPTION::Version`
  - `r/R/dw_io.R::dw_toolkit_version()` (literal + roxygen `@return`)
  - `r/R/dw_io.R` header comment
  - `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd` (regenerated)
  - 2 testthat assertions on `dw_toolkit_version()` and `body$toolkit` in `dw_publish` echo

## Post-merge actions

  1. Tag `v0.4.5` at the merge commit on `main`.
  2. Create GitHub Release v0.4.5 (paste the v0.4.5 NEWS section as body).
  3. Open 4 DW-Production install PRs (NT, IM, WS, HVA) against `review/sector-*-2026-05-18` branches.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on this PR (R CMD check x 4 OS/R combos).
- [ ] `git diff main...develop -- r/DESCRIPTION` shows only Version 0.4.4 → 0.4.5.
- [ ] `git diff main...develop -- r/NAMESPACE` shows + 9 lines (1 importFrom + 8 export(dw_*)).
- [ ] After merge: tag `v0.4.5` is reachable from `main` HEAD.
- [ ] GitHub Release v0.4.5 published with the v0.4.5 NEWS section.

prs.title: chore(release): cut v0.4.5
prs.body:
## Summary

Cuts cso-toolkit **v0.4.5** by merging the two v0.4.5 milestone PRs (already on `develop`) into `main` and stamping the release.

Three substantive deliverables in this cycle:

1. **#46** — Standalone-source `%>%` binding (PR #47): vendored consumers can `source("aggregate_data.R")` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` without first attaching magrittr/dplyr. Pre-flight gate binds `%>%` locally when not already in scope; no-op in installed-package context.
2. **#42** — `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases (PR #48): 8 additional `dw_*` aliases close out the namespace-prefix program that began in v0.4.4 (which aliased only the two exports touched by #36).
3. **Magrittr dependency declaration** (companion to PR #47): adds `magrittr` to `DESCRIPTION::Imports` and emits `importFrom(magrittr, "%>%")` to NAMESPACE — previously came in transitively via dplyr. R CMD check is now honest about the dependency.

Issue #40 (`dw_regions` API redesign against Country-and-Region-Metadata-API) is deferred to v0.5.0 since the function is being rewritten — the value-arg bug fix folds into the redesign rather than landing in v0.4.5.

## Version-stamp bumps in this release PR

  | File:line | From | To |
  |---|---|---|
  | `r/DESCRIPTION::Version` | `0.4.4` | `0.4.5` |
  | `r/DESCRIPTION::Date`    | `2026-05-29` | `<release date>` |
  | `r/R/dw_io.R:254` (docstring) | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/R/dw_io.R:266` (literal)   | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R:21` | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R:120`          | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |

NEWS: rename `## Unreleased` → `## v0.4.5 (<release date>)` and add a fresh `## Unreleased` block.

## What ships in v0.4.5

### Public-surface additions (all non-breaking)

- `dw_aggregate_data` — alias of `aggregate_data`
- `dw_generate_agg_footnote` — alias of `generate_agg_footnote`
- `dw_apply_time_window` — alias of `apply_time_window`
- `dw_generate_markdown_report` — alias of `generate_markdown_report`
- `dw_process_all_csv_files` — alias of `process_all_csv_files`
- `dw_create_profile` — alias of `create_profile`
- `dw_review_profile` — alias of `review_profile`
- `dw_test_scripts` — alias of `test_scripts`

### Behavioural changes

- Vendored helpers (`aggregate_data.R`, `aggregate_data_v2.R`) are now safe to `source()` standalone without `library(magrittr)` or `library(dplyr)` first.
- `magrittr` is a first-class declared Import; R CMD check no longer relies on transitive resolution through dplyr.

### Documentation

- NEWS entry under `v0.4.5 (<release date>)` covering both deliverables + the magrittr declaration.
- Roxygen `@rdname` ties on the 8 aliases mean both names share the same man page.

## DW-Production downstream propagation plan

Once this PR merges and `v0.4.5` is tagged, the manifest pulls go out to each of the four sectors that consumed v0.4.4 (NT, IM, WS, HVA) on their respective `review/sector-*-2026-05-18` branches. The diff vs v0.4.4 affects 4 files (aggregate_data.R, aggregate_data_v2.R, zzz.R, DESCRIPTION — though DESCRIPTION may not be vendored on the DW-Production side). The remaining 7 vendored files are byte-identical to v0.4.4.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on `develop` head (R CMD check: ubuntu R-release, ubuntu R-devel, macos R-release, windows R-release).
- [ ] No substantive Copilot findings on the release PR.
- [ ] `r/DESCRIPTION::Version` reads `0.4.5`.
- [ ] `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.5"` (both in dw_io.R source and the two tests).
- [ ] `r/NAMESPACE` carries `importFrom(magrittr,"%>%")` + 8 new `export(dw_*)` lines vs. v0.4.4.
- [ ] NEWS.md `## Unreleased` block is empty after rename, ready for v0.4.6+ entries.
- [ ] Tag `v0.4.5` reachable from `main`.
- [ ] GitHub Release v0.4.5 published with copy of the v0.4.5 NEWS section.
- [ ] After release: 4 DW-Production install PRs queued (NT, IM, WS, HVA).

prs.title: feat(r): dw_ canonical aliases for 8 remaining un-prefixed exports (closes #42)
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #42 — Adds `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the remaining 8 un-prefixed exports, completing the namespace-prefix program that began in v0.4.4 (PR #43, which aliased the two exports touched by #36).

## What this PR does

Eight new `dw_` aliases land across 5 R files. Each alias is exported via roxygen `@rdname` so both names share the same man page (same mechanism as v0.4.4):

| Source file | Un-prefixed export | New `dw_`-prefixed alias |
|---|---|---|
| `r/R/aggregate_data.R`           | `aggregate_data`           | `dw_aggregate_data` |
| `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R`        | `generate_agg_footnote`    | `dw_generate_agg_footnote` |
| `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R`        | `apply_time_window`        | `dw_apply_time_window` |
| `r/R/generate_markdown_report.R` | `generate_markdown_report` | `dw_generate_markdown_report` |
| `r/R/generate_markdown_report.R` | `process_all_csv_files`    | `dw_process_all_csv_files` |
| `r/R/profile_helpers.R`          | `create_profile`           | `dw_create_profile` |
| `r/R/profile_helpers.R`          | `review_profile`           | `dw_review_profile` |
| `r/R/test_scripts.R`             | `test_scripts`             | `dw_test_scripts` |

## Non-breaking

- Un-prefixed names continue to work indefinitely in v0.4.x (no behaviour change for existing consumers).
- A future v0.5.x cycle may emit a `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` warning on the un-prefixed forms, but only after sector leads confirm migration is complete.

## Explicit non-renames

- `create_dw_sector_script` already carries a `dw` infix (it's a DW-Production-specific wrapper of `create_sector_script`, which received `dw_create_sector_script` in v0.4.4). Not aliased; the name is already prefix-bearing.
- `test_scripts` carries a roxygen note flagging a possible future v0.5.x rename to `dw_audit_scripts` — both to surface the audit intent and to avoid the cosmetic collision with `testthat::test_*` naming. The prefix-only alias here keeps this PR additive.

## Tests

`r/tests/testthat/test-dw_aliases-v045.R` — 8 identity tests verifying each `dw_<name>` is `identical()` to its un-prefixed source. Mirrors the v0.4.4 `test-dw_aliases.R` pattern.

## Roxygen / NAMESPACE

`r/man/*.Rd` (8 files): regenerated to add `\alias{dw_<name>}` + `\usage{dw_<name>(...)}` entries via `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")`.

`r/NAMESPACE`: gains 8 export entries (one per alias).

## Cross-references

- Issue: #42
- Parent program: #36 (first 2 aliases landed in v0.4.4 via PR #43)
- v0.4.5 milestone: https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/4
- Companion v0.4.5 work: #46 / PR #47 (standalone-source pipe binding)

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green (R CMD check, lints, tests).
- [ ] Copilot reviewer leaves no substantive findings.
- [ ] `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")` is idempotent locally (no further `r/man/*.Rd` deltas).
- [ ] `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_aliases-v045.R` passes all 8 identity tests.
- [ ] `r/NAMESPACE` has exactly 8 new `export(dw_*)` lines vs. develop.
- [ ] After merge: develop branch carries all 8 aliases; un-prefixed exports unchanged.

prs.title: fix(r): standalone-source %>% binding for aggregate_data + aggregate_data_v2 (closes #46)
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements [#46](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/46) — standalone-source `%>%` binding for the two files that use the pipe outside the installed-package context.

Surfaced by Copilot review of DW-Production [PR #144](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/144) (WS v0.4.4 install) on 2026-05-29: v0.4.4's #36 fix added a `.cso_require()` fallback but missed that `requireNamespace()` doesn't attach `%>%`. The first pipe call in `aggregate_data_v2.R` still errored.

## Changes

### `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R` + `r/R/aggregate_data.R`

Same one-line gate pattern, just like `.cso_require`:

```r
if (!exists("%>%", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)) {
  `%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`
}
```

- Installed-package context: NAMESPACE's `importFrom(magrittr, "%>%")` wins → gate is a no-op
- Standalone-source: local binding via `magrittr` namespace → no `library(magrittr)` needed

### `r/R/zzz.R`

The `globalVariables()` comment claimed `.cso_require()` "attaches magrittr at source-time" — that's exactly the misdiagnosis Copilot caught. Updated to describe the actual v0.4.5 standalone-source mechanism (local `%>%` fallback gated by `exists()`).

### `r/tests/testthat/test-standalone-pipe-binding.R` (new — 3 tests)

- Installed-package binding (NAMESPACE path)
- Standalone fallback fires when `%>%` not in scope
- Gate is no-op when `%>%` already bound (preserves consumer-set pipe)

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 3 new tests added; existing testthat suite (300+ after v0.4.4) unchanged

prs.title: release: develop → main — v0.4.1 + v0.4.2 + v0.4.3 + v0.4.3.1 + v0.4.4 catch-up
prs.body:
## Summary

`develop → main` catch-up after 5 tagged minor / patch releases since the last sync (PR #23, v0.4.0 merged 2026-05-27).

| Tag | Date | Theme |
|---|---|---|
| `v0.4.1` | 2026-05-27 | dialect + overwrite cleanups |
| `v0.4.2` | 2026-05-27 | `dialect = "base"` separator fix (Copilot-flagged silent-CSV bug) |
| `v0.4.3` | 2026-05-28 | parquet/dta `col_select = NULL` hotfix + `dw_use(cols_lenient = TRUE)` |
| `v0.4.3.1` | 2026-05-28 | bump 3 internal `dw_io.R` version stamps (release-process gap from v0.4.3) |
| **`v0.4.4`** | **2026-05-29** | quality release: `.dw_frozen_root()` discoverability + `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` helper + carry-forward bugs + `dw_` aliases |

## Headline themes since v0.4.0

### IO contract hardening (v0.4.3 → v0.4.4)

- `dw_use()` parquet / dta `col_select = NULL` returns all columns (was returning empty schema) — surfaced by DW-Production NT 1b CMRS-series 594K-row read (#30)
- New `dw_use(cols_lenient = FALSE)` flag for `any_of()`-style schema intersect — replaces the toxic `dplyr::any_of()` at-top-level pattern (#31)
- `.dw_frozen_root()` 3-tier resolution now surfaces the chosen tier in messages + error envelopes (#38)
- `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` exported helper consolidates the per-consumer regex duplication (#37)

### `dw_save()` byte-parity + consistency (v0.4.1, v0.4.2)

- `dialect = "base"` honours dispatched separator (was silently producing CSV in `.tsv` files — Copilot-flagged)
- Overwrite gate semantics for reviewer-mode scratch writes

### Helper resilience (v0.4.4)

- `aggregate_data_v2.R` now safe to source standalone (local `.cso_require()` fallback)
- `create_sector_script()` profile sentinel relaxed from `isTRUE()` to `!is.null()`; default re-aligned with `create_profile()`'s output
- `dw_aggregate_data_v2()` + `dw_create_sector_script()` canonical aliases added (non-breaking)

### Release-process learnings

- v0.4.3 → v0.4.3.1 patch surfaced that the release-cut checklist needed `grep -rn '"0\.[0-9]\.[0-9]"' r/R/ r/tests/` BEFORE tagging — now applied successfully to v0.4.4

## Empirical validation (DW-Production fanout audit, 2026-05-27 → 2026-05-29)

Every fix in v0.4.1 through v0.4.4 was surfaced empirically during DW-Production's reviewer-mode reproducibility audits:

- **NT** (PR #138; DW-Production): Run #8 (90.7 min) — 24/25 stages OK; 5b cell-level diff vs canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced; v0.4.2 → v0.4.3 fixes ratified
- **IM** (PR #139): Run #3 (12.8 min) — 87,573 rows × 19 cols, byte-equivalent to v0.1.0-rc1 baseline; v0.4.3.1 ratified; surfaced #37 + #38
- **WS** (PR #140): Run #2 (12.8 min) — 702,877 rows across 3 settings; v0.4.3.1 ratified
- **HVA**: scaffold install Copilot review surfaced the 3 carry-forward bugs in #36

## Follow-ups (post-merge)

| Issue | Title | Target |
|---|---|---|
| [#40](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/40) | `dw_regions()` redesign against `Country-and-Region-Metadata-API` | v0.5.0 |
| [#42](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/42) | Rename remaining 8 un-prefixed exports to `dw_` convention | v0.4.5 |
| [#15](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/15) | `dw_publish()` live submission branch (dry-run STUB shipped in v0.4.0) | v0.5.0 |

## No breaking API changes since v0.4.0

`v0.4.0` shipped the producer/reviewer mode contract (breaking from v0.3.0); since then the toolkit has been purely additive plus bug fixes. v0.3.0 → v0.4.x consumers had to migrate once; v0.4.0 → v0.4.4 consumers experience no behaviour change beyond the bug fixes.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI matrix on the develop-side passes (verified across PRs #29 / #33 / #34 / #35 / #39 / #41 / #43 / #44 — each merged with 4/4 CI green)
- [ ] After merge: `main` HEAD has DESCRIPTION Version = 0.4.4, `dw_toolkit_version()` = "0.4.4"
- [ ] Tag chain visible: `v0.4.0` ... `v0.4.4` all reachable from `main`
- [ ] DW-Production consumers' next `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.4")` resolves cleanly

prs.title: fix(r): aggregate_data_v2 standalone-source + create_sector_script profile sentinel (closes #36 minus dw_regions)
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes 2 of 3 sub-fixes flagged on [#36](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/36). The third (`dw_regions` value-arg propagation) is moot — `dw_regions` is being redesigned in [#40](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/40) (v0.5.0); the affected code path is removed.

Also adds the two `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the touched exports per the maintainer's API-consistency directive 2026-05-28. Follow-up [#42](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/42) tracks the remaining 8 un-prefixed exports as a single cleanup PR.

## Sub-fix 1 — `aggregate_data_v2.R` standalone-source resilience

Pre-fix: calling `.cso_require()` from `zzz.R` unconditionally — if `aggregate_data_v2.R` was sourced WITHOUT `zzz.R` first, the first call to `aggregate_data_v2(...)` errored with `could not find function .cso_require`.

Fix: a local `.cso_require()` fallback at the top of `aggregate_data_v2.R`, gated by `exists(".cso_require", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)`. The fallback is byte-equivalent to the `zzz.R` version. When `zzz.R` has been sourced into `.GlobalEnv`, the shared helper wins (no redefinition).

## Sub-fix 2 — `create_sector_script()` profile sentinel

Pre-fix: the generated template checked for a `profile_DW_Production` variable that the DW-Production profile never defines.

Fix:
- Default `profile_name` changed `"profile_DW_Production"` → `"projectFolder"` (a variable the DW-Production profile actually sets)
- Sentinel check relaxed `isTRUE(<name>)` → `!is.null(<name>)` (accepts character paths, numerics, or boolean sentinels)
- Error message now names the missing variable
- Roxygen `@param` doc updated with common alternatives
- `create_dw_sector_script()` wrapper updated to pass `profile_name = "projectFolder"`

## `dw_`-prefixed aliases (per maintainer directive)

| Un-prefixed | New canonical | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| `aggregate_data_v2` | `dw_aggregate_data_v2` | `@rdname` alias |
| `create_sector_script` | `dw_create_sector_script` | `@rdname` alias |

Both names point to the same function and share the same `\link{}` man page via roxygen `@rdname`. Non-prefixed names continue to work — **no breaking change**.

## Tests

- `test-aggregate_data_v2-standalone.R` (new — 3 tests): standalone-source defines `.cso_require`; local fallback behaves like the zzz.R version; shared helper preserved when `zzz.R` is sourced first
- `test-dw_aliases.R` (new — 2 tests): `dw_aggregate_data_v2 === aggregate_data_v2`, `dw_create_sector_script === create_sector_script`

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 5 new tests added; pre-existing suite unchanged
- [ ] Smoke: `Rscript -e 'source("00_functions/aggregate_data_v2.R"); aggregate_data_v2(<minimal-args>)'` no longer errors with `.cso_require` not found
- [ ] Smoke: `create_dw_sector_script("Test", "tst")` generates a runner script whose profile-sentinel check succeeds against the DW-Production profile

## Cross-references

- Issue: #36
- Companion v0.4.4: #38 (frozen-root; MERGED via PR #39), #37 (allowlist helper; MERGED via PR #41), #40 (`dw_regions` redesign deferred to v0.5.0)
- Follow-up: #42 (prefix-cleanup for the other 8 un-prefixed exports)
- v0.4.4 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>

prs.title: feat(r): dw_default_unicef_allowlist() helper for consumers (closes #37)
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements [#37](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/37) — new exported helper `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` returning a character vector of `^...`-anchored regex patterns for UNICEF-DRP GitHub-raw + repo URLs. Consumers seed `dw_url_allowlist` from this constant instead of hand-writing the same patterns in every project profile.

Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production IM reviewer-mode audit on 2026-05-28 (PR #139): every URL-using sector script was duplicating the `^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/` pattern.

## Changes

### `r/R/dw_io.R`

New `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` exported function (~5 LOC + roxygen). Two patterns:

```r
c(
  "^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/",
  "^https://github\.com/unicef-drp/"
)
```

### Tests (`r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-default-allowlist.R`, new — 6 tests)

- Non-empty character vector returned
- All patterns anchored with `^`
- Canonical `raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/` URL matches `.is_allowlisted_url`
- Generic `github.com/unicef-drp/` URL matches
- **Non-UNICEF-DRP raw URL is REJECTED** (the safety guarantee preserved)
- Composition with project-specific extras via `c(...)` works

### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.4 candidate entry with usage example, the empirical-surface citation, and the safety-contract note.

### Roxygen / NAMESPACE

`man/dw_default_unicef_allowlist.Rd` (new); `NAMESPACE` export entry.

## Safety contract preserved

Purely additive. The URL-freeze contract is unchanged: consumers must opt in by composing the helper into their `dw_url_allowlist`; no URL is fetchable without explicit ratification. The helper covers `unicef-drp` org only (not other UNICEF data partners like OCHA-DAP / IATI — those remain consumer-controlled).

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 6 new tests added; existing testthat suite (~290 tests after PR #39) unchanged
- [ ] DW-Production smoke: replace IM profile's hand-rolled allowlist with `dw_url_allowlist <- c(dw_default_unicef_allowlist(), ...)` → IM Run still produces 87,573 × 19 cols

## Cross-references

- Issue: #37
- Companion v0.4.4: #38 (frozen-root discoverability; MERGED via PR #39), #36 (carry-forward bugs; reduced scope after #40)
- DW-Production PR [#139](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/139) (IM audit; the empirical surface)
- v0.4.4 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>

prs.title: fix(r): .dw_frozen_root() resolution discoverability + error envelope (closes #38)
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements [#38](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/38) — `.dw_frozen_root()` resolution discoverability + extended error envelope for missing frozen copies.

Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production IM reviewer-mode audit on 2026-05-28 against v0.4.3.1: the toolkit's `.dw_frozen_root()` fallback silently resolved to `<githubFolder>/_frozen` instead of DW-Production's `<projectFolder>/01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/` convention. Three IM runs (75+ min on cold Teams) to diagnose what a single session-start message could have surfaced.

## Changes

### `r/R/dw_io.R`

- New internal helper `.dw_frozen_root_resolved()` returns `(path, source)` where `source` is `"dw_frozen_root"` / `"githubFolder"` / `"getwd"`.
- New internal helper `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` emits a session-scoped notice on tier #2/#3 fallback (`message()` for #2, `warning()` for #3); silent when consumer explicitly sets `dw_frozen_root`. Gated by `.__cso_toolkit_frozen_root_notified__` sentinel.
- `.dw_frozen_root()` (path-only legacy API) preserved unchanged; body delegates to `.dw_frozen_root_resolved()$path` for single-source-of-truth.
- `.resolve_remote_url()` error envelope now includes "Frozen-root resolution: `<path>` (`<tier-name>`)" + a 2-step Fix block distinguishing "wrong path" from "missing file."

### `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-frozen-root.R` (new — 7 tests)

- Tier #1 / #2 / #3 resolution
- `.dw_frozen_root()` legacy contract preserved
- Error envelope mentions resolution tier
- `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` fires at most once per session
- `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` silent when `dw_frozen_root` explicit

### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.4 candidate entry under Unreleased citing the DW-Production IM audit.

## No public-API changes

`dw_frozen_root` global semantics unchanged. Consumers who already explicitly set it (like DW-Production after the IM Run #2 patch) get no behaviour change. v0.4.3.1 callers of `.dw_frozen_root()` keep working.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] `testthat::test_dir("r/tests/testthat")` adds 7 new tests + the legacy 285+ all pass
- [ ] Smoke test: re-run DW-Production IM `01_immunization.R` without the profile-side `dw_frozen_root` patch — verify the new error envelope surfaces the wrong-path tier on the first failure (instead of requiring grep diving)

## Cross-references

- Issue: #38
- DW-Production PR [#139](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/139) (IM audit; the empirical surface)
- Companion v0.4.4 work: #37 (allowlist helper), #36 (carry-forward bugs)

prs.title: chore(release): v0.4.3.1 — bump three version stamps inside dw_io.R
prs.body:
## Summary

Patch release. v0.4.3 (PR #34 earlier today) bumped `DESCRIPTION::Version` and renamed `NEWS.md` but **missed three version stamps inside `r/R/dw_io.R`**. Caught by Copilot review on DW-Production install PRs #134 + #136.

The drift would have polluted `dw_publish()` provenance sidecars with the wrong toolkit version — the whole point of carrying the stamp inside the file is to survive copy-out into a consumer's `00_functions/`.

## Changes

- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 3: `# Toolkit version: 0.4.2` → `0.4.3.1`
- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 254: docstring `Currently "0.4.2"` → `"0.4.3.1"`
- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 266: `dw_toolkit_version()` return `"0.4.2"` → `"0.4.3.1"`
- `r/DESCRIPTION` Version: `0.4.3` → `0.4.3.1`; Date unchanged
- `NEWS.md`: new `## v0.4.3.1` section between Unreleased and v0.4.3
- `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd`: regenerated by `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")` to pick up the docstring change

No behavioural changes. Drop-in replacement for v0.4.3.

## Release-cut checklist gap

For the next minor (v0.5.0), the release-PR template should require running `grep -rn '0\.[0-9]\.[0-9]' r/R/` before tagging. This patch documents the gap; the procedural fix lands separately.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs, same as v0.4.3 release)
- [ ] `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.3.1"` in a smoke session
- [ ] After merge: tag `v0.4.3.1` and re-sync the 4 DW-Production install PRs (#134/#135/#136/#137)

prs.title: chore(release): v0.4.3
prs.body:
## Summary

Cuts v0.4.3 on develop.

- `NEWS.md`: "Unreleased" → "v0.4.3 (2026-05-28)"; three h4 subsections demoted to h3 (the dropped "candidate entries" h3 was their previous parent); duplicated lead paragraph trimmed; fresh "Unreleased" section added back at the top.
- `r/DESCRIPTION`: `Version: 0.4.2 → 0.4.3`, `Date: 2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28`.

## Contents of v0.4.3

| Issue | Title | PR |
|---|---|---|
| #30 | dw_use(): parquet / dta `col_select = NULL` conditional dispatch | #33 |
| #31 | dw_use(): `cols_lenient = FALSE` flag for `any_of()`-style schema intersect | #33 |
| #32 | Provenance sidecars (producer → reviewer → ingestor integrity chain) | **design-only at this stage; implementation deferred to a follow-up PR within the same milestone** |

Both code-shipping issues (#30, #31) merged via PR #33 (commit `1493751`). Empirical validation came from the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit on 2026-05-27 (DW-Production PR [#133](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/133)) — NT pipeline now runs end-to-end through 4b → 5b in reviewer mode; cell-level diff against canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced.

## After this PR merges

1. Tag `v0.4.3` on the resulting merge commit on `develop`.
2. Cut a GitHub Release pointing at the tag, pulling the v0.4.3 section of NEWS.md as the release body.
3. DW-Production runs `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.3")` — drops `dw_io.R` from `local_edits`, bumps `pulled_version` from `v0.4.2` to `v0.4.3`.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs — same as PR #33's post-fix run)
- [ ] DESCRIPTION + NEWS heading hierarchy renders cleanly on the GitHub Release page

prs.title: fix(r): port v0.4.3 candidates from DW-Production NT audit (closes #30, #31)
prs.body:
## Summary

Ports the two `dw_use` fixes that landed first as DW-Production `local_edits` against v0.4.2 (DW-Production PR [#133](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/133)) into canonical cso-toolkit. Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit on 2026-05-27.

Closes #30. Closes #31.

(Issue #32 — provenance sidecars — is the design-foundation companion; implementation is a separate PR within the v0.4.3 milestone.)

### Why this PR exists

DW-Production's NT pipeline cannot reproduce its canonical warehouse output without these two fixes:

1. **dw_use() returns empty schemas on default parquet reads.** Run #6 (before fix): 10/25 NT stages OK with 13 downstream stages failing `object 'COLUMN' not found` because `dw_use(*.parquet)` was returning a zero-column tibble.
2. **dw_use(cols = dplyr::any_of(...)) errors fatally under tidyselect >= 1.2.0.** Sector scripts use `any_of()` for "select these IF present" semantics; that pattern no longer works at top level.

After both fixes + the cols_lenient feature on call sites: Run #8 (90.7 min) → 24/25 stages OK; 5b cell-level diff vs canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced.

### Changes

#### `r/R/dw_io.R`

- Parquet and dta branches now use conditional dispatch (`if is.null(cols) pass through ...`, `else pass col_select = cols`).
- New `cols_lenient = FALSE` parameter on `dw_use()`.
- New internal helper `.dw_schema_cols(path, fmt)` — cheap metadata-only schema introspection per format (parquet via `arrow::open_dataset()`, csv/tsv/txt via `data.table::fread(nrows = 0)`, dta via `haven::read_dta(n_max = 0)`, xlsx via `readxl::read_xlsx(n_max = 0)`).

#### `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-cols-lenient.R` (new)

Seven tests covering:
- parquet without cols → all columns
- dta without cols → all columns
- parquet with explicit cols → strict selection respected
- `cols_lenient = TRUE` intersects with parquet schema
- `cols_lenient = TRUE` intersects with csv schema
- empty intersection → warning + read all
- `cols_lenient = FALSE` default preserves strict semantics

#### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.3 candidate entries added under "Unreleased". At release cut, the section rename folds the entries up.

### Migration for sector scripts

```r
# Before (errors fatally under tidyselect >= 1.2.0):
dw_use(path, cols = dplyr::any_of(c("a", "b", "c")))

# After:
dw_use(path, cols = c("a", "b", "c"), cols_lenient = TRUE)
```

`dplyr::all_of()` at top level is deprecated in tidyselect 1.2.0 anyway — pass a bare character vector for strict semantics.

### Validation harness

DW-Production PR #133 was the empirical validation: the exact code in this PR ran the NT pipeline (`tests/test_nt_full_pipeline.R`) end-to-end through 4b → 5b in reviewer mode. Stage table from Run #8:

| Stage | Status | Time |
|---|---|---|
| 0_execute_conductor | OK | 854.2s |
| 1a–1f imports | all OK | <1m total |
| **2a_agg_prep** (was FATAL on `any_of()`) | **OK** | 794.9s |
| 2b–2j aggregations | all OK | ~55s total |
| 3a–3c targets | all OK | ~10s total |
| **4b_dw_format_merge** (was unreachable before fixes) | **OK; wrote dw_nut_tocopy.csv** | 1200.2s |
| 5a_compare_vs_lastrun | (separate sector script bug; fixed in DW-Prod) | n/a |
| 5b_compare_vs_warehouse | **OK; cell-level diff produced** | 565.2s |

### After this PR lands

- Tag `v0.4.3` on cso-toolkit (separate PR or `develop` → `main` workflow).
- DW-Production runs `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.3")` to drop `dw_io.R` from `local_edits` and bump `pulled_version` to `v0.4.3`.
- Provenance sidecars (#32) get a follow-up PR within the same milestone.

## Test plan

- [ ] `R CMD check r/` passes (or the equivalent CI invocation)
- [ ] `testthat::test_dir("r/tests/testthat")` passes; new `test-dw_io-cols-lenient.R` adds 7 tests
- [ ] Smoke-test on a real DW-Production sector script (NT 2a already validated via DW-Production PR #133 Run #8)
- [ ] Copilot review surfaces no NEW substantive findings (the in-PR comments already explain the rationale)

prs.title: v0.4.2: dialect="base" honours dispatched separator (fix silent-CSV bug)
prs.body:
## Summary

Patch release fixing a Copilot-flagged silent-CSV bug in v0.4.1's new `dialect = "base"` dispatch on `dw_save()`. Surfaced by Copilot review of [DW-Production#128](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/128) (the v0.4.1 cleanup pull) before any sector pipeline could write a corrupted `.tsv`.

## The bug

v0.4.1 restored a `dialect = c("fwrite", "base")` parameter on `dw_save()`. The `"base"` branch dispatched through `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)`, which **hardcodes a comma separator**. That meant:

```r
dw_save(x, "out.tsv", dialect = "base")
# Wrote comma-separated content into a .tsv file -- silent corruption.
```

Indistinguishable from a real TSV at the filename level, but with wrong delimiters inside.

## The fix

Switch the `dialect = "base"` branch in `.write_csv()` from:

```r
utils::write.csv(x, file = path)
```

…to the equivalent underlying call with the dispatched separator:

```r
utils::write.table(x, file = path,
                   sep = sep,
                   col.names = NA,
                   qmethod = "double")
```

`write.csv` is itself a wrapper around `write.table` with those exact args plus an enforced `sep = ","`. So:

- For `.csv` (sep = `,`) the byte output is **identical** to `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)`. The byte-parity guarantee v0.4.1 introduced for legacy callers (DW-Production NT `2[bcfg]_agg_*` scripts) is preserved.
- For `.tsv` / `.txt` (sep = `\t`) the file now contains actual **tab** separators with the same `row.names` / quoted-string defaults.

## Test coverage

New regression test file `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-dialect.R`:

| Test | Assertion |
|---|---|
| `dialect='base'` on `.csv` | byte-identical to `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)` |
| `dialect='base'` on `.tsv` | output contains tab chars + no commas in data rows (the fix) |
| `dialect='base'` with `compress = TRUE` | envelope-shaped error (gzip is fwrite-only) |
| `dialect='unknown'` | envelope-shaped error from `match.arg()` |

Plus version-stamp assertion bumps in `test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` and `test-dw_publish.R` (`"0.4.1"` → `"0.4.2"`).

## Files changed

- `r/R/dw_io.R` — `.write_csv()` dispatch + `@param dialect` roxygen + header + `dw_toolkit_version()` + `@return` roxygen
- `r/DESCRIPTION` — `Version: 0.4.1 → 0.4.2`
- `NEWS.md` — v0.4.2 entry
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-dialect.R` — NEW (4 regression tests)
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` — version-stamp assertion
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R` — version-stamp assertion
- `r/man/dw_save.Rd` — `@param dialect` description
- `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd` — `Currently "0.4.1" → "0.4.2"`

## No public-API change

`dw_save()`'s signature is unchanged. Callers using `dialect = "base"` on `.csv` see no behavioural difference. Callers passing `.tsv` / `.txt` with `dialect = "base"` get correct tab output instead of silent comma output (which was the bug).

## Validation

Locally verified before backport: see DW-Production#128 commit `4a38b07` which applied the same fix and confirmed via focused Rscript checks:

- `.csv` byte-parity: `TRUE` (write.table with col.names=NA + qmethod="double" produces identical output to write.csv)
- `.tsv` contains tabs: `TRUE` (fix addresses the silent CSV-with-TSV-extension bug)

## Cross-references

- Triggering finding: Copilot review on [DW-Production#128](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/128) (commit `0e6d7eb`, line 675)
- Predecessor: [cso-toolkit#24](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/24) (v0.4.1 release; introduced the `dialect` parameter)

prs.title: v0.4.1: restore dialect param + lenient overwrite default in dw_save
prs.body:
## Summary

Patch two regressions identified by Copilot review of DW-Production PR [unicef-drp/DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127) (the v0.4.0 pull into NT review). Both patches were validated against the NT branch in DW-Production before this backport — see PR #127 commit `12791e8` and the NT regression test log in PR #127's comment thread.

## Patch A — Restore `dialect` parameter on `dw_save()` / `.write_csv()`

**Regression**: v0.4.0 dropped the `dialect` parameter that v0.3.x exposed for byte-parity with `utils::write.csv()`. DW-Production NT scripts depend on `dialect = "base"` for legacy CSV format preservation:

- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2b_agg_iod.R:52`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2c_agg_vas_series.R:47`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2f_agg_bw.R:52`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2g_agg_iycf.R:240`

The NT post-pilot writeup ([`nt/reviews/2026-05-25-jpazvd-pilot/01_post_pilot_writeup.md`](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/blob/review/sector-nt-2026-05-18/01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/reviews/2026-05-25-jpazvd-pilot/01_post_pilot_writeup.md)) explicitly relies on byte-identical output to `utils::write.csv()`. Under v0.4.0, the `dialect` argument is silently forwarded via `...` into `data.table::fwrite`, which doesn't accept it — sector scripts error or silently lose byte parity.

**Fix**:

- Add `dialect = c("fwrite", "base")` as an explicit parameter on `dw_save()`. Validates via `match.arg()`.
- Plumb `dialect` through the CSV/TSV/TXT dispatch lines to `.write_csv()`.
- In `.write_csv()`, branch on dialect:
  - `"fwrite"` (default) — existing `data.table::fwrite` path
  - `"base"` — `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)` (preserves `row.names = TRUE` + default-quoted strings)
- `dialect = "base"` with `compress = TRUE` raises an explanatory error (gzip is fwrite-only).

## Patch B — Lenient overwrite default for reviewer mode

**Design mismatch**: v0.4.0 shipped `overwrite = FALSE` uniformly across both modes (strict). The closed issue #14 design discussion (jpazvd, 2026-05-27) preferred lenient: producer-mode requires explicit `overwrite = TRUE`; reviewer-mode keeps default-TRUE for scratch writes to local sandbox under `01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/_local/`.

**Rationale**: reviewer sandbox writes are already gitignored and easy to nuke + re-run. Forcing explicit `overwrite = TRUE` there is friction without safety benefit. Producer writes target the canonical Teams + Z: deposit where accidental overwrite is unsafe — explicit acknowledgement remains required.

**Fix**:

- Signature: `overwrite = FALSE` → `overwrite = NULL` (sentinel).
- After `is_reviewer` / `is_producer` detection in `dw_save` body, resolve:
  ```r
  if (is.null(overwrite)) {
    overwrite <- isTRUE(is_reviewer)
    # reviewer  -> TRUE  (scratch is safe to re-run)
    # producer  -> FALSE (must be explicit)
    # mode unset -> FALSE (safe default)
  }
  ```
- Existing `if (!isTRUE(overwrite))` gate at the post-mirror-check unchanged — the NULL resolution above coerces to TRUE/FALSE before it fires.

## Verification

**On DW-Production PR #127** (NT branch with these patches applied):

- `Rscript -e "parse('00_functions/dw_io.R')"` → SYNTAX OK
- Focused unit tests in `tests/test_v041_nt.R`:
  - `.cso_require()` defined after zzz.R vendor (DW-Production-side, not relevant here)
  - `dw_save(x, p, dialect = "base")` produces byte-identical output to `utils::write.csv(x, p)` ✓
  - `dw_save(x, p)` reviewer mode → default overwrite resolves to TRUE; re-run against existing file succeeds silently ✓
  - `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.0"` (will be `"0.4.1"` after this PR merges + tag) ✓
- NT active conductor end-to-end run (`0_execute_conductor.R`) with patched toolkit: [log to be posted on PR #127 once the background run completes; will cross-reference here]

## What's NOT in scope for this PR

Three additional Copilot findings on DW-Production#127 are real canonical bugs but deferred to separate issues — they have zero blast radius on current DW-Production sector scripts because no sector currently calls `dw_regions()` / `dw_publish()` / `dw_pop()` (the wrappers are inert).

Filed as separate issues:

- `dw_save` flow ordering: mirror destinations computed before `.gz` suffix; `mirror_to_z` stripped from `dots` but `...` still forwards original.
- `dw_save` producer self-mirror when `teamsWrkData == teamsFolderCanonical`.
- `dw_regions` `Aggregate` column not renamed to caller's `value` arg.
- `create_sector_script` DW wrapper: wrong default paths + missing profile sentinel.

These would land in a v0.4.2 cleanup release.

## Migration notes for v0.4.1 changelog

- **`dw_save(..., dialect = ...)` parameter restored.** Sector scripts that depend on byte-parity with `utils::write.csv()` (e.g., DW-Production NT 2[bcfg]_agg_*) can pass `dialect = "base"` again. The default remains `"fwrite"` (`data.table::fwrite`).
- **`dw_save()` overwrite default is now mode-aware.** Reviewer mode keeps default-TRUE (scratch repo writes are safe to re-run). Producer mode keeps default-FALSE introduced in v0.4.0 (explicit `overwrite = TRUE` required). Pass `overwrite = NULL` to use the mode default; pass `overwrite = TRUE` / `FALSE` explicitly to override.

## Cross-references

- Triggering review: [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127) (Copilot findings #2 + #3)
- Design discussion: cso-toolkit#14 (closed; mode contract)
- NT regression test: `tests/test_v041_nt.R` in DW-Production PR #127

prs.title: release: v0.4.0 — mode contract + Stata parity + demographics + dw_publish STUB
prs.body:
## v0.4.0 release

Brings every PR merged into `develop` since v0.3.0 (2026-05-25) to `main` and tags `v0.4.0`. Five housekeeping commits flip the version stamps + roadmap.

## Substantive PRs landed since v0.3.0

| PR | Issue | Title |
|---|---|---|
| #11 | — | DW-Production alignment audit (B1–B4 backports) |
| #12 | — | testthat regression suite + GitHub Actions CI |
| #16 | — | README overhaul + bidirectional cross-linking |
| #19 | #14 | feat(r+py): producer/reviewer mode contract tightening (**BREAKING**) |
| #20 | #5 | feat(stata): `dw_use` + `dw_require_no_api` + `dw_load_config` |
| #21 | #17 + #18 | feat(r): `dw_pop()` + `dw_regions()` |
| #22 | #15 | feat(r): `dw_publish()` STUB (dry-run only) |

## Release-housekeeping commits (this PR)

1. `17d9728` chore(r): bump csotoolkit Version to 0.4.0 (2026-05-26)
2. `d8ce404` chore(py): bump cso-toolkit Python package to 0.4.0
3. `0393219` chore(templates): bump .toolkit_manifest.yml to v0.4.0
4. `a31c775` docs(news): promote Unreleased → v0.4.0 (2026-05-26)
5. `66e0615` docs(readme): roadmap table — v0.4.0 in flight → released

## Breaking change (one)

`dw_save(overwrite = ...)` default flipped **TRUE → FALSE** in v0.4.0. The overwrite check now examines ALL destinations that will be written (primary, Teams mirror, Z: mirror) and refuses if any of them already exists. Pass `overwrite = TRUE` to restore v0.3.0 behaviour. See migration notes in `NEWS.md` under issue #14.

## Verification

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → **245 / 0**
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes** (banner: `csotoolkit 0.4.0`)
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` → **all 34 invariants pass**
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` → **30 / 0**
- [x] `dw_toolkit_version()` (R) and `cso_toolkit.dw_toolkit_version()` + `cso_toolkit.__version__` (Python) all return `"0.4.0"`.

## Post-merge actions

1. Sync develop with main (back-merge of the version-bump commits).
2. `gh release create v0.4.0 --target main --title "v0.4.0 ..." --notes-file NEWS-v0.4.0-extract.md` (avoiding local `git push --tags` OOM per repo convention).

## Plan position

- ✅ **PR-B** (#19) — `feat(r+py): #14`
- ✅ **PR-C** (#20) — `feat(stata): #5`
- ✅ **PR-D** (#21) — `feat(r): #17 + #18`
- ✅ **PR-E** (#22) — `feat(r): #15 STUB`
- ⏭ **Release PR** (this PR) — version bumps + tag v0.4.0

What's *not* in v0.4.0 (deferred to v0.5.0):

- Live `dw_publish()` submission branch (issue #15 stays open).
- Python + Stata siblings of `dw_pop()` / `dw_regions()` (issue #18 stays open per user decision).

prs.title: feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub — dry-run only, live submission deferred to v0.5.0
prs.body:
## Summary

Lands `dw_publish()` as a **deliberate STUB** (issue #15). Ships the public signature + dry-run validation now so DW-Production sector scripts can wire the canonical Helix-submission call site today, then have the live branch light up automatically when v0.5.0 lands.

This PR is **independent of every other v0.4.0 PR** — pure R addition, no changes to existing helpers.

## Scope boundary (folded into the helper's roxygen + body)

The long-running DW-Production confusion is finally explicit:

| Helper | Surface | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_save()` | Filesystem | Teams folder + Z: drive mirror |
| `dw_publish()` (this PR) | API | Helix submission only |

## What's in (v0.4.0)

- Public signature matching the v0.5.0 contract:
  ```r
  dw_publish(path, indicator, vintage, sector,
             endpoint = "helix", dry_run = TRUE, ...)
  ```
  Note `dry_run = TRUE` is the v0.4.0 default; live branch is gated.
- **Producer-only mode contract** — reviewer-mode calls raise BEFORE any I/O via the same envelope shape as `dw_api_fetch()`.
- **Argument validation** — empty / missing `path` / `indicator` / `vintage` / `sector` raises the envelope; `path` must exist on disk and not be a directory; `endpoint` must be `"helix"`.
- **`dry_run = TRUE` returns a validated payload** with `sha256`, `bytes`, `built_at`, `built_by`, and the toolkit-version stamp. Caller scripts can assert the payload shape today without ever hitting the network.

## What's deferred to v0.5.0

- Live submission (`dry_run = FALSE`) raises with the envelope-shaped *"Live submission not yet implemented"* message and a pointer to GitHub issue #15. Real Helix endpoint integration ships when sector leads (@karavan88, @sbrar29, @laurenfrancis1202) finalise the submission contract.

## Atomic commits

1. `<feat1>` feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub
2. `<test1>` test(r): #15 dw_publish() regression suite
3. `<docs1>` docs(news): #15 v0.4.0 dw_publish() STUB entry

## Test plan

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → **235 pass / 0 fail** (was 196 + 6 new asserts that exercise mode lockout, validation, dry-run payload, and the live-submission stop)
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` → clean regeneration with new `dw_publish.Rd`
- [x] Tests assert: reviewer-mode raises BEFORE any path-existence check; dry-run payload has every documented key; `dry_run = FALSE` envelope includes the `issues/15` pointer.

## Files added

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| R source | `r/R/dw_publish.R` |
| R generated | `r/NAMESPACE` (+ `dw_publish` export), `r/man/dw_publish.Rd` (+ trivial `@seealso` updates in 5 sibling Rd files) |
| R tests | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R` |
| Release notes | `NEWS.md` |

## Plan position

- ✅ **PR-B** (#19) — `feat(r+py): #14`
- ✅ **PR-C** (#20) — `feat(stata): #5`
- ✅ **PR-D** (#21) — `feat(r): #17 + #18`
- ⏭ **PR-E** (this PR) — `feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub`
- ⏭ **Release PR** — version bumps + tag `v0.4.0`

prs.title: feat(r): #17 + #18 dw_pop() + dw_regions() — population denominators + UNICEF regional aggregates
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #17 and #18. Adds two convenience wrappers that almost every sector pipeline needs but that v0.3.0 made users write themselves. **R-only in v0.4.0**; Python + Stata parity tracked at the same issues.

This PR is **independent of PR-B (#19) and PR-C (#20)** — pure R additions, no source changes to existing helpers.

## What's new

| Helper | Wraps | Default behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_pop(year, indicator, countries, refresh, cache_key)` | `dw_api_fetch(api = "wb", indicator = "SP.POP.TOTL")` | Latest year per country; tidy `(REF_AREA, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE)` tibble |

Both helpers carry `@family demographics` and surface in the new pkgdown reference section of the same name.

prs.title: feat(stata): #5 v0.4.0 — dw_use + dw_require_no_api + dw_load_config
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #5. Ships the three Stata helpers that complete the v0.4.0 producer / reviewer mode contract on the Stata side. The Stata sibling now has the full read + mode-contract surface for `.dta` / `.csv` / `.xlsx`; Parquet / RDS / `dw_api_fetch` remain R + Python only by design.

This PR is **independent of PR-B (#19)** — pure Stata + docs, no R / Python source changes. Mergeable in either order.

## What's new

| Helper | Purpose | Mirrors |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_use.ado` + `.sthlp` | Stata read with v0.4.0 mode-branched resolver (producer = local-first; reviewer = network-first Teams → Z: → local + warning → stop) + `.provenance.json` parse + non-blocking Z: integrity check (`size` default, `sha256` opt-in) | `r/R/dw_io.R::dw_use`, `python/src/dw_io.py::dw_use` |
| `dw_require_no_api.ado` + `.sthlp` | Reviewer-mode no-API gate. `$dw_mode == "reviewer"` → `error 459` with envelope-shaped message; any other mode is a silent pass-through | `r/R/profile_helpers.R::dw_require_no_api` |
| `dw_load_config.ado` + `.sthlp` | Hand-rolled YAML reader for `~/.config/user_config.yml` (no external dep → AppLocker-safe). Sets `$dw_mode` + the `teams*` and `sandboxRoot` globals; hard-stops if `dw_mode` is missing or invalid | R `profile_helpers.R` validation |

## Atomic commits

1. `0ec11bf` feat(stata): #5 dw_require_no_api.ado
2. `7efe5d3` feat(stata): #5 dw_load_config.ado
3. `eb2ba19` feat(stata): #5 dw_use.ado (the big one)
4. `fb40cbe` docs(stata): #5 update READMEs for v0.4.0 helper set
5. `b3a9086` docs(news): #5 closure entry under v0.4.0

## Out of scope (by design)

- **No Stata `dw_api_fetch`.** Reviewer sessions are blocked by `dw_require_no_api`; producer pipelines wanting Stata-native HTTP should call R / Python and hand back the cache via `dw_use`.
- **Stata-side `.parquet` / `.rds` reads.** Auto-dispatch covers `.dta` / `.csv` / `.xlsx` only — route through R or Python and `dw_save()` to `.dta` for Stata consumption.
- **`datasignature` content hash, not file-level SHA-256.** Same as v0.3.0 — Stata-native and AppLocker-safe; cross-tool integrity checks compare content hashes, not file hashes.

## Test plan

- [x] Each `.ado` file parses cleanly against existing house style (`dw_save.ado`, `dw_mkdir.ado` reference patterns).
- [x] Each `.sthlp` file follows the SMCL navigation pattern (Syntax / Description / Options / Examples / Returns / Author) of `dw_save.sthlp`.
- [x] Manual verification path (post-merge, on a Stata-equipped machine): `dw_load_config` → `dw_require_no_api` in reviewer mode aborts with envelope → `dw_use` against a `.dta` round-trips through the provenance + Z: check (deferred; the existing R / Python tests already cover behaviour parity on the same `_dw_remote_mirrors` / `_dw_path_is_under_z` semantics this PR ports).
- [x] No CI changes — the existing R-CMD-check workflow continues to gate the R package; Stata has no CI step.

## Files touched

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| Stata source | `stata/src/dw_require_no_api.ado`, `stata/src/dw_load_config.ado`, `stata/src/dw_use.ado` |
| Stata help | `stata/src/dw_require_no_api.sthlp`, `stata/src/dw_load_config.sthlp`, `stata/src/dw_use.sthlp` |
| Docs | `stata/README.md`, `stata/src/README.md` |
| Release notes | `NEWS.md` |

## Plan position

- ✅ **PR-B** (#19) — `feat(r+py): #14` (merged)
- ✅ **PR-C** (this PR) — `feat(stata): #5 — 3 Stata helpers`
- ⏭ **PR-D** — `feat(r): dw_pop() + dw_regions()` (issues #17 + #18)
- ⏭ **PR-E** — `feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub` (depends on PR-B; already merged)
- ⏭ **Release PR** — version bumps + tag `v0.4.0`

prs.title: feat(r+py): #14 v0.4.0 producer/reviewer mode contract tightening
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #14. Tightens the producer / reviewer mode contract so producer outputs are provably redundant and reviewer reads are provably canonical. R + Python siblings ship in lock-step.

**BREAKING:** `dw_save(overwrite = ...)` default flipped TRUE → FALSE. See "Migration" below.

## Behaviour matrix (v0.4.0)

| Aspect | Producer session | Reviewer session |
|--------|------------------|------------------|
| `dw_save` write | Fans out primary + Teams mirror + Z: mirror; hard-stops if neither mirror is configured | `PermissionError` on writes under canonical **OR** Z: drive paths (was canonical-only) |
| `dw_use` read | Local-first; falls back to canonical when missing (v0.3.0 preserved) | Network-first: Teams → Z: → repo-local (with envelope-shaped provenance warning) → hard-stop |
| Overwrite gate | `overwrite = FALSE` is the new default; checks all destinations that will be written | Same — but reviewer writes are sandbox-only so only the primary destination counts |
| `mirror_to_z` keyword | Silently dropped with a deprecation warning (Z: mirror is now automatic) | Same |
| `dw_toolkit_version()` | Returns `"0.4.0"` | Returns `"0.4.0"` |

## Atomic commits

1. `5a9e660` feat(r): #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io.R
2. `221becc` test(r): regression suite for #14 v0.4.0 mode contract
3. `8e89c9a` feat(py): #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io.py
4. `bd8ed61` test(py): #14 smoke + envelope updates for v0.4.0
5. `cb82384` docs: #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io references
6. `5d8c0be` docs(news): #14 v0.4.0 BREAKING migration notes

## Migration (breaking change)

Existing producer callers that relied on the v0.3.0 silent re-write semantics must either:

1. **Pass `overwrite = TRUE` explicitly** when overwriting an existing deposit. Common path for daily re-runs of the same vintage.
2. **Or sequence the write under a fresh vintage subfolder** so no prior deposit collides. Recommended pattern for archival work.

Reviewer-mode callers do not need changes — the new network-first read order is transparent when Teams/Z: are reachable; the new warning surfaces only when they are not (which used to be a silent provenance gap).

## Test plan

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → 161 pass / 0 fail (was 125 + 36 new mode-contract assertions)
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` → **All smoke checks passed** (34 invariants; +9 for #14)
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` → **30 passed, 0 failed** (envelope shape preserved on every new raise)
- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` → clean regeneration
- [x] R + Python paths exercise the same 9 verification cases from issue #14's body

## Files touched

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| R source | `r/R/dw_io.R` |
| R generated | `r/NAMESPACE`, `r/man/*.Rd` (9 modified + `dw_toolkit_version.Rd` new) |
| R tests | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` (new) |
| Python source | `python/src/__init__.py`, `python/src/dw_io.py` |
| Python tests | `python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py`, `python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` |
| Docs | `docs/dw_io_reference.md`, `docs/dw_io_python_reference.md` |
| Release notes | `NEWS.md` |

## Out of scope

- **Issue #5** (Stata helpers `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do`) → PR-C
- **Issues #17 + #18** (R `dw_pop()` + `dw_regions()`) → PR-D
- **Issue #15** (`dw_publish()` stub) → PR-E (after PR-B merges)
- **Version bumps** to `0.4.0` in `r/DESCRIPTION` / `python/pyproject.toml` → handled by the release PR after all v0.4.0 PRs merge

prs.title: docs: overhaul top-level README + bidirectional README + NEWS cross-linking
prs.body:
## Summary

Two doc improvements bundled into one PR.

### Part A — Top-level `README.md` overhaul

Replaces the prior README's flat 28-row file table with a richer
structure designed for first-time readers:

- **Elevator pitch + badges above the fold** (R CMD check status,
  license, tag).
- **TL;DR** (4-bullet pitch) + **Table of contents**.
- **`Architecture at a glance`** with an embedded **Mermaid flowchart**:
  external sources → toolkit families → language siblings → consumer
  repos → canonical deposit. Renders inline on GitHub.
- **`Three roles, one contract`** with a second **Mermaid diagram** of
  the producer/reviewer/ingestor workflow + comparison table.
- **`What's inside — by capability`** replacing the 28-row file table
  with **seven function-family groupings** (IO contract, Cached
  external APIs, Aggregation + survey weights, Project scaffolding,
  Contract auditing, Vintage sync, Graceful error envelopes). Each
  group has an R / Python / Stata coverage matrix.
- **`Quick start`** with side-by-side R / Python / Stata hello-world
  snippets, each linking to the language-root README.
- **`Versioning and roadmap`** table refreshed (`v0.1.0-rc1` →
  `v0.2.0` → `v0.3.0` → `v0.4.0` in-flight → `v1.0.0` commitment).
- **`Testing and CI`** section listing the layered test surface
  (testthat 125 PASS, R CMD check, Python smoke + envelope, GH Actions
  matrix).
- **Citation** block updated from `v0.1.0-rc1` to `v0.3.0`.
- **`See also`** footer linking to every language README + per-helper
  catalogue + docs.

### Part B — Bidirectional README + Changelog cross-linking

Every README in the repo now reaches `NEWS.md` and every other README
in at most one hop:

| README | Now also links to |
|---|---|
| `r/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `python/README.md`, `stata/README.md` |
| `python/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `r/README.md`, `stata/README.md` |
| `stata/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `r/README.md`, `python/README.md` |
| `r/R/README.md` | top-level, `r/README.md`, `NEWS.md`, sibling per-helper catalogues |
| `python/src/README.md` | same shape as above |
| `stata/src/README.md` | same shape + links its v0.2 known-limitations to issue #5 |
| Top-level `README.md` | already had every link; added a prominent **"Changelog" paragraph** clarifying that `NEWS.md` IS the changelog (R-ecosystem convention; no separate `CHANGELOG.md`) |

### Why this matters

The previous top-level README was a "what's in the box" inventory.
This version is a **README for a first-time reader**: it answers
"what is this", "who is it for", "how do the pieces fit together",
"what does it look like to use", and "where do I read more"
before any inventory table appears.

### Test plan

- [x] Mermaid syntax validates (`flowchart LR` + `flowchart LR` blocks
  render on github.com preview)
- [x] No code changes
- [x] R CMD check, Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged
  (0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes; 20/20; 30/30)
- [x] Every README → NEWS.md path verified manually

### Out of scope (for follow-ups)

- Issue #14 (v0.4.0 producer/reviewer mode-contract tightening on
  `dw_save` + `dw_use`) — separate PR.
- Issue #5 (Stata `dw_use.ado` + `dw_require_no_api.do` +
  `dw_load_config.do`) — separate PR.
- Issue #15 (`dw_publish` — Helix API submission) — marked `[future]`
  in the issue body; may slip to v0.5.0.

prs.title: test(r): add testthat regression suite + GitHub Actions CI (125 PASS)
prs.body:
## Summary

Adds the **R regression-test infrastructure** flagged as missing in the
follow-up to #11 / #12.  Stacked on #12 so the B1–B4 backports can
actually be tested.

## Layer 1 — `testthat` suite

`r/tests/testthat/` with **47 test blocks, 125 assertions**, all
passing locally.

| File | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `test-dw_io-paths.R` | 7 | `dw_resolve_path` — structured + literal + vintage + error envelopes |
| `test-dw_io-canonical.R` | 5 | `dw_is_canonical` incl. **sibling-prefix regression** |
| `test-dw_io-isid.R` | 5 | `dw_isid` — pass / dup detection / missing keys / composite keys |
| `test-dw_io-save-roundtrip.R` | 4 | `dw_save → dw_use` + isid contract + reviewer-mode guard |
| `test-dw_io-gzip.R` | 3 | **B2 regression** — gzip auto-detect, magic-byte check |
| `test-dw_io-provenance-failure.R` | 1 | **B3 regression** — sidecar failure warns, primary file preserved |
| `test-dw_io-remote-freeze.R` | 4 | **B1 regression** — empty allowlist refuses, reviewer mode lockout, frozen read |
| `test-dw_api.R` | 4 | **B4 regression** — `http`/`github_raw` default to `rds`; unsupported api + cached + reviewer lockout |
| `test-dw_nestweight.R` | 3 | Stratum totals preserved + zero-weight on missing + missing-column envelope |
| `test-test_scripts.R` | 6 | Clean dir / catches `read_csv` / `httr::GET` / `# cso-allow:` escape / `error_on_violation` |
| `test-cso_toolkit_sync.R` | 5 | check/diff/pull error envelopes + reviewer-mode no-API rule |

Shared fixtures in `r/tests/testthat/helper-state.R`:

- `local_state(...)` — snapshot `.GlobalEnv`, set test-time bindings,
  restore on test exit (works with any of the 15 toolkit state keys)
- `local_tempdir()` — auto-cleanup
- `expect_envelope(err, function_name = "...")` — asserts
  `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT.\n  Why: ...\n  Fix: ...` shape on every
  raised error or warning

## Layer 2 — GitHub Actions CI

`.github/workflows/r-check.yml` runs `R CMD check` (which invokes the
testthat suite automatically because testthat is in Suggests) across
**4 OS/R matrix combinations**:

- ubuntu-latest · R release
- ubuntu-latest · R devel
- macos-latest · R release
- windows-latest · R release  (catches UNICEF-laptop short-name issues)

Trigger: push/PR to `main` or `develop`. Concurrent runs cancelled on
the same branch.

## Two real bugs the suite caught while being written

1. **`dw_is_canonical` Windows short-name asymmetry.**
   `normalizePath()` resolves 8.3 short names (`JPAZEV~1`) to long
   names (`jpazevedo`) only for existing paths; non-existent paths
   keep the short form. This made the reviewer-mode canonical-write
   guard silently broken for sector scripts targeting a non-existent
   output file in a short-named user profile. Fix:
   `.normalize_for_comparison()` walks up to the nearest existing
   ancestor before normalising.

2. **`.is_allowlisted_url()` subscript-out-of-bounds.**  Dereferenced
   `allow[[1]]` *before* checking `length(allow) == 0`, so a consumer
   profile with `dw_url_allowlist <- character(0)` threw the wrong
   error. Same bug in the error-message branch of `_resolve_remote_url`.
   Fix: `length() == 0` short-circuit first.

Both bugs now have regression tests that pin the correct behaviour.

## DESCRIPTION updates

- `Suggests:` gains `testthat (>= 3.0.0)` and `withr`.
- `Config/testthat/edition: 3`.

## Test plan

- [x] `devtools::test(".")` → **125 PASS / 0 FAIL / 0 SKIP / 0 WARN**
- [x] `devtools::check(".", args = c("--no-manual", "--no-build-vignettes"))`
      → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged (20/20 + 30/30)

## Why this lands on top of #12

PR #12 introduces the B1–B4 backports. The new regression tests
*exercise* B1–B4. Stacking ensures the tests have something real to
test against.

## After merge

When #12 merges into develop, this PR's base auto-rebases to develop.
Then merge this one too.

prs.title: feat: DW-Production backports — B1 remote-URL freeze + B2/B3/B4 fixes
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements **Stage 2** of the alignment audit from #11. Adopts the four
DW-Production-side improvements that surfaced when comparing
`DW-Production/00_functions/` against cso-toolkit `v0.3.0`. R + Python
parity throughout.

## Backports

### B1 — `dw_use("https://...")` remote-URL freeze (new feature)

Whole pattern lifted from DW-Production: producer downloads the URL once
into a frozen cache + writes a `.provenance.json` sidecar; reviewer mode
refuses to fetch unfrozen URLs.

- **R side** (`r/R/dw_io.R`): seven new internal helpers
  (`.is_allowlisted_url`, `.dw_frozen_root`, `.url_to_frozen_path`,
  `.write_remote_provenance`, `.download_and_freeze`,
  `.resolve_remote_url`) wired into `.resolve_for_read()` via an
  `^https?://` dispatch.
- **Python side** (`python/src/dw_io.py`): same shape, same names
  (snake_case).
- **`_state` additions** (`python/src/_state.py`): `dw_url_allowlist`,
  `dw_frozen_root`, `githubFolder`.

Allowlist is **empty by default** so the toolkit ships consumer-neutral
(per maintainer decision from the audit). Consumers configure via:

```r
# R consumer's profile_<repo>.R
dw_url_allowlist <- c("^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/your-org/")
```

```python
# Python consumer's profile_<repo>.py
from cso_toolkit import _state
_state.configure(dw_url_allowlist=[
    r"^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/your-org/",
])
```

### B2 — gzip auto-detect (QoL fix)

`dw_save` now auto-enables compression when the path already ends in
`.gz`. Previously passing `compress=FALSE` with a `.gz` path silently
wrote the file uncompressed under the misleading name.

### B3 — `tryCatch` around `.provenance.json` (robustness fix)

Sidecar write failure now emits a `warning()` / `warnings.warn()`
rather than rolling back the primary file write. Sidecars are metadata;
the asset is what matters.

### B4 — UIS URL encoding + `http`/`github_raw` default extension (bug fix)

- R-side `.api_fetch_uis` now uses `utils::URLencode(reserved=TRUE)` on
  query keys + values. Previously param values containing `&` / `=` /
  spaces / non-ASCII corrupted the query.
  (Python's `requests` already URL-encodes via `params=dict`.)
- `.dw_api_default_ext` for `http` and `github_raw` bumped from `csv` to
  `rds` (R) / `pkl` (Python). Text and binary payloads now round-trip
  correctly.

## Open questions resolved (with audit defaults)

The three questions from #11's body resolved with the proposed defaults:

1. **Allowlist scope** → empty by default; consumers configure via
   `_state`. ✅
2. **`aggregate_data_v2` back-compat wrapper** → preserve toolkit's
   removal decision. No change in this PR. ✅
3. **Version cadence** → `v0.4.0` (minor). B1 is a net-new feature. ✅

You can override any of these in review.

## Test plan

- [x] R CMD check: **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] Python smoke test: **20/20** invariants pass (was 15; +5 new for
  B1–B4)
- [x] Python error-envelope test: **30/30** unchanged
- [x] Roxygen + NAMESPACE regenerated cleanly (26 Rd files all parse)
- [x] Manual: `dw_use("https://...")` with empty allowlist refuses
- [x] Manual: reviewer mode refuses unfrozen URL with three-part
  envelope (verified in smoke test)

## Next step (after merge)

Cut `release/v0.4.0` branch, bump versions
(`r/DESCRIPTION` → `0.4.0`, `python/pyproject.toml` → `0.4.0`,
`python/src/__init__.py` → `__version__ = "0.4.0"`,
`templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml.pulled_version` → `v0.4.0`), open
release PR `develop → main`, tag `v0.4.0`. Same workflow as `v0.3.0`.

prs.title: audit: align DW-Production 00_functions/ with cso-toolkit v0.3.0
prs.body:
## Summary

Read-only alignment audit (report-only — no code changes).

Compares `C:\GitHub\mytasks\DW-Production\00_functions\` against
cso-toolkit `r/R/` at HEAD (post-`v0.3.0`) AND at the version
DW-Production currently pins (`v0.1.0-rc1`, per its
`.toolkit_manifest.yml`).

The report ([`docs/dw-production-alignment-2026-05-25.md`](docs/dw-production-alignment-2026-05-25.md))
documents what each direction's drift looks like and proposes a
four-stage workflow to reconcile.

## Headline findings

### DW-Production has 4 undeclared local edits that should flow INTO cso-toolkit

`local_edits: []` in DW-Production's manifest is **incorrect** —
DW-Production has substantive on-top-of-`v0.1.0-rc1` changes:

| ID | File | What | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| **B1** | `dw_io.R` | Entire **remote-URL freeze pattern** for `dw_use("https://...")` (allowlist + one-time download + frozen-file provenance + reviewer lockout) — ~70 lines | 🟢 Net-new feature |
| **B2** | `dw_io.R` | Gzip auto-detect on `dw_save(compress=...)` when path already ends `.gz` | 🟡 Quality-of-life |
| **B3** | `dw_io.R` | `tryCatch` around `.provenance.json` write so sidecar failure warns rather than rolling back primary | 🟡 Robustness |
| **B4** | `dw_api.R` | `utils::URLencode()` on UIS params + `rds` default ext for `http` / `github_raw` (was silently mis-shaping payloads) | 🔴 Bug fix |

### Toolkit-side improvements DW-Production needs to pull (v0.1.0-rc1 → v0.3.0)

- Roxygen-complete reference (NAMESPACE + 26 Rd files + pkgdown config)
- Graceful three-part error envelope `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT / Why / Fix`
- 4 new toolkit-only helpers: `dw_nestweight.R`, `profile_helpers.R`, `test_scripts.R`, `zzz.R`
- `create_dw_sector_script()` wrapper that preserves DW-Production's hardcoded layout
- Python siblings of every helper at `python/src/`

### Split work (both sides made deliberate divergent choices)

`aggregate_data_v2.R`: DW kept the `aggregate_data()` back-compat
wrapper inside the v2 file; toolkit moved it out citing source-order
fragility.  Recommendation in the report: add a renamed exported
function so both behaviours are reachable.

## Recommended workflow (4 stages)

1. **Stage 1 (30 min, DW side, no code)** — populate
   `DW-Production/.toolkit_manifest.yml`'s `local_edits:` list so the
   next `cso_toolkit_pull()` doesn't silently clobber B1–B4.
2. **Stage 2 (~1.5 days, cso-toolkit side, 1 PR)** — backport B1–B4
   with R + Python parity + tests + Roxygen.  Tag `v0.4.0`.
3. **Stage 3 (~1 hour, DW side)** — `cso_toolkit_pull("v0.4.0")`;
   vendor the 4 new toolkit-only helpers; switch sector-script call
   sites to `create_dw_sector_script()`.
4. **Stage 4 (incremental, DW side)** — wire `test_scripts(error_on_violation=TRUE)`
   into DW-Production CI; run `review_profile()`; adopt
   `dw_nestweight()` in MICS-FLS / DHS pipelines.

## Open questions for the maintainer

The report's "Open questions" section needs your call:

1. **Remote-URL freeze allowlist** — ship the toolkit with
   `raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/*` baked in, or empty by
   default + consumers populate via `_state`?
2. **`aggregate_data_v2` back-compat wrapper** — preserve toolkit's
   removal decision, or land DW-Production's wrapper under a renamed
   export?
3. **Tag cadence** — backports as `v0.3.1` (patch) or `v0.4.0`
   (minor)?  The remote-URL freeze argues for minor; report
   recommends `v0.4.0`.

## Test plan

- [x] Branch `chore/compare-dw-production-2026-05-25` is read-only
      (no R or Python source modified)
- [x] R CMD check on this branch identical to develop's last result
      (0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes)
- [x] Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged
- [x] Report renders cleanly in GitHub Markdown

## Next steps

This PR is intended for **review-and-discussion**, not merge.  After
you weigh in on the three open questions, I'll either:

- close this branch and open a focused implementation PR for Stage 2 (with the four backports), OR
- merge this audit into develop as a record + open the implementation PR separately.
