prs.title: release: v0.3.0 — Python parity + Roxygen-complete R + graceful errors
prs.body:
## Release: v0.3.0 (2026-05-25)

First release with full **Python parity** for every R helper, plus the
**R Roxygen-complete reference** (NAMESPACE + 26 Rd files + pkgdown
config), and a **three-part error envelope**
(`[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT / Why / Fix`) standardised across R and
Python.

## Summary of changes since v0.2.0

### Python helpers — new

- `python/src/` — full Python port of every R helper. Same behaviour
  contract; mode-aware path routing, Z: drive mirror, provenance
  sidecars, version-drift detection. 10 modules + foundation
  (`_state.py`, `__init__.py`); 26 public entries.
- `python/pyproject.toml` — optional `pip install -e python/` for local
  development. Optional-dep groups: `excel`, `parquet`, `yaml`, `http`,
  `sdmx`, `worldbank`, `test`, `all`.
- `python/src/py.typed` — PEP 561 marker.
- `python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` — 15-invariant end-to-end test.
- `python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` — 30-path contract test
  asserting every public raise emits the `[cso_toolkit.<func>]` prefix
  + `Fix:` guidance.
- `docs/dw_io_python_reference.md` + `docs/dw_api_python_reference.md`.

### R helpers — documentation hygiene

- Roxygen pass across `dw_io.R`, `dw_api.R`, `cso_toolkit_sync.R`,
  `generate_markdown_report.R`, and `aggregate_data.R`. Every exported
  function carries `@param` / `@return` / `@export`; every internal
  helper carries `@keywords internal` + `@noRd`.
- `@seealso` + `@family` Roxygen tags on every export, grouped into
  eight families: `io`, `api`, `sync`, `aggregate`, `survey-weights`,
  `reporting`, `scaffolding`, `audit`.
- Generated `r/NAMESPACE` (26 exports) + `r/man/` (26 `.Rd` files).
- `r/_pkgdown.yml` — pkgdown reference site config with the eight
  grouped sections, UNICEF cyan/navy theme.
- `r/LICENSE` — CRAN-style 2-line MIT pointer.
- `r/R/zzz.R` — single shared `.cso_require()` helper +
  `utils::globalVariables()` declarations.

### Graceful error envelopes (R + Python)

- Every R `stop()` / `warning()` and every Python `raise` follows the
  three-part `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT.\n  Why: ...\n  Fix: ...`
  shape with `call. = FALSE` (R) or typed exceptions (Python).
- Python: HTTP / JSON-parse / CSV-parse / YAML failures wrapped with
  URL + status + body snippet; no bare `requests.HTTPError` /
  `KeyError` / `pd.errors.ParserError` bubbles up uncaught.
- Python: sensitive keys (`token`, `headers`, `api_key`, `password`,
  …) in `dw_api_fetch` kwargs redacted before they reach the
  `.provenance.json` sidecar.

### Sync / vendoring infrastructure

- `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — header expanded with a
  field-by-field reference; `pulled_version` bumped to `v0.3.0`.
- `r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` — release-eve smoke test that
  simulates a consumer repo end-to-end against api.github.com.

### Documentation

- New `r/README.md`, `python/README.md`, `stata/README.md` —
  per-language top-level READMEs with install paths, layout,
  quick-start, mode contract, error envelope, testing.
- Top-level `README.md` Status + Versioning bullets refreshed
  (previously frozen on `v0.1.0-rc1`).

## Version bumps

- `r/DESCRIPTION` — `Version: 0.3.0`, `Date: 2026-05-25`
- `python/pyproject.toml` — `version = "0.3.0"`
- `python/src/__init__.py` — `__version__ = "0.3.0"`
- `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — `pulled_version: "v0.3.0"`

## Verification

- [x] `R CMD check` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] Python smoke test → **15/15** invariants pass
- [x] Python error-envelope test → **30/30** raise paths pass
- [x] `tools::parse_Rd()` on every `r/man/*.Rd` → clean
- [x] `cso_toolkit.__version__ == "0.3.0"` (Python)
- [x] Both feature PRs (#6, #7) reviewed by Copilot; all substantive
      findings addressed before this release

## Post-merge

Tag `v0.3.0` on `main` once this merges. Update any consumer
`.toolkit_manifest.yml` files with `pulled_version: "v0.3.0"`.

prs.title: feat(python): port all 10 R helpers to python/src/
prs.body:
## Summary

Python parity for the R helpers in `r/R/`.  Companion to #6 (Roxygen +
sync); together they fulfil the "review all R functions + generate the
Python equivalent" ask.  No R-side or Stata-side files touched —
this PR is purely additive under `python/`.

### Architecture

Vendoring mirrors the R side: drop `python/src/` into a consumer repo's
`00_functions/` (renamed `cso_toolkit/`), then:

```python
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "00_functions")
from cso_toolkit import dw_save, dw_use, dw_api_fetch
from cso_toolkit import _state as cso_state
```

Session-level globals (mode, paths, Z: drive availability) configured
once at profile load via `cso_state.configure(...)`.

### Module catalogue (10 modules + foundation)

| R file                          | Python file                      | Public entries |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | -------------- |
| `dw_io.R`                       | `dw_io.py`                       | `dw_save`, `dw_use`, `dw_compare`, `dw_merge`, `dw_isid`, `dw_verify_z`, `dw_resolve_path`, `dw_is_canonical` |
| `dw_api.R`                      | `dw_api.py`                      | `dw_api_fetch`, `dw_api_cached`, `dw_api_inventory` |
| `cso_toolkit_sync.R`            | `cso_toolkit_sync.py`            | `cso_toolkit_check`, `cso_toolkit_diff`, `cso_toolkit_pull` |
| `aggregate_data.R` + `aggregate_data_v2.R` | `aggregate_data.py`   | `aggregate_data`, `aggregate_data_v2`, `generate_agg_footnote`, `apply_time_window` |
| `dw_nestweight.R`               | `dw_nestweight.py`               | `dw_nestweight` |
| `generate_markdown_report.R`    | `generate_markdown_report.py`    | `generate_markdown_report`, `process_all_csv_files` |
| `create_sector_script.R`        | `create_sector_script.py`        | `create_sector_script`, `create_dw_sector_script` |
| `profile_helpers.R`             | `profile_helpers.py`             | `create_profile`, `review_profile` |
| `test_scripts.R`                | `test_scripts.py`                | `test_scripts` (now scans `.py` for raw IO/HTTP) |
| —                               | `_state.py`                      | `configure`, `_get` |
| —                               | `__init__.py`                    | re-exports all 26 public entries |

### Behaviour-parity highlights

- **Path resolution** — same `kind = "wrk" | "raw" | "meta"` matrix,
  same `name` / `sector` / `vintage` arguments.
- **Mode contract** — `dw_save` raises `PermissionError` (Python
  analogue of R's `stop()`) on reviewer-mode canonical writes without
  `allow_canonical_write=True`.
- **Z: drive mirror** — automatic carbon-copy on canonical writes;
  non-blocking size or sha256 integrity check on canonical reads.
- **Provenance sidecar** — identical JSON schema to R (`path`,
  `format`, `written_at`, `user`, `dw_mode`, `vintage`, `sha256`,
  `isid`, `schema`, optional `metadata`).
- **Auto-dispatch on extension** — `.csv` / `.tsv` / `.txt` / `.xlsx` /
  `.parquet` / `.dta` / `.json` / `.yaml`, plus Python additions
  `.pkl` / `.pickle` (analogue of R's `.rds`).

### Scaffolding adaptations

The three scaffolding helpers generate Python (not R):

- `create_sector_script.py` writes `00_run_<code>.py` with profile
  import, `log_message` usage, `start_time` tracking, and a
  `try / except` block.
- `create_profile.py` writes `profile_<repo>.py` that wires into the
  Python toolkit via `cso_toolkit._state.configure(dw_mode=..., dw_apis_allowed=...)`.
- `test_scripts.py` registry detects Python-flavoured violations
  (`pd.read_csv`, `df.to_excel`, `requests.get`, `sdmx.Client`,
  `wbgapi.data.*`, etc.) instead of the R-flavoured ones.

### Dependency layering

- **Core** (always required): `pandas`, `numpy`.
- **Format-specific** (lazy-imported): `openpyxl` (xlsx), `pyarrow`
  (parquet), `PyYAML` (yaml).
- **API-specific** (lazy-imported by `dw_api_fetch` only): `requests`,
  `sdmx1`, `wbgapi`.

Consumers running purely reviewer-mode pipelines never touch the
API libs.

## Test plan

- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` — 12 in-process checks
  pass end-to-end (see commit `9758f50` body for the full list)
- [x] `cso_toolkit.__version__` resolves to `"0.2.1.dev0"`
- [x] `len(cso_toolkit.__all__) == 27`
- [x] `dw_save` → `dw_use` CSV roundtrip + `.provenance.json` sidecar
- [x] `dw_isid` rejects duplicates with sample rows in the error
- [x] `aggregate_data_v2(method="weighted_mean", coverage_threshold=0.5)`
- [x] `dw_nestweight` preserves stratum totals to 1e-9 tolerance
- [x] `create_profile` → `review_profile` reports 7-pass / 0-fail
- [x] `test_scripts` 0 violations on clean file; 1 violation
  (`io-read-csv`) on file with raw `pd.read_csv`
- [x] `cso_toolkit_check` with missing manifest returns `None`

## Sequencing note

PR #6 (Roxygen + sync) and this PR are **independent** code-wise.
Either can merge first.  Reading them as a pair tells the full story
(R docs polished → Python port lands), but the Python port doesn't
depend on the R Roxygen artefacts to compile or run.

prs.title: docs(r) + chore(r): Roxygen pass + sync infrastructure
prs.body:
## Summary

Two atomic phases of pre-Python-port hygiene on the R helpers.

### docs(r): Roxygen pass on 5 R files + regenerate NAMESPACE/man (66f2dec)

Brings the 5 partially-documented R files up to the Roxygen-complete bar
already set by `aggregate_data_v2.R`, `dw_nestweight.R`,
`create_sector_script.R`, `profile_helpers.R`, and `test_scripts.R`.

| File                          | Functions touched |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `dw_io.R`                     | 9 exported + ~10 internal |
| `dw_api.R`                    | 3 exported + 9 internal fetchers |
| `cso_toolkit_sync.R`          | 3 exported + 3 internal |
| `generate_markdown_report.R`  | 2 exported |
| `aggregate_data.R`            | added missing `@param country.coverage`, cleaned `@export` whitespace |

Every exported function now carries `@param` / `@return` / `@export`;
every internal helper carries `@keywords internal` + `@noRd`. Also
fixed a Roxygen-block bug in `aggregate_data_v2.R` where the top-of-file
banner used `#'` and got attached to `.cso_require`, generating a spurious
`dot-cso_require.Rd`.

Regenerated artefacts via `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)`:

- `r/NAMESPACE` — first-time generation; exports 26 functions.
- `r/man/*.Rd` — 26 files, all parse cleanly via `tools::parse_Rd()`.

**No behaviour change.** Pure documentation.

### chore(r): sync infrastructure + bump to 0.2.1.9000 (20b195e)

Three small additions that move the vendoring contract forward.

- **(a)** `r/DESCRIPTION` → `0.2.1.9000` + `NEWS.md` Unreleased entries.
- **(b)** `r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` — manual smoke test that
  simulates a consumer repo, drops the three core helpers + manifest into
  it, sources them, and calls `cso_toolkit_check()` end-to-end against
  the live GitHub API. Verifies the returned list shape (`source`,
  `pinned_version`, `upstream_version`, `updates_available`,
  `updated_files`). "Manual" because the test hits the GitHub API; not
  part of any automated suite.
- **(c)** `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — header expanded with a
  field-by-field reference; `pulled_version` bumped `v0.1.0-rc1 → v0.2.0`;
  the seven optional helpers (`aggregate_data`, `dw_nestweight`, etc.)
  listed as commented-out entries so consumers can opt in without
  re-deriving the file list.

Smoke-test output as of this PR:

```
[cso-toolkit] unicef-drp/cso-toolkit: pinned v0.2.0 == upstream v0.2.0 (up to date)
OK: shape matches expected (source, pinned_version, upstream_version, updates_available, updated_files).
```

## Why this lands before the Python port

The Python port will derive its docstrings from the R Roxygen blocks
(one source of truth per function). Doing the Roxygen pass first means
the Python port lands with docstrings that match the R reference exactly
— no later sync churn.

## Test plan

- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` writes 26 Rd files, 0 errors
- [x] `tools::parse_Rd()` on every Rd file → all clean
- [x] `devtools::load_all('.', export_all = FALSE)` → loads cleanly
- [x] `Rscript r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` → end-to-end pass against api.github.com

prs.title: release: develop -> main (Stata helpers, R toolkit expansion, CSO rebrand, workflow diagrams)
prs.body:
## Summary

Promotes `develop` to `main` after the three feature PRs (#1, #2, #3) landed on develop earlier today (2026-05-24). This is the **first substantive release** beyond the initial scaffold.

### What this release adds

**R helpers (`r/R/`)** — 7 new files:
- `aggregate_data.R` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` — `aggregate_data()` (legacy) + `aggregate_data_v2()` with `weighted_mean` / `mean` / `sum` / `proportion`, coverage threshold, metadata columns; plus `generate_agg_footnote()` and `apply_time_window()`
- `generate_markdown_report.R` — descriptive-stats Markdown report from a CSV file or a folder of CSVs
- `create_sector_script.R` — `create_sector_script(sector_name, sector_code, base_dir, ...)` + DW-Production convenience wrapper `create_dw_sector_script()`
- `profile_helpers.R` — `create_profile(repo_name, ...)` scaffolds a `profile_<repo>.R`; `review_profile(path, ...)` audits an existing one against the toolkit contract
- `test_scripts.R` — `test_scripts(path, ...)` recursively scans `.R` scripts and flags direct calls to file-IO or external-API commands wrapped by `dw_io.R` / `dw_api.R` (16 built-in rules; per-line `# cso-allow: <rule-id>` escape hatch; CI mode via `error_on_violation = TRUE`)
- `dw_nestweight.R` — `dw_nestweight()` redistributes survey weights from missing nested observations (R port of `edukit_nestweight`)

**Stata helpers (`stata/src/`)** — first three Stata helpers fill the v0.2 placeholder:
- `dw_save.ado` — Stata sibling of R `dw_save()`. `isid` + `compress` + `save` + sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar (JSON-escaped); enforces producer / reviewer mode via `$dw_mode`
- `dw_compare.ado` — Stata sibling of R `dw_compare()`. Merges on `idvars` and classifies per value column; optional Markdown report
- `dw_mkdir.ado` — recursive `mkdir`
- Companion `.sthlp` help files for all three

**Docs (`docs/`)**:
- `dw_io_reference.md` / `dw_api_reference.md` — per-function references
- `git_workflow.md` — gitflow + branch-protection contract reference
- `roles_and_workflow.md` — extended with Mermaid data-flow + role-vs-action diagrams

**Branding + meta**:
- Top-level `README.md` rebranded as **UNICEF Chief Statistician Office toolkit**, with a new **Objective and motivation** section spelling out the reproducibility-and-scalability mission for the D&A Section in OSE
- `NEWS.md` Unreleased section enumerating every addition
- `r/R/README.md` and `stata/src/README.md` updated to the live helper inventory

### Lineage credits

Three Stata helpers and the R `dw_nestweight` are ports from the World Bank [EduAnalyticsToolkit](https://github.com/worldbank/EduAnalyticsToolkit). Each ported file credits the original author (Diana Goldemberg for `dw_save` / `dw_nestweight`, Kristoffer Bjärkefur for `dw_compare` / `dw_mkdir`).

### Review fixes folded in

Every PR landed with all Copilot review comments addressed: 11 fixes on PR #1 (multi-col `by` correctness, `n_distinct` NA-counting, namespace hygiene, removed source-time side effects, `%||%` collision, generated-template defaults), 2 on PR #2 (Mermaid paths matching documented layout, pushed-commit recovery recipe), 5 on PR #3 (`capture isid` vs `quietly isid`, JSON-escaped sidecar values, DRY compare logic, non-numeric guard).

## Suggested next steps after merge

- [ ] Tag `v0.2.0` on `main` per the versioning policy (v0.1.0-rc1 was scaffold; v0.2 was scoped to ship the Stata side, which now exists)
- [ ] Update the GitHub release notes from this PR body
- [ ] Open a tracking issue for the three remaining Stata gaps: `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do`

## Commit log

23 commits from main → develop (atomic per logical change). See the Files Changed tab for the full diff (+3,047 / −30 across 21 files).

## Test plan

- [x] All R files parse (`Rscript -e parse(...)`); test_scripts self-audit returns 0 violations
- [x] R smoke tests: `aggregate_data` multi-col `by`; `create_profile` + `review_profile` round-trip; `dw_nestweight` numeric + non-numeric
- [ ] Stata files: smoke-tested manually post-merge against a real Stata install (Stata not on CI / sandbox)
- [ ] Visual confirm: Mermaid diagrams render on GitHub for `roles_and_workflow.md` + `git_workflow.md`

prs.title: feat: port edukit_save / comparefiles / rmkdir to Stata as dw_* helpers, and edukit_nestweight to R as dw_nestweight
prs.body:
## Summary

Lifts four commands from the World Bank
[EduAnalyticsToolkit](https://github.com/worldbank/EduAnalyticsToolkit)
into cso-toolkit. Three become **Stata helpers** (filling the v0.2
placeholder); one is **converted to R**.

### New files

**R (`r/R/`):**
- `dw_nestweight.R` — port of `edukit_nestweight` (Diana Goldemberg). Redistributes survey weights from missing nested observations. Pure analytics helper; sits alongside `aggregate_data*`. The `dw_` prefix is kept for namespace consistency though it's not contract-bound.

**Stata (`stata/src/`):**
- `dw_save.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `edukit_save` / `savemetadata` (Diana Goldemberg). isid + compress + save + sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar matching the R `dw_io` shape. Enforces producer / reviewer mode via `$dw_mode`; canonical writes blocked in reviewer mode unless `allow_canonical_write` is passed. Content hash via Stata-native `datasignature` (no shell-out / AppLocker issue).
- `dw_compare.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `comparefiles` / `edukit_comparefiles` (Kristoffer Bjärkefur). Merges two `.dta` files on idvars and classifies each value column as identical / numerically-equivalent (within `tol()`) / different; optional Markdown report.
- `dw_mkdir.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `rmkdir` / `edukit_rmkdir` (Kristoffer Bjärkefur). Recursive `mkdir` (Stata's built-in is non-recursive).

### Modified files

- `stata/src/README.md` — replaces the v0.1.0-rc1 "scaffolded, empty" placeholder with live helper docs: lineage table, install via adopath, mode-contract wiring, and three explicit known limitations.

### Deliberately deferred

- **Top-level `README.md` table updates** and **`NEWS.md` Unreleased entries** — these would conflict with PR #1's bigger rebrand rewrite, which already adds an Unreleased section. Cleaner to add them in a follow-up after #1 merges, into the section #1 created.
- **`edukit` (version meta)** — covered by `cso_toolkit_sync.R` + `.toolkit_manifest.yml`.
- **`edukit_datalibweb`** — WB-internal `datalibweb` retry wrapper; not applicable.
- **`edukit_dlwcheck`** — hardcoded education-assessment vocabularies (PISA / TIMSS / PIRLS / SACMEQ / EGRA); not applicable to UNICEF CSO indicator scope.

## Lineage credits

| cso-toolkit | EduAnalyticsToolkit ancestor | Original author |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_save.ado` | `edukit_save` / `savemetadata` | Diana Goldemberg |
| `dw_compare.ado` | `comparefiles` / `edukit_comparefiles` | Kristoffer Bjärkefur |
| `dw_mkdir.ado` | `rmkdir` / `edukit_rmkdir` | Kristoffer Bjärkefur |
| `dw_nestweight.R` | `nestweight` / `edukit_nestweight` | Diana Goldemberg |

Each helper credits the original author in its header.

## Deliberate divergences from the upstream lineage

1. **Naming**: renamed `dw_*` to align with the cso-toolkit family and avoid clashing if both toolkits are installed.
2. **Metadata model (Stata side)**: upstream `edukit_save` embeds metadata as `char _dta[...]` inside the `.dta`; `dw_save` writes a sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar matching the R-side shape, so producer / reviewer integrity checks work across both languages.
3. **Compare scope**: `dw_compare` is sized for the IO contract (publication-gate counts, per-column n_diff), not full editorial diffs. The upstream `comparefiles` remains the right tool for rich Markdown row-level diff reports — `dw_compare`'s `.sthlp` points users there.

## Test plan

- [x] R `dw_nestweight()` smoke test: 200-row tibble across two strata, 60 randomly NA'd — per-stratum totals byte-identical (diff = 0 in both strata).
- [ ] Stata `.ado` files: **no automated syntax check available** in this environment (Stata not on PATH). Manual review done; needs `do dw_save.ado` etc. against a real Stata install before tagging.
- [ ] End-to-end smoke: `dw_mkdir` → `dw_save` → `dw_compare` round-trip in producer mode, confirm `.provenance.json` sidecar shape matches what the R `dw_io` integrity check expects.
- [ ] Reviewer-mode hard-fail test: with `global dw_mode = "reviewer"` and a canonical path, confirm `dw_save` aborts with Stata error 459 unless `allow_canonical_write` is passed.

## Out-of-PR follow-ups (after this and #1 land)

- Add `dw_nestweight`, `dw_save`, `dw_compare`, `dw_mkdir` rows to PR #1's "What's in the box" table.
- Add four bullets to PR #1's NEWS Unreleased section.
- Open a tracking issue for the three remaining Stata-side gaps: `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do` (read `dw_mode` from `~/.config/user_config.yml`).

prs.title: docs: add Mermaid diagrams for role + data-flow and git workflow
prs.body:
## Summary

Adds two Mermaid-based workflow diagrams so the contract this repo encodes
is visible at a glance, not buried in prose.

### `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` (extended)
- **Data flow at a glance** — flowchart of producer (APIs → cache →
  pipeline → canonical → Z: mirror), reviewer (canonical → sandbox →
  compare report → issues), and ingestor (canonical → Helix / SDMX /
  data.unicef.org / SoWC). Coloured by role boundary.
- **Role-vs-action matrix** — five allowed actions vs four forbidden
  actions, side-by-side.

### `docs/git_workflow.md` (new)
- Branches-at-a-glance table summarising the protection state of `main`
  and `develop` (default, PR required, force push, deletion, admin bypass).
- Mermaid flowchart of the trunks: `feat/*` → `develop` via PR, `develop`
  → `main` via release PR, hotfix admin push → `develop`.
- "The contract these rules enforce" prose explaining each setting.
- Standard recipes for the three workflows (task work, release, hotfix)
  with copy-able `git` / `gh` commands.
- Recovery recipe for an accidental commit on `develop` (force-push
  blocked, so recovery is local-only).

## Format choice

Mermaid embedded in Markdown rather than standalone SVG so:
- GitHub renders both diagrams inline (no binary checked in).
- Future contract changes diff cleanly in PR review.
- No external tool needed to update — change the source, push, done.

## Out of scope (deliberate)

- Linking these diagrams from `README.md` would conflict with the
  pending #1 rebrand PR; cleaner to add the cross-link in a follow-up
  after #1 merges.

## Test plan

- [x] Both `.md` files render Mermaid blocks (`mermaid` fenced code)
  — will display on GitHub once the PR opens.
- [ ] Open the PR on GitHub.com and visually confirm both diagrams
  render before merging (Mermaid syntax errors are silent in local
  preview).

prs.title: feat: lift aggregate/reporting/scaffolding helpers from DW-Production, add profile + contract-audit helpers, rebrand as UNICEF CSO toolkit
prs.body:
## Summary

Lifts the cross-sector, reusable helpers from `DW-Production/00_functions/`
into the canonical upstream (`cso-toolkit`), rebrands the repo as the
**UNICEF Chief Statistician Office (CSO) toolkit**, adds two new helper
modules (`profile_helpers.R`, `test_scripts.R`), and spells out the
objective: facilitate reproducibility and scalability of analytics developed
by the UNICEF Data and Analytics Section in OSE.

### New R helpers (`r/R/`)
- `aggregate_data.R` — legacy `aggregate_data()` (lifted verbatim)
- `aggregate_data_v2.R` — `aggregate_data_v2()` with `weighted_mean` /
  `mean` / `sum` / `proportion`, coverage threshold, metadata columns;
  ships `generate_agg_footnote()`, `apply_time_window()`, and a
  v1-compatible wrapper
- `generate_markdown_report.R` — descriptive-stats Markdown from CSV
  (annotated with `# cso-allow: io-read-csv` because it accepts arbitrary
  paths)
- `create_sector_script.R` — generalized over the DW-Production original;
  adds `base_dir`, `profile_name`, `profile_file`, `input_subpath`,
  `output_subpath`, `overwrite` parameters. Ships a
  `create_dw_sector_script()` convenience wrapper that pins the
  DW-Production conventions.
- `profile_helpers.R` — `create_profile(repo_name, ...)` scaffolds a
  `profile_<repo>.R` template with the standard CSO building blocks
  (cross-platform user identification, YAML config load, optional
  producer/reviewer `dw_mode`, optional Z: drive advisory, packages,
  sentinel). `review_profile(path, ...)` audits an existing profile for
  the same blocks with pass/warn/fail reporting.
- `test_scripts.R` — `test_scripts(path, ...)` recursively scans `.R`
  scripts and flags any direct call to a raw file-IO or external-API
  command that `dw_io.R` / `dw_api.R` is meant to wrap (16 built-in
  rules across two families). Per-line escape hatch via
  `# cso-allow: <rule-id>`; CI mode via `error_on_violation = TRUE`.

### New docs (`docs/`)
- `dw_io_reference.md` — per-function reference for `dw_io.R` (lifted
  from `00_functions/README.md`)
- `dw_api_reference.md` — per-function reference for `dw_api.R` (lifted
  from `00_functions/README.md`)

### Updated docs
- `README.md` — rebrand + **Objective and motivation** section + updated
  table + updated citation
- `r/R/README.md` — split into "core (v0.1.0-rc1)" vs "added
  post-v0.1.0-rc1" sections
- `NEWS.md` — Unreleased section itemising every new file

## Compatibility with existing vendoring

Filenames and function names match `DW-Production/00_functions/` 1:1 so
the next `cso_toolkit_pull()` is a clean mechanical copy. The
DW-Production `.toolkit_manifest.yml` will need to add the four new
filenames to the `vendored:` list when the consumer chooses to refresh.

## Test plan

- [x] All new R files parse cleanly (`parse(file = ...)`)
- [x] `create_profile()` + `review_profile()` round-trip end-to-end on a
  tempdir target — 7 pass / 1 expected warn (Z: omitted)
- [x] `test_scripts()` self-audit on `r/R/` returns 0 violations (the
  toolkit's own wrapped commands are correctly ignored via the default
  `ignore_files` list; the `generate_markdown_report.R` raw `read_csv`
  is exempted via `# cso-allow: io-read-csv`)
- [ ] Vendoring smoke test: `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = ...)` in
  DW-Production pulls the four new files without conflict (deferred to a
  follow-up consumer-side PR)

issues.title: feat(docs): DW Operations Hub dashboard (cso-toolkit hosted, GitHub Pages)
issues.body:
## Summary

Adds a single-page sector progress dashboard for the DW-Production / cso-toolkit ecosystem, served via GitHub Pages from `docs/dashboard/` and refreshed nightly by `.github/workflows/dashboard.yml` (06:00 UTC cron).

## Why this lives on cso-toolkit

- **Free hosting**: public repo gets unlimited GitHub Actions minutes and free GitHub Pages.
- **Cross-cutting**: the dashboard reports on toolkit + DW-Production + Teams snapshots together. cso-toolkit is the neutral cross-cutting home — DW-Production is sector-pipeline-only.
- **Operator-visible secrets stay off DW-Production**: only a read-scoped PAT (`DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN`) crosses the boundary, in one direction.

## Required org-admin action: cross-repo PAT secret

A new repository secret on `cso-toolkit` must be set before the nightly cron will succeed:

| Secret name | Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN` | `repo:read` on `unicef-drp/DW-Production` only | Lets `collect.R` pull PRs / branches / issues / milestones from the private DW-Production repo |

Org admin (@karavan88) must create a PAT with read-only scope on DW-Production and set it as a repository secret on cso-toolkit. Until then, the `dw_production` block in `state.json` is recorded as `reachable: false` (graceful degrade — dashboard still renders).

## Eight tabs

1. **Landing** — KPIs, pipeline-phase distribution, activity feed, watch list of stalled sectors.
2. **Sectors** — per-sector cards (status, rows, indicators, wall-time, fixes, toolkit findings, blockers) + five summary charts.
3. **Pipeline phases** — kanban: Production / Review / Publishing / Live.
4. **Branches** — cso-toolkit branches table (name, link, SHA, protected flag).
5. **Issues** — cso-toolkit issues grouped by milestone (v0.4.6 / v0.5.0 / unlabelled), severity badge.
6. **DBM actions** — TODO / IN-PROGRESS / DONE kanban sourced from `docs/dashboard/data/actions/<id>.yml`.
7. **History** — line charts of state over time (placeholder day-1; grows nightly).
8. **cso-toolkit** — toolkit version per branch, open v0.4.6 issues, cycle burndown.

## Nightly cron

`.github/workflows/dashboard.yml` runs at 06:00 UTC daily plus on push-to-`develop` / `main` and `workflow_dispatch`:

1. `Rscript docs/dashboard/collect.R` — assembles `state.json` from SDMX (rsdmx + cache fallback), cso-toolkit `gh api`, DW-Production `gh api`, and operator snapshots.
2. `Rscript docs/dashboard/render.R` — inlines five SVG charts + data-flow diagram, writes self-contained `index.html`.
3. Commits refreshed artifacts back to the branch with `[skip ci]`.
4. Publishes `docs/` to GitHub Pages.

## Coexistence with pkgdown

Per Phase 6 discovery (2026-05-30): cso-toolkit currently has no pkgdown workflow (only `r-check.yml`), so `dashboard.yml` owns Pages deployment and publishes the full `docs/` tree.

If pkgdown is added later, the conflict resolution is documented inline at the top of `dashboard.yml`:

- pkgdown takes over the Pages deploy (it rebuilds the whole site).
- `dashboard.yml` drops its `deploy` job and only commits refreshed files back to the branch.
- The pkgdown workflow must use `clean: false` so the `docs/dashboard/` subtree survives pkgdown rebuilds.

## Open v0.4.6 issues surfaced by the dashboard

The Issues tab and the DBM-actions kanban both surface the current open v0.4.6 blockers, with one DBM action YAML per issue under `docs/dashboard/data/actions/`:

- [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) — standalone-source `%>%` gate should use `.cso_require()` envelope
- [#52](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/52) — manifest sha256 normalisation (Windows CRLF / LF cross-platform drift)
- [#53](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/53) — re-export `dw_root()` public wrapper around `.dw_root_for()`
- [#54](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/54) — `dw_save` reviewer-mode mode-lock missed OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path

## Companion PR on DW-Production

The operator-side Teams snapshot script feeds this dashboard. Lives on DW-Production because it reads a Teams path only the operator can see:

- [unicef-drp/DW-Production — feat(snapshots): operator-side Teams snapshot script](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/new/docs/operator-snapshot) (linked after merge)

## Test plan

- [ ] Org admin sets `DW_PROD_READ_TOKEN` repository secret
- [ ] `gh workflow run dashboard.yml` succeeds (or wait for next 06:00 UTC tick)
- [ ] GitHub Pages serves the dashboard at the published URL
- [ ] All 8 tabs render; the DW-Production block in `state.json` is non-empty
- [ ] Operator runs `Rscript snapshots/snapshot-teams.R` (DW-Production companion PR) and commits the resulting snapshots to cso-toolkit; dashboard re-renders with fresh data on the next cron

issues.title: release: cut v0.4.6 (#51 + #52 + #53 + #54)

milestone.title: v0.4.6
milestone.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

issues.body:
## Summary

Cuts cso-toolkit **v0.4.6** — a corrective release driven by the 2026-05-30 HVA + ED audit incident, plus three smaller correctness fixes batched into the same cycle.

The headline is **#54 (HIGH severity)**: `dw_is_canonical()` failed to recognise the OneDrive-mounted Teams `Documents` path on Windows, so reviewer-mode writes silently fell through and *overwrote canonical Teams artefacts* during back-to-back audit runs. Mode-lock is now tight.

Closes:
- [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) — `.cso_require` envelope on `%>%` standalone-source gate
- [#52](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/52) — `.gitattributes` force LF on R/Rd/YAML/MD/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION
- [#53](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/53) — re-export `dw_root()` public wrapper around `.dw_root_for()`
- [#54](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/54) — `dw_is_canonical` recognises OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path **(HIGH — mode-lock regression)**

## Why #54 is the headline

Empirical evidence from the **2026-05-30 HVA + ED audit runs**: two consecutive reviewer-mode audits *did not trigger* the mode-lock hard-stop, and instead wrote to the producer-canonical Teams path on disk. Root cause: on this Windows + OneDrive layout the canonical Teams root resolves through `…/OneDrive - UNICEF/Documents/…` rather than the bare UNC mount, and the prior `dw_is_canonical()` predicate only matched the latter. The producer/reviewer contract memo ([feedback_reviewer_mode_read_write_contract](https://github.com/jpazvd/...)) was correct; the predicate was the leak.

After this PR the OneDrive-mounted path is recognised as canonical, so reviewer-mode writes correctly hit the hard-stop with the explanatory warning.

## Migration notes

- **Consumers**: `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.6")` brings in the new `dw_root()` public wrapper *and* the LF-normalised file set.
- **One-time hash drift**: any consumer repo with cached CRLF manifests will see a one-time hash drift after upgrade (R/Rd/YAML/MD/NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION re-normalised to LF via `.gitattributes`). This is expected; re-pin the manifest after first pull on v0.4.6.
- No public-API removals. `dw_root()` is additive.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check passes on all 4 OS/R combos (ubuntu/macos × R-release/R-devel)
- [ ] New `test-dw-is-canonical.R` passes (covers OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path recognition)
- [ ] Existing `test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` + `test-dw_publish.R` adjustments are green
- [ ] No new lintr / styler diffs in the changed `R/*.R` files
- [ ] Reviewer sector lead (DW-Production MNCH) signs off before merge

## Do not merge

This PR is **gated on sector lead review** (per `feedback_explicit_ack_before_dev_merge`). Do not squash-merge on the strength of green CI alone.

---

Generated as part of the v0.4.6 release cycle. Parent tracker: v0.4.6 milestone.

issues.title: docs: rename third role INGESTOR -> PUBLISHER (DBM/DBR/DBP)
issues.body:
## Summary

Renames the third data-warehouse role **INGESTOR → PUBLISHER** across the docs, and spells out the role acronyms **DBM / DBR / DBP**. **Docs-only** — no code, no exported-API change, and no mode-contract value changed (the contract still exposes `producer` / `reviewer`; a `publisher` mode remains future scope, #15).

### Why

The code already commits to the verb: there is a `dw_publish()` helper (and `[cso_toolkit.dw_publish]` error envelopes), but **no `dw_ingest()`**. The role's own gloss was already "warehouse publisher", so "INGESTOR" contradicted both the code and its own definition. `PUBLISHER` aligns the label with the verb, and **DBM / DBR / DBP** is a clean acronym set (the README role table already labelled PRODUCER as "DBM").

### Mapping

- **DBM** = Database Manager → PRODUCER
- **DBR** = Database Reviewer → REVIEWER
- **DBP** = Database Publisher → PUBLISHER (was INGESTOR)

### Files (7)

- `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` — rename across prose, both mermaid diagrams, the folder-layout map, and the role / helper / boundary tables; adds a DBM/DBR/DBP acronym legend + acronyms in the role matrix
- `README.md` — mermaid node label + edge verb (`ingest` → `publish`); role-table rows gain `(DBR)` / `(DBP)`; See-also reference
- `r/DESCRIPTION`, `docs/git_workflow.md`, `docs/toolkit_strategy.md`, `templates/dbm_submission_template.md` — role-triplet references
- `NEWS.md` — `## Unreleased` entry (historical entries left intact)

### Out of scope (kept surgical)

- No `DESCRIPTION` version bump — change logged under `## Unreleased`.
- Wiring a `publisher` `dw_mode` value into the contract — future scope (#15).
- Internal mermaid node IDs (`IN_PUB` / `IN_RAW`) keep their names (invisible identifiers).

## Test plan

- [ ] `grep -ri "ingestor"` over `docs/`, `README.md`, `r/DESCRIPTION`, `templates/` returns only the two historical `NEWS.md` entries
- [ ] mermaid diagrams in `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` + `README.md` still render (node `I` now reads PUBLISHER; edge verb `publish`)
- [ ] no `.R` / `.py` / `.ado` files touched (docs-only)

issues.title: v0.4.6 — dw_save reviewer-mode mode-lock missed OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path

milestone.title: v0.4.6
milestone.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

issues.body:
## Background

The v0.4.5 `dw_save()` reviewer-mode mode-lock is supposed to refuse writes to canonical Teams paths in reviewer mode. During the 2026-05-30 sector-by-sector replication pass (NT/HVA/IM/WS/MNCH/CME/ED/cp-cluster), it correctly engaged for IM/WS/CME (repo-local writes) but **silently allowed** both HVA and ED to overwrite canonical Teams `dw_*.csv` files even with `dw_mode = "reviewer"`.

## Reproduction

  - `dw_mode = "reviewer"` confirmed via `dw_toolkit_version()` echo and reviewer-mode read-fallback warnings on `dw_use`.
  - Sector scripts call `dw_save(df, file.path(canonical_outputdir, "dw_<sector>.csv"), sector = "<sector>")` where `canonical_outputdir` resolves to:
    - HVA: `<Teams>/060.DW-MASTER/01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/hva/2025/dw_hiv.csv` ← written ✗
    - ED step 2: `<Teams>/060.DW-MASTER/01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/ed/dw_ed_edu.csv` ← written ✗
  - The IM script (which writes to `dwWrkData/_local/im/output/DW_IM_HELIX.csv` — a repo-relative path) is NOT affected; mode-lock engages correctly there.

## Root-cause hypothesis

`dw_is_canonical()` likely checks for the legacy `Z:/...` UNC prefix and the `teamsFolder/...` substring, but the Teams folder on this UNICEF laptop is mounted via OneDrive at `C:/Users/<user>/UNICEF/Chief Statistician Office - Documents/060.DW-MASTER/...` — neither pattern would match. The mode-lock then falls through to "not canonical, allow write".

## Impact

Reviewer-mode runs can overwrite producer's canonical artefacts when sector pipelines route their writes through `dw_root("wrk")` or set `canonical_outputdir` based on Teams-canonical resolution. During the 2026-05-30 audit the prior v6 HVA producer-run output (67 MB, 214,674 rows, 2026-05-26) was overwritten by the reviewer-mode HVA run.

## Proposed fix (v0.4.6)

Extend `dw_is_canonical()` to recognise:

1. The OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents pattern:
   `^C:/Users/[^/]+/UNICEF/Chief Statistician Office - Documents/060\.DW-MASTER/`
2. The Z:-drive mirror (existing).
3. Any path resolved via `teamsFolder` global at call time (regardless of how `teamsFolder` itself is constructed).

The third pattern is the most robust — match against the resolved string of `teamsFolder` from `.GlobalEnv` rather than a hard-coded regex.

## Cross-references

  - 2026-05-30 sector-replication audit: workflow run id `w1i00pspa` + manual per-sector runs
  - HVA test report (PR #160 merged into review/sector-hva-2026-05-18): documented the gap as a v0.4.6 follow-up
  - Companion v0.4.6 issues: #51 (.cso_require envelope), #52 (manifest CRLF/LF), #53 (dw_root wrapper)

## Severity

**HIGH** — reviewer-mode contract is the core safety guarantee of the v0.4.x toolkit. A reviewer-mode session must NOT be able to overwrite canonical producer artefacts. Today's audit shows this contract can be violated without warning when sector scripts route writes through `dw_root("wrk")` or hardcoded canonical paths.

issues.title: v0.4.6 — re-export dw_root() public wrapper around .dw_root_for() (sector scripts depend on it)

milestone.title: v0.4.6
milestone.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

issues.body:
## Background

The HVA R port ([unicef-drp/DW-Production#117](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/117), 3 files: `00_run_hiv.R`, `010_hiv_dw_1.R`, `011_hiv_dw_2.R`) calls `dw_root("wrk")` to resolve the canonical output directory for the sector:

```r
# 00_run_hiv.R:35-39
# Output folder — mode-aware via dw_root() helper in dw_io.R.
#   Producer mode -> canonical Teams (same path Savvy's Stata writes to)
#   Reviewer mode -> repo-local mirror of the canonical suffix path
canonical_outputdir <- file.path(dw_root("wrk"), "hva", "2025")
```

The same pattern appears in `010_hiv_dw_1.R:27` and `011_hiv_dw_2.R:22`. Other sector R adoptions on DW-Production (NT, IM, MNCH, GN, PV) presumably follow the same idiom once they reach the dw_io adoption stage.

## Problem

`dw_root()` does NOT exist in cso-toolkit v0.4.5. The canonical lift kept only the internal helper `.dw_root_for(kind = c("wrk", "raw", "meta"))` (defined at `r/R/dw_io.R:105`), without a public wrapper.

Reproduction (v0.4.5 toolkit, HVA R port from PR #117, reviewer-mode test on 2026-05-29):

```
Error in dw_root("wrk") : could not find function "dw_root"
Calls: cat
Execution halted
```

The mismatch came from the v4 dw_io rollout (see plan `fluffy-greeting-pretzel.md`). DW-Production's local copy of `dw_io.R` previously exported a `dw_root()` public wrapper as one of the "local edits applied on top of vendored files". When `00_functions/dw_io.R` was re-lifted from canonical, the public wrapper disappeared, but the sector scripts still call `dw_root()` — they depend on a contract the canonical never carried.

## Proposed fix (small, additive, non-breaking)

Expose `dw_root()` as a thin public wrapper around `.dw_root_for()` in `r/R/dw_io.R`. Roxygen-document it as the public entry point; keep `.dw_root_for()` as the underlying internal that future mode-aware refactors mutate.

```r
#' Resolve the mode-aware repo / canonical root for a vendored kind
#'
#' Public wrapper around the internal `.dw_root_for()`. Sector scripts use
#' this to build paths like `file.path(dw_root("wrk"), "<sector>", "<vintage>")`
#' without needing to know whether the session is producer or reviewer mode.
#'
#' @param kind One of "wrk", "raw", "meta" — selects which of the three
#'   vendored data roots to return.
#' @return Character. Absolute path to the resolved root.
#' @seealso [.dw_root_for()] (internal, mutated by future v4 mode-aware
#'   plan); [dw_resolve_path()] for the full sector/vintage/name path.
#' @family io
#' @export
dw_root <- function(kind = c("wrk", "raw", "meta")) .dw_root_for(kind)
```

NAMESPACE gains `export(dw_root)`. `.dw_root_for()` stays internal — callers should migrate to `dw_root()`.

## Migration

- DW-Production scripts already call `dw_root()` (HVA R port — only sector currently affected at the source level; other sectors that haven't adopted dw_io yet don't use this idiom).
- The shim is a one-line wrapper; no behavioural change to existing callers of the internal `.dw_root_for()`.

## Cross-references

- HVA R port: [DW-Production#117](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/117), files `01_dw_prep/012_codes/hva/{00_run_hiv,010_hiv_dw_1,011_hiv_dw_2}.R`.
- Reproduction: HVA reviewer-mode test against v0.4.5 vendored copy on 2026-05-29 (this audit).
- Companion v0.4.6 issues: [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) (.cso_require envelope on %>% gate), [#52](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/52) (manifest CRLF/LF normalisation).
- v4 dw_io plan: `~/.claude/plans/fluffy-greeting-pretzel.md` — references DW-Production's local `dw_root()` wrapper as an edit that needs upstream-porting.

## Suggested release vehicle

v0.4.6 (alongside #51 + #52). Estimated implementation: 5 minutes for the wrapper + NAMESPACE export + roxygen note.

issues.title: v0.4.6 — manifest sha256 normalisation (Windows CRLF / LF cross-platform drift)

milestone.title: v0.4.6
milestone.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

issues.body:
## Background

`cso_toolkit_pull()` computes per-file sha256 hashes for `00_functions/.toolkit_manifest.yml::files.<name>` and `cso_toolkit_check()` re-computes those hashes against the vendored copies to detect drift. The current implementation hashes the **on-disk file content** (working tree), which on Windows-checked-out repos with `core.autocrlf = true` includes CRLF line endings. The canonical Git blob (stored upstream) is LF-only.

Result: every manifest produced by a Windows operator records a CRLF hash. Every manifest produced by a Linux/Mac operator records an LF hash. The two never agree on the same release.

## Concrete evidence (v0.4.5 install audit, 2026-05-29)

DW-Production sector-NT install branch (Windows operator):

```
manifest files.dw_io.R sha256:        01aec12156fc3eae72b2e8d02fb7b653a7d0ad8de26039ac10a7bfae515de11c
git blob sha256 (LF, canonical):      67964059f204ca288f92d7183039fa2510a6eab69fd3b34e9de25ca7d125068d
xxd r/R/dw_io.R | head -1            -> bytes show `0d 0a` (CRLF) at line endings
git show v0.4.5:r/R/dw_io.R | xxd    -> bytes show `0a` (LF)     at line endings
```

The actual file content is byte-identical between DW-Production install branch and cso-toolkit v0.4.5 (both have CRLF after checkout on Windows; both have LF in their respective Git blobs). The drift is purely the hash recording convention.

Same pattern present in v0.4.4 install manifests — this is **not a v0.4.5 regression**, it's been wrong since the manifest scheme launched.

## Impact

  | Surface | Impact |
  |---|---|
  | DW-Production runtime (Windows) | None. `cso_toolkit_check()` reads checked-out files (CRLF) → matches the CRLF manifest entry. False-positive cannot fire. |
  | Cross-platform sanity audits | Workflow-driven verifications that hash the Git blob (`git show <tag>:r/R/<file> \| sha256sum`) report drift on every file even though content is aligned. |
  | Linux/Mac CI checks | Would false-alarm: install-time hash (LF) ≠ runtime-check hash on a Windows machine pulling the same manifest (CRLF). |
  | Audit reproducibility | A manifest cannot be re-validated independently of the OS the install ran on. |

Today none of these are blocking — the project is Windows-only on the consumer side. But the manifest is supposed to be the platform-portable contract between upstream and vendored copies, and right now it isn't.

## Proposed fix (two parts; minimum non-breaking)

### Part 1 — force LF on R/Rd/YAML/MD source via `.gitattributes`

Add to repo root:

```
# Source files: stored AND checked out as LF on every platform.
# This keeps sha256 hashes in cso-toolkit and consumer-side manifests
# (e.g. unicef-drp/DW-Production/00_functions/.toolkit_manifest.yml)
# stable across Windows / Linux / Mac.
*.R      text eol=lf
*.Rd     text eol=lf
*.yml    text eol=lf
*.yaml   text eol=lf
*.md     text eol=lf
NAMESPACE text eol=lf
DESCRIPTION text eol=lf
```

Effect: Windows-clone working trees still see LF in the editor (some editors auto-translate for display, but the on-disk bytes are LF). `sha256sum` produces the canonical hash regardless of OS.

### Part 2 — `cso_toolkit_sync.R` should hash the Git blob, not the working tree

Inside `cso_toolkit_pull()` and `cso_toolkit_check()`, compute the canonical sha256 via:

```r
# Reads the file in binary mode, no OS line-ending translation.
hash <- digest::digest(file = path, algo = "sha256", serialize = FALSE,
                       file = TRUE)  # already binary-safe in digest >= 0.6.x
# Or equivalently for the upstream-tag side:
hash <- digest::digest(file = file_at_tag_path, algo = "sha256", ...)
```

`digest::digest(file = ...)` reads in binary mode, so it should already give the on-disk byte hash. The fix is to make sure the **on-disk bytes are LF everywhere** (Part 1 handles that). Part 2 is a no-op if Part 1 lands first.

### Part 3 (optional, audit-only) — add a `cso_toolkit_audit_blob()` that hashes via `git show <tag>:r/R/<file>` for cross-platform manifest validation

For CI use. Compares the Git blob (always LF, platform-independent) against the manifest entry; reports drift independent of `core.autocrlf` state.

## Migration

  - All existing manifests on consumer repos (DW-Production v0.4.4 + v0.4.5 install PRs) will become "wrong" after Part 1 lands and the upstream blobs switch to LF on every checkout. They were already wrong as soon as a non-Windows operator pulled, so this just makes the issue visible everywhere.
  - One-time mass refresh of consumer manifests via `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.6")` rewrites every manifest entry to the canonical LF hash. Single line change to consumer side: re-run the pull.
  - Documentation in `r/vignettes/vendoring.Rmd` (or wherever the vendoring story is told) should call out the `.gitattributes` requirement and the LF convention.

## Suggested release vehicle

v0.4.6 (alongside [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) — both are manifest / standalone-source UX cleanups, both small, both non-breaking). Estimated implementation: 30 minutes for `.gitattributes` + repo-wide LF re-normalization commit; 1 hour for `cso_toolkit_sync.R` audit harness if Part 3 is wanted.

## Cross-references

  - Surfaced by the DW-Production v0.4.5 install alignment audit (jpazvd workflow run on 2026-05-29; audit task id `w52a2c4bx`).
  - DW-Production install PRs that carry the CRLF manifest hashes: [#146 NT](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/146), [#147 IM](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/147), [#148 WS](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/148), [#149 HVA](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/149) — all 4 will gain the correct LF hashes when v0.4.6 ships and they re-pull.
  - Companion v0.4.6 work: [#51](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/51) (`.cso_require` envelope on `%>%` gate).

issues.title: v0.4.6 — standalone-source %>% gate should use .cso_require() envelope

milestone.title: v0.4.6
milestone.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

issues.body:
## Background

v0.4.5 PR [#47](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/47) added a standalone-source `%>%` gate to `aggregate_data.R` and `aggregate_data_v2.R`:

```r
if (!exists("%>%", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)) {
  `%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`
}
```

The gate works as intended when magrittr is installed (which it is in every DW-Production environment via dplyr's hard import). But when magrittr is NOT installed, `magrittr::%>%` is evaluated immediately and `source()` fails with R's generic message:

```
Error in loadNamespace(x) : there is no package called 'magrittr'
```

That message doesn't tell the consumer how to fix the problem, and it bypasses the `[cso_toolkit.<helper>] ... Fix: install.packages('<pkg>')` envelope that `.cso_require()` provides for every other helper dependency.

## Where this surfaced

Copilot's review of the DW-Production v0.4.5 install PR ([unicef-drp/DW-Production#146](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/146), NT install) flagged both gate sites as inconsistent with the rest of the toolkit's dependency-error UX. The behaviour is correct in practice (DW-Production sectors all have dplyr installed → magrittr is always available), but the error UX is hypothetical-only weak.

## Proposed fix (small, additive, non-breaking)

Use `.cso_require()` to check for magrittr before binding the pipe. Two flavours:

### aggregate_data_v2.R (already has a local `.cso_require()` fallback)

```r
if (!exists("%>%", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)) {
  .cso_require("magrittr", where = "aggregate_data_v2")
  `%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`
}
```

The local fallback for `.cso_require()` is defined above the gate; the gate can call it directly.

### aggregate_data.R (no local `.cso_require()` fallback today)

Either:

1. Add a local `.cso_require()` fallback to `aggregate_data.R` matching the one in `aggregate_data_v2.R`, then use it in the gate. (Most consistent with the rest of the toolkit.)
2. Hand-roll the envelope inside the gate (less DRY but smaller diff).

Recommend option 1 — the fallback is 12 lines and is the canonical pattern.

## Out of scope

- The current gate behaviour is correct when magrittr is installed; no functional bug.
- Existing DW-Production sectors are unaffected (magrittr is always present).
- No tests fail today — this would be a UX hardening, not a regression fix.

## Suggested release vehicle

v0.4.6 (patch release). Sub-1-hour implementation; no API changes; no migration cost for existing consumers.

## Cross-references

- v0.4.5 PR [#47](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/47) (introduces the gates)
- v0.4.5 issue [#46](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/46) (original motivation)
- DW-Production NT install PR [#146](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/146) (Copilot inline comment that surfaced this)

milestone.description: Naming cleanup (#42) + standalone-source pipe binding fix — RELEASED 2026-05-29 as v0.4.5

issues.body:
## Summary

Promotes v0.4.5 from `develop` to `main`. All v0.4.5 milestone work merged:

  - PR #47 → #46 (standalone-source `%>%` binding + magrittr declared in Imports)
  - PR #48 → #42 (8 `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the remaining un-prefixed exports)
  - PR #49 → release stamp (Version 0.4.5; dw_io.R header + .Rd regen; NEWS reorganisation)

No new code lands in this PR — it is the standard develop→main release-promotion PR.

## What ships in v0.4.5

### Public-surface additions (non-breaking)

8 new `dw_*` aliases via roxygen `@rdname`, sharing the same man page as their un-prefixed counterparts:

  - `dw_aggregate_data`
  - `dw_generate_agg_footnote`
  - `dw_apply_time_window`
  - `dw_generate_markdown_report`
  - `dw_process_all_csv_files`
  - `dw_create_profile`
  - `dw_review_profile`
  - `dw_test_scripts`

### Behavioural changes

  - `aggregate_data.R` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` now safe to `source()` standalone — neither `library(dplyr)` nor `library(magrittr)` required first.
  - `magrittr` declared as a first-class Import; NAMESPACE carries `importFrom(magrittr,"%>%")`. R CMD check no longer relies on transitive resolution through dplyr.

### Version-stamp consistency

All five live version-stamped sites read `0.4.5`:

  - `r/DESCRIPTION::Version`
  - `r/R/dw_io.R::dw_toolkit_version()` (literal + roxygen `@return`)
  - `r/R/dw_io.R` header comment
  - `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd` (regenerated)
  - 2 testthat assertions on `dw_toolkit_version()` and `body$toolkit` in `dw_publish` echo

## Post-merge actions

  1. Tag `v0.4.5` at the merge commit on `main`.
  2. Create GitHub Release v0.4.5 (paste the v0.4.5 NEWS section as body).
  3. Open 4 DW-Production install PRs (NT, IM, WS, HVA) against `review/sector-*-2026-05-18` branches.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on this PR (R CMD check x 4 OS/R combos).
- [ ] `git diff main...develop -- r/DESCRIPTION` shows only Version 0.4.4 → 0.4.5.
- [ ] `git diff main...develop -- r/NAMESPACE` shows + 9 lines (1 importFrom + 8 export(dw_*)).
- [ ] After merge: tag `v0.4.5` is reachable from `main` HEAD.
- [ ] GitHub Release v0.4.5 published with the v0.4.5 NEWS section.

issues.body:
## Summary

Cuts cso-toolkit **v0.4.5** by merging the two v0.4.5 milestone PRs (already on `develop`) into `main` and stamping the release.

Three substantive deliverables in this cycle:

1. **#46** — Standalone-source `%>%` binding (PR #47): vendored consumers can `source("aggregate_data.R")` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` without first attaching magrittr/dplyr. Pre-flight gate binds `%>%` locally when not already in scope; no-op in installed-package context.
2. **#42** — `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases (PR #48): 8 additional `dw_*` aliases close out the namespace-prefix program that began in v0.4.4 (which aliased only the two exports touched by #36).
3. **Magrittr dependency declaration** (companion to PR #47): adds `magrittr` to `DESCRIPTION::Imports` and emits `importFrom(magrittr, "%>%")` to NAMESPACE — previously came in transitively via dplyr. R CMD check is now honest about the dependency.

Issue #40 (`dw_regions` API redesign against Country-and-Region-Metadata-API) is deferred to v0.5.0 since the function is being rewritten — the value-arg bug fix folds into the redesign rather than landing in v0.4.5.

## Version-stamp bumps in this release PR

  | File:line | From | To |
  |---|---|---|
  | `r/DESCRIPTION::Version` | `0.4.4` | `0.4.5` |
  | `r/DESCRIPTION::Date`    | `2026-05-29` | `<release date>` |
  | `r/R/dw_io.R:254` (docstring) | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/R/dw_io.R:266` (literal)   | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R:21` | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |
  | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R:120`          | `"0.4.4"` | `"0.4.5"` |

NEWS: rename `## Unreleased` → `## v0.4.5 (<release date>)` and add a fresh `## Unreleased` block.

## What ships in v0.4.5

### Public-surface additions (all non-breaking)

- `dw_aggregate_data` — alias of `aggregate_data`
- `dw_generate_agg_footnote` — alias of `generate_agg_footnote`
- `dw_apply_time_window` — alias of `apply_time_window`
- `dw_generate_markdown_report` — alias of `generate_markdown_report`
- `dw_process_all_csv_files` — alias of `process_all_csv_files`
- `dw_create_profile` — alias of `create_profile`
- `dw_review_profile` — alias of `review_profile`
- `dw_test_scripts` — alias of `test_scripts`

### Behavioural changes

- Vendored helpers (`aggregate_data.R`, `aggregate_data_v2.R`) are now safe to `source()` standalone without `library(magrittr)` or `library(dplyr)` first.
- `magrittr` is a first-class declared Import; R CMD check no longer relies on transitive resolution through dplyr.

### Documentation

- NEWS entry under `v0.4.5 (<release date>)` covering both deliverables + the magrittr declaration.
- Roxygen `@rdname` ties on the 8 aliases mean both names share the same man page.

## DW-Production downstream propagation plan

Once this PR merges and `v0.4.5` is tagged, the manifest pulls go out to each of the four sectors that consumed v0.4.4 (NT, IM, WS, HVA) on their respective `review/sector-*-2026-05-18` branches. The diff vs v0.4.4 affects 4 files (aggregate_data.R, aggregate_data_v2.R, zzz.R, DESCRIPTION — though DESCRIPTION may not be vendored on the DW-Production side). The remaining 7 vendored files are byte-identical to v0.4.4.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on `develop` head (R CMD check: ubuntu R-release, ubuntu R-devel, macos R-release, windows R-release).
- [ ] No substantive Copilot findings on the release PR.
- [ ] `r/DESCRIPTION::Version` reads `0.4.5`.
- [ ] `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.5"` (both in dw_io.R source and the two tests).
- [ ] `r/NAMESPACE` carries `importFrom(magrittr,"%>%")` + 8 new `export(dw_*)` lines vs. v0.4.4.
- [ ] NEWS.md `## Unreleased` block is empty after rename, ready for v0.4.6+ entries.
- [ ] Tag `v0.4.5` reachable from `main`.
- [ ] GitHub Release v0.4.5 published with copy of the v0.4.5 NEWS section.
- [ ] After release: 4 DW-Production install PRs queued (NT, IM, WS, HVA).

issues.title: feat(r): dw_ canonical aliases for 8 remaining un-prefixed exports (closes #42)

milestone.description: Naming cleanup (#42) + standalone-source pipe binding fix — RELEASED 2026-05-29 as v0.4.5

issues.body:
## Summary

Closes #42 — Adds `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the remaining 8 un-prefixed exports, completing the namespace-prefix program that began in v0.4.4 (PR #43, which aliased the two exports touched by #36).

## What this PR does

Eight new `dw_` aliases land across 5 R files. Each alias is exported via roxygen `@rdname` so both names share the same man page (same mechanism as v0.4.4):

| Source file | Un-prefixed export | New `dw_`-prefixed alias |
|---|---|---|
| `r/R/aggregate_data.R`           | `aggregate_data`           | `dw_aggregate_data` |
| `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R`        | `generate_agg_footnote`    | `dw_generate_agg_footnote` |
| `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R`        | `apply_time_window`        | `dw_apply_time_window` |
| `r/R/generate_markdown_report.R` | `generate_markdown_report` | `dw_generate_markdown_report` |
| `r/R/generate_markdown_report.R` | `process_all_csv_files`    | `dw_process_all_csv_files` |
| `r/R/profile_helpers.R`          | `create_profile`           | `dw_create_profile` |
| `r/R/profile_helpers.R`          | `review_profile`           | `dw_review_profile` |
| `r/R/test_scripts.R`             | `test_scripts`             | `dw_test_scripts` |

## Non-breaking

- Un-prefixed names continue to work indefinitely in v0.4.x (no behaviour change for existing consumers).
- A future v0.5.x cycle may emit a `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` warning on the un-prefixed forms, but only after sector leads confirm migration is complete.

## Explicit non-renames

- `create_dw_sector_script` already carries a `dw` infix (it's a DW-Production-specific wrapper of `create_sector_script`, which received `dw_create_sector_script` in v0.4.4). Not aliased; the name is already prefix-bearing.
- `test_scripts` carries a roxygen note flagging a possible future v0.5.x rename to `dw_audit_scripts` — both to surface the audit intent and to avoid the cosmetic collision with `testthat::test_*` naming. The prefix-only alias here keeps this PR additive.

## Tests

`r/tests/testthat/test-dw_aliases-v045.R` — 8 identity tests verifying each `dw_<name>` is `identical()` to its un-prefixed source. Mirrors the v0.4.4 `test-dw_aliases.R` pattern.

## Roxygen / NAMESPACE

`r/man/*.Rd` (8 files): regenerated to add `\alias{dw_<name>}` + `\usage{dw_<name>(...)}` entries via `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")`.

`r/NAMESPACE`: gains 8 export entries (one per alias).

## Cross-references

- Issue: #42
- Parent program: #36 (first 2 aliases landed in v0.4.4 via PR #43)
- v0.4.5 milestone: https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/4
- Companion v0.4.5 work: #46 / PR #47 (standalone-source pipe binding)

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green (R CMD check, lints, tests).
- [ ] Copilot reviewer leaves no substantive findings.
- [ ] `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")` is idempotent locally (no further `r/man/*.Rd` deltas).
- [ ] `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_aliases-v045.R` passes all 8 identity tests.
- [ ] `r/NAMESPACE` has exactly 8 new `export(dw_*)` lines vs. develop.
- [ ] After merge: develop branch carries all 8 aliases; un-prefixed exports unchanged.

issues.body:
## Summary

Implements [#46](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/46) — standalone-source `%>%` binding for the two files that use the pipe outside the installed-package context.

Surfaced by Copilot review of DW-Production [PR #144](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/144) (WS v0.4.4 install) on 2026-05-29: v0.4.4's #36 fix added a `.cso_require()` fallback but missed that `requireNamespace()` doesn't attach `%>%`. The first pipe call in `aggregate_data_v2.R` still errored.

## Changes

### `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R` + `r/R/aggregate_data.R`

Same one-line gate pattern, just like `.cso_require`:

```r
if (!exists("%>%", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)) {
  `%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`
}
```

- Installed-package context: NAMESPACE's `importFrom(magrittr, "%>%")` wins → gate is a no-op
- Standalone-source: local binding via `magrittr` namespace → no `library(magrittr)` needed

### `r/R/zzz.R`

The `globalVariables()` comment claimed `.cso_require()` "attaches magrittr at source-time" — that's exactly the misdiagnosis Copilot caught. Updated to describe the actual v0.4.5 standalone-source mechanism (local `%>%` fallback gated by `exists()`).

### `r/tests/testthat/test-standalone-pipe-binding.R` (new — 3 tests)

- Installed-package binding (NAMESPACE path)
- Standalone fallback fires when `%>%` not in scope
- Gate is no-op when `%>%` already bound (preserves consumer-set pipe)

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 3 new tests added; existing testthat suite (300+ after v0.4.4) unchanged
- [ ] Smoke: `Rscript -e 'source("aggregate_data_v2.R"); aggregate_data_v2(<minimal-args>)'` in a fresh R session (without `library(magrittr)`) no longer errors at the first `%>%`

## Cross-references

- Issue: #46
- Parent #36 (addressed `.cso_require` but missed `%>%`): closed via PR #43
- v0.4.5 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/4>

issues.title: [v0.4.5] aggregate_data_v2.R standalone-source needs local %>% binding too

milestone.title: v0.4.5
milestone.description: Naming cleanup (#42) + standalone-source pipe binding fix — RELEASED 2026-05-29 as v0.4.5

issues.body:
# [v0.4.5] aggregate_data_v2.R standalone-source needs local `%>%` binding too

**Surfaced by**: Copilot review on DW-Production PR #144 (WS install of v0.4.4) — 2026-05-29
**Milestone**: v0.4.5
**Labels**: `bug`, `aggregate_data_v2`, `standalone-source`

## Summary

v0.4.4's #36 fix made `aggregate_data_v2.R` *partially* safe to source standalone by adding a local `.cso_require()` fallback. But the file still uses the unqualified `%>%` operator (e.g. `r/R/aggregate_data_v2.R:117,118,127,130,…`) without a binding. `.cso_require()` calls `requireNamespace()`, which loads but does NOT attach package exports. So `source("aggregate_data_v2.R")` still errors at the first `%>%` evaluation — `Error: could not find function "%>%"` — even when `magrittr` and `dplyr` are installed.

In the installed-package context this works because `NAMESPACE` carries `importFrom(magrittr, "%>%")`. In standalone-source mode (the use case #36 aimed at) there's no NAMESPACE, so the pipe is unbound.

## Proposed fix

Add a local pipe binding at the top of `aggregate_data_v2.R`, gated by the same exists-check pattern the `.cso_require` fallback uses:

```r
# v0.4.5 (#46): in standalone-source mode the file uses the magrittr
# pipe without an importFrom NAMESPACE entry; bind it locally if it
# isn't already in the search path. No-op when the file is loaded by
# the installed package (NAMESPACE has the importFrom).
if (!exists("%>%", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)) {
  `%>%` <- magrittr::`%>%`
}
```

The same pattern should be applied to any other vendored `.R` file that uses `%>%` standalone — quick survey:

```bash
grep -lE '%>%' r/R/*.R
```

If `r/R/aggregate_data.R`, `r/R/dw_pop.R`, etc. all use the pipe, each needs the same local binding (or a shared helper).

## Alternatives considered

- **Convert `%>%` to `|>` (native pipe)** — would work without any binding (R 4.1+); but the existing code uses `dplyr::ungroup() %>%` chains where the magrittr pipe is idiomatic, and the file's `R (>= 4.1.0)` is implicit, not declared. Converting all pipes is broader than scope.
- **Add `library(magrittr)` at the top** — would work but attach magrittr globally, which can collide with consumer scripts and tested-by-package semantics.
- **Use the qualified form `magrittr::"%>%"`** throughout — verbose; loses the pipe's readability advantage.

The local binding gated by `exists("%>%")` is the smallest fix that preserves both the installed-package path and the standalone-source path.

## Test plan

- [ ] Sourcing `aggregate_data_v2.R` standalone (without `library(magrittr)` or `library(dplyr)` first) defines `%>%` in the source environment
- [ ] In the installed-package context, the local binding is a no-op (NAMESPACE wins)
- [ ] `aggregate_data_v2(<minimal-args>)` runs end-to-end in a vanilla R session after standalone source
- [ ] Same survey + same fix applied to any other vendored file that uses `%>%` standalone

## Cross-references

- cso-toolkit#36 (parent — addressed `.cso_require` but missed `%>%`): closed via PR #43
- v0.4.4 release: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/releases/tag/v0.4.4>
- DW-Production PR #144 (the empirical surface): <https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/144>
- v0.4.5 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/TBD>

issues.title: release: develop → main — v0.4.1 + v0.4.2 + v0.4.3 + v0.4.3.1 + v0.4.4 catch-up
issues.body:
## Summary

`develop → main` catch-up after 5 tagged minor / patch releases since the last sync (PR #23, v0.4.0 merged 2026-05-27).

| Tag | Date | Theme |
|---|---|---|
| `v0.4.1` | 2026-05-27 | dialect + overwrite cleanups |
| `v0.4.2` | 2026-05-27 | `dialect = "base"` separator fix (Copilot-flagged silent-CSV bug) |
| `v0.4.3` | 2026-05-28 | parquet/dta `col_select = NULL` hotfix + `dw_use(cols_lenient = TRUE)` |
| `v0.4.3.1` | 2026-05-28 | bump 3 internal `dw_io.R` version stamps (release-process gap from v0.4.3) |
| **`v0.4.4`** | **2026-05-29** | quality release: `.dw_frozen_root()` discoverability + `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` helper + carry-forward bugs + `dw_` aliases |

## Headline themes since v0.4.0

### IO contract hardening (v0.4.3 → v0.4.4)

- `dw_use()` parquet / dta `col_select = NULL` returns all columns (was returning empty schema) — surfaced by DW-Production NT 1b CMRS-series 594K-row read (#30)
- New `dw_use(cols_lenient = FALSE)` flag for `any_of()`-style schema intersect — replaces the toxic `dplyr::any_of()` at-top-level pattern (#31)
- `.dw_frozen_root()` 3-tier resolution now surfaces the chosen tier in messages + error envelopes (#38)
- `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` exported helper consolidates the per-consumer regex duplication (#37)

### `dw_save()` byte-parity + consistency (v0.4.1, v0.4.2)

- `dialect = "base"` honours dispatched separator (was silently producing CSV in `.tsv` files — Copilot-flagged)
- Overwrite gate semantics for reviewer-mode scratch writes

### Helper resilience (v0.4.4)

- `aggregate_data_v2.R` now safe to source standalone (local `.cso_require()` fallback)
- `create_sector_script()` profile sentinel relaxed from `isTRUE()` to `!is.null()`; default re-aligned with `create_profile()`'s output
- `dw_aggregate_data_v2()` + `dw_create_sector_script()` canonical aliases added (non-breaking)

### Release-process learnings

- v0.4.3 → v0.4.3.1 patch surfaced that the release-cut checklist needed `grep -rn '"0\.[0-9]\.[0-9]"' r/R/ r/tests/` BEFORE tagging — now applied successfully to v0.4.4

## Empirical validation (DW-Production fanout audit, 2026-05-27 → 2026-05-29)

Every fix in v0.4.1 through v0.4.4 was surfaced empirically during DW-Production's reviewer-mode reproducibility audits:

- **NT** (PR #138; DW-Production): Run #8 (90.7 min) — 24/25 stages OK; 5b cell-level diff vs canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced; v0.4.2 → v0.4.3 fixes ratified
- **IM** (PR #139): Run #3 (12.8 min) — 87,573 rows × 19 cols, byte-equivalent to v0.1.0-rc1 baseline; v0.4.3.1 ratified; surfaced #37 + #38
- **WS** (PR #140): Run #2 (12.8 min) — 702,877 rows across 3 settings; v0.4.3.1 ratified
- **HVA**: scaffold install Copilot review surfaced the 3 carry-forward bugs in #36

## Follow-ups (post-merge)

| Issue | Title | Target |
|---|---|---|
| [#40](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/40) | `dw_regions()` redesign against `Country-and-Region-Metadata-API` | v0.5.0 |
| [#42](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/42) | Rename remaining 8 un-prefixed exports to `dw_` convention | v0.4.5 |
| [#15](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/15) | `dw_publish()` live submission branch (dry-run STUB shipped in v0.4.0) | v0.5.0 |

## No breaking API changes since v0.4.0

`v0.4.0` shipped the producer/reviewer mode contract (breaking from v0.3.0); since then the toolkit has been purely additive plus bug fixes. v0.3.0 → v0.4.x consumers had to migrate once; v0.4.0 → v0.4.4 consumers experience no behaviour change beyond the bug fixes.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI matrix on the develop-side passes (verified across PRs #29 / #33 / #34 / #35 / #39 / #41 / #43 / #44 — each merged with 4/4 CI green)
- [ ] After merge: `main` HEAD has DESCRIPTION Version = 0.4.4, `dw_toolkit_version()` = "0.4.4"
- [ ] Tag chain visible: `v0.4.0` ... `v0.4.4` all reachable from `main`
- [ ] DW-Production consumers' next `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.4")` resolves cleanly

Cuts v0.4.4 on develop.

| Issue | Title | PR |

| [#36](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/36) | 3 carry-forward bugs (2 sub-fixes + `dw_` aliases) | [#43](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/43) |
| [#37](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/37) | `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` helper | [#41](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/41) |

All three surfaced empirically during the DW-Production v0.4.3.1 fanout audit (DW-Production PRs [#138](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/138) / [#139](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/139) / [#140](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/140) merged 2026-05-28).

## Sanity grep before tagging (v0.4.3.1 lesson)

Per the checklist established after v0.4.3 missed three version stamps inside `dw_io.R` (PR #34 → patch PR #35), this release-cut PR runs:

```bash

```

→ **no matches**. All 6 version-stamp locations are updated to `0.4.4`. `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.4"`; the file header banner and docstring `@return` agree; both test expectations updated.

## No public-API changes

`v0.4.4` is purely additive (one new export: `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()`) + 3 fixes + 2 aliases for existing functions. v0.4.3.1 consumers experience no behaviour change; the new helpers must be explicitly composed into consumer profiles.

issues.title: fix(r): aggregate_data_v2 standalone-source + create_sector_script profile sentinel (closes #36 minus dw_regions)

milestone.title: v0.4.4
milestone.description: Carry-forward bugs from v0.4.3 audit + URL-allowlist helper

issues.body:
## Summary

Closes 2 of 3 sub-fixes flagged on [#36](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/36). The third (`dw_regions` value-arg propagation) is moot — `dw_regions` is being redesigned in [#40](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/40) (v0.5.0); the affected code path is removed.

Also adds the two `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases for the touched exports per the maintainer's API-consistency directive 2026-05-28. Follow-up [#42](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/42) tracks the remaining 8 un-prefixed exports as a single cleanup PR.

## Sub-fix 1 — `aggregate_data_v2.R` standalone-source resilience

Pre-fix: calling `.cso_require()` from `zzz.R` unconditionally — if `aggregate_data_v2.R` was sourced WITHOUT `zzz.R` first, the first call to `aggregate_data_v2(...)` errored with `could not find function .cso_require`.

Fix: a local `.cso_require()` fallback at the top of `aggregate_data_v2.R`, gated by `exists(".cso_require", mode = "function", inherits = TRUE)`. The fallback is byte-equivalent to the `zzz.R` version. When `zzz.R` has been sourced into `.GlobalEnv`, the shared helper wins (no redefinition).

## Sub-fix 2 — `create_sector_script()` profile sentinel

Pre-fix: the generated template checked for a `profile_DW_Production` variable that the DW-Production profile never defines.

Fix:
- Default `profile_name` changed `"profile_DW_Production"` → `"projectFolder"` (a variable the DW-Production profile actually sets)
- Sentinel check relaxed `isTRUE(<name>)` → `!is.null(<name>)` (accepts character paths, numerics, or boolean sentinels)
- Error message now names the missing variable
- Roxygen `@param` doc updated with common alternatives
- `create_dw_sector_script()` wrapper updated to pass `profile_name = "projectFolder"`

## `dw_`-prefixed aliases (per maintainer directive)

| Un-prefixed | New canonical | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| `aggregate_data_v2` | `dw_aggregate_data_v2` | `@rdname` alias |
| `create_sector_script` | `dw_create_sector_script` | `@rdname` alias |

Both names point to the same function and share the same `\link{}` man page via roxygen `@rdname`. Non-prefixed names continue to work — **no breaking change**.

## Tests

- `test-aggregate_data_v2-standalone.R` (new — 3 tests): standalone-source defines `.cso_require`; local fallback behaves like the zzz.R version; shared helper preserved when `zzz.R` is sourced first
- `test-dw_aliases.R` (new — 2 tests): `dw_aggregate_data_v2 === aggregate_data_v2`, `dw_create_sector_script === create_sector_script`

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 5 new tests added; pre-existing suite unchanged
- [ ] Smoke: `Rscript -e 'source("00_functions/aggregate_data_v2.R"); aggregate_data_v2(<minimal-args>)'` no longer errors with `.cso_require` not found
- [ ] Smoke: `create_dw_sector_script("Test", "tst")` generates a runner script whose profile-sentinel check succeeds against the DW-Production profile

## Cross-references

- Issue: #36
- Companion v0.4.4: #38 (frozen-root; MERGED via PR #39), #37 (allowlist helper; MERGED via PR #41), #40 (`dw_regions` redesign deferred to v0.5.0)
- Follow-up: #42 (prefix-cleanup for the other 8 un-prefixed exports)
- v0.4.4 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>
