issues.title: [v0.4.5+] Rename remaining 8 un-prefixed exports to the dw_ convention
issues.body:
# [v0.4.5 or v0.5.0] Rename remaining un-prefixed exports to use the `dw_` convention

**Surfaced by**: jpazvd directive 2026-05-28 during PR #42 (#36 reduced) discussion: "should we add the prefix dw_ in those as well?"
**Milestone**: v0.4.5 (or v0.5.0; design decision)
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `api`, `breaking-change-deferred`, `naming`

## Summary

Toolkit-export naming consolidates around the `dw_` prefix in v0.4.x. Eight exports predate that convention and still ship without the prefix:

| File | Un-prefixed export | Status today |
|---|---|---|
| `aggregate_data.R` | `aggregate_data` | un-prefixed (legacy v1) |
| `aggregate_data_v2.R` | `generate_agg_footnote` | un-prefixed |
| `aggregate_data_v2.R` | `apply_time_window` | un-prefixed |
| `generate_markdown_report.R` | `generate_markdown_report` | un-prefixed |
| `create_sector_script.R` | `create_dw_sector_script` | has `dw` infix, not prefix |
| `profile_helpers.R` | `create_profile` | un-prefixed |
| `profile_helpers.R` | `review_profile` | un-prefixed |
| `test_scripts.R` | `test_scripts` | un-prefixed |

PR #42 (closing #36) already added the canonical aliases for the two `#36`-touched exports:

- `dw_aggregate_data_v2` (alongside `aggregate_data_v2`)
- `dw_create_sector_script` (alongside `create_sector_script`)

This issue tracks adding aliases for the remaining 8.

## Proposed approach

Same pattern PR #42 used: keep the un-prefixed names exported for back-compat; add `dw_`-prefixed canonical aliases via roxygen `@rdname` so both names share the same man page.

```r
#' @rdname aggregate_data
#' @export
dw_aggregate_data <- aggregate_data

#' @rdname generate_markdown_report
#' @export
dw_generate_markdown_report <- generate_markdown_report

# ... and so on for the remaining 6
```

Naming choices that need explicit confirmation before implementation:

| Un-prefixed | Proposed prefixed | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `aggregate_data` | `dw_aggregate_data` | direct prefix add |
| `generate_agg_footnote` | `dw_aggregate_footnote` OR `dw_generate_agg_footnote` | shorter wins; the `generate_` verb feels redundant when the function already returns a string |
| `apply_time_window` | `dw_apply_time_window` OR `dw_filter_time_window` | the function filters more than it "applies"; sector-author input welcome |
| `generate_markdown_report` | `dw_markdown_report` OR `dw_generate_markdown_report` | shorter wins |
| `create_dw_sector_script` | `dw_create_sector_script_for_dw` (clunky) OR DROP and document the parametrised `dw_create_sector_script(...)` as the canonical | the `dw` infix is the historical thumbprint; redundant with the new prefix |
| `create_profile` | `dw_create_profile` | direct prefix add |
| `review_profile` | `dw_review_profile` | direct prefix add |
| `test_scripts` | `dw_audit_scripts` OR `dw_test_scripts` | `test_` collides with testthat's `test_*` conventions; renaming to `audit_` would surface the intent (the function is an `00_functions/` audit script, not a unit-test runner) |

## Scope (in)

- 8 new `dw_`-prefixed alias exports
- `NAMESPACE` additions
- NEWS entry under the next minor version (v0.4.5)
- A migration-guide section in the next release's NEWS suggesting that consumers move to the prefixed names

## Scope (out)

- Removing the un-prefixed names — separate deprecation cycle (target v0.5.0 with `.Deprecated()` warnings + `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()`)
- Restructuring how `aggregate_data_v2` relates to legacy `aggregate_data` — that's a separate API design question
- Changing function bodies — names only

## Cross-references

- PR [#43](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/43) (#36 reduced; introduced the first two aliases)
- Naming convention discussion: in the [PR #43](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/42) review thread
- v0.4.4 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>

issues.title: feat(r): dw_default_unicef_allowlist() helper for consumers (closes #37)

milestone.title: v0.4.4
milestone.description: Carry-forward bugs from v0.4.3 audit + URL-allowlist helper

issues.body:
## Summary

Implements [#37](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/37) — new exported helper `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` returning a character vector of `^...`-anchored regex patterns for UNICEF-DRP GitHub-raw + repo URLs. Consumers seed `dw_url_allowlist` from this constant instead of hand-writing the same patterns in every project profile.

Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production IM reviewer-mode audit on 2026-05-28 (PR #139): every URL-using sector script was duplicating the `^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/` pattern.

## Changes

### `r/R/dw_io.R`

New `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` exported function (~5 LOC + roxygen). Two patterns:

```r
c(
  "^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/",
  "^https://github\.com/unicef-drp/"
)
```

### Tests (`r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-default-allowlist.R`, new — 6 tests)

- Non-empty character vector returned
- All patterns anchored with `^`
- Canonical `raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/` URL matches `.is_allowlisted_url`
- Generic `github.com/unicef-drp/` URL matches
- **Non-UNICEF-DRP raw URL is REJECTED** (the safety guarantee preserved)
- Composition with project-specific extras via `c(...)` works

### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.4 candidate entry with usage example, the empirical-surface citation, and the safety-contract note.

### Roxygen / NAMESPACE

`man/dw_default_unicef_allowlist.Rd` (new); `NAMESPACE` export entry.

## Safety contract preserved

Purely additive. The URL-freeze contract is unchanged: consumers must opt in by composing the helper into their `dw_url_allowlist`; no URL is fetchable without explicit ratification. The helper covers `unicef-drp` org only (not other UNICEF data partners like OCHA-DAP / IATI — those remain consumer-controlled).

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] 6 new tests added; existing testthat suite (~290 tests after PR #39) unchanged
- [ ] DW-Production smoke: replace IM profile's hand-rolled allowlist with `dw_url_allowlist <- c(dw_default_unicef_allowlist(), ...)` → IM Run still produces 87,573 × 19 cols

## Cross-references

- Issue: #37
- Companion v0.4.4: #38 (frozen-root discoverability; MERGED via PR #39), #36 (carry-forward bugs; reduced scope after #40)
- DW-Production PR [#139](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/139) (IM audit; the empirical surface)
- v0.4.4 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>

issues.title: [v0.5.0] Redesign dw_regions() to consume the canonical Country-and-Region-Metadata-API

milestone.title: v0.5.0
milestone.description: Live dw_publish() submission branch (#15) + dw_regions() API redesign (#40)

issues.body:
# [v0.5.0] Redesign `dw_regions()` to consume the canonical Country-and-Region-Metadata-API

**Milestone**: `v0.5.0` (proposed; alongside the live `dw_publish` submission branch already in v0.5.0 milestone)
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `dw_regions`, `breaking-change`, `api-integration`
**Surfaced by**: jpazvd directive 2026-05-28 (during the v0.4.4 planning conversation): "dw_regions needs to be an API type of call to the database on https://github.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API"

## Summary

Today `dw_regions.R` reads region classifications from the **legacy** repo `unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata` via raw-content URLs:

- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata/refs/heads/main/output/AU.csv`
- `.../AUREC.csv`
- `.../SDGRC.csv`

Each URL is one classification per file, each consumer hand-writes the URL pattern, each cached frozen copy is one CSV. The legacy repo is being superseded by [`unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API`](https://github.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API) — a Python package (`pip install .`) that publishes a single canonical artifact at `~/.country_region_metadata/output/all_regions_long_format.csv` plus a Python entry point:

```python
from country_region_metadata import getRegionalClassification
df = getRegionalClassification("World Bank", "Region")
```

`dw_regions()` should consume the API package's canonical artifact, not the legacy raw URLs.

## Two integration options (need design decision)

### Option A — file-level integration via the canonical CSV (low-coupling)

The Python package writes `all_regions_long_format.csv` to a stable location. cso-toolkit's `dw_regions()` reads that file via `dw_use(path)`. Consumers (DW-Production, child-data, etc.) run the Python package once per cache TTL (default 24h) as a "refresh" step; reviewer-mode sessions read the cached file via the URL-freeze contract.

- **Pros**: zero R → Python language bridge; clean separation; works under the existing URL-freeze / dw_use contract; mode-aware (reviewer reads frozen, producer can refresh).
- **Cons**: consumers need a Python install + the package; refresh is a separate step.

### Option B — direct R → Python bridge via `reticulate`

`dw_regions()` calls the Python `getRegionalClassification(agency, classification)` directly via `reticulate`. The R side becomes a thin wrapper.

- **Pros**: idiomatic for R consumers; single function call.
- **Cons**: introduces a Python runtime dependency on every cso-toolkit consumer; `reticulate` has known reliability issues on UNICEF-laptop's AppLocker; the URL-freeze contract doesn't naturally apply (no URL, no cache).

**Recommendation**: Option A. It composes with the existing `dw_use` / URL-freeze contract, doesn't add a Python runtime requirement to R consumers who haven't installed the package, and keeps the reviewer/producer separation. The Python package becomes a producer-side ETL tool, and the resulting CSV is just another input file from R's perspective.

## Concrete API proposal (Option A)

```r
#' Region classification by agency
#'
#' Reads the canonical regions table from cso-toolkit's region-metadata
#' source (the `unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API` Python
#' package's published `all_regions_long_format.csv` artifact),
#' filtered by `agency` and `classification`.
#'
#' @param agency Character. Source agency. One of: "UNICEF", "World Bank",
#'   "UN", "UNSD", "FAO", "WHO", "AU", ... (the full set published by
#'   the Country-and-Region-Metadata-API package; see
#'   <https://github.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API>).
#' @param classification Character. Filter to a single classification
#'   within the agency (e.g., "Region", "Reporting Region", "Income
#'   Group"). If NULL, returns all classifications for the agency.
#' @param refresh_max_age Numeric. Maximum cache age in hours before
#'   the local artifact is considered stale. Default: 24 (matches the
#'   upstream package's `--cache-ttl` default).
#' @export
dw_regions <- function(agency,
                       classification = NULL,
                       refresh_max_age = 24) {
  # 1. Resolve the canonical CSV via dw_use() (network-first in reviewer,
  #    local-first in producer, frozen-snapshot in reviewer-without-Teams)
  csv <- dw_use(name = "all_regions_long_format.csv",
                sector = "_metadata", kind = "raw")
  # 2. Apply the standard filter
  df <- csv[csv$Agency == agency, ]
  if (!is.null(classification)) {
    df <- df[df$Classification == classification, ]
  }
  # 3. Cache-age warning (informational; the producer refreshes, not us)
  if (file.info(csv_resolved_path)$mtime < Sys.time() - refresh_max_age * 3600) {
    warning(sprintf(
      "[dw_regions] canonical regions CSV is more than %d hours old. ",
      "Consider asking the producer to re-run the regions Python pipeline.",
      refresh_max_age
    ))
  }
  df
}
```

The current `dw_regions()` signature (`dw_regions(data, value = ...)` etc.) becomes deprecated — the new function is purely a metadata accessor, not a data-mutation step. Consumers that today do `dw_regions(data, value = ...)` migrate to:

```r
# old
result <- dw_regions(data, value = "OBS_VALUE")

# new
regions <- dw_regions(agency = "World Bank", classification = "Region")
result  <- data %>% left_join(regions, by = c("REF_AREA" = "ISO3Code"))
```

The redesign is intentionally breaking — `dw_regions` becomes a thin
accessor, and the join logic moves to the caller where it belongs.

## Implications for v0.4.4 (current milestone)

- `dw_regions.R`'s value-arg propagation bug (one of the three carry-forward sub-bugs in [#36](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/36)) becomes **moot**: the function is being rewritten with a different signature. Don't spend a v0.4.4 patch on a soon-to-be-removed code path.
- v0.4.4's #36 PR drops the `dw_regions.R` sub-fix. The other two sub-fixes (`aggregate_data_v2.R` `.cso_require()` dependency; `create_sector_script.R` profile sentinel) land as planned.
- Update issue #36 to note that the `dw_regions` sub-fix is dropped pending this v0.5.0 redesign.

## Scope (in)

- New `dw_regions()` signature + implementation (Option A)
- Deprecation notice on the v0.4.x signature
- Migration guide in NEWS + a vignette section
- `Country-and-Region-Metadata-API` package added to `Suggests` (for producer-mode refresh; reviewer mode doesn't need it)

## Scope (out)

- Hosting the canonical CSV — that's the producer's responsibility (run the Python package on a schedule, commit the output to a Teams location consumers can read)
- Reticulate bridge (Option B above) — rejected per the recommendation
- Backporting the new signature to v0.4.x — too invasive

## Cross-references

- Country-and-Region-Metadata-API repo: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata-API>
- Legacy Country-and-Region-Metadata repo (being superseded): <https://github.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata>
- v0.5.0 milestone: <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/TBD>
- DW-Production IM audit (the sector that exercised the legacy URLs): PR #139
- v0.4.4 milestone (which originally tracked the value-arg bug): <https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/milestone/2>
- Companion v0.4.4 issue: #36 (3 carry-forward bugs; will drop the dw_regions sub-fix per this issue)

issues.body:
## Summary

Implements [#38](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/issues/38) — `.dw_frozen_root()` resolution discoverability + extended error envelope for missing frozen copies.

Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production IM reviewer-mode audit on 2026-05-28 against v0.4.3.1: the toolkit's `.dw_frozen_root()` fallback silently resolved to `<githubFolder>/_frozen` instead of DW-Production's `<projectFolder>/01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/` convention. Three IM runs (75+ min on cold Teams) to diagnose what a single session-start message could have surfaced.

## Changes

### `r/R/dw_io.R`

- New internal helper `.dw_frozen_root_resolved()` returns `(path, source)` where `source` is `"dw_frozen_root"` / `"githubFolder"` / `"getwd"`.
- New internal helper `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` emits a session-scoped notice on tier #2/#3 fallback (`message()` for #2, `warning()` for #3); silent when consumer explicitly sets `dw_frozen_root`. Gated by `.__cso_toolkit_frozen_root_notified__` sentinel.
- `.dw_frozen_root()` (path-only legacy API) preserved unchanged; body delegates to `.dw_frozen_root_resolved()$path` for single-source-of-truth.
- `.resolve_remote_url()` error envelope now includes "Frozen-root resolution: `<path>` (`<tier-name>`)" + a 2-step Fix block distinguishing "wrong path" from "missing file."

### `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-frozen-root.R` (new — 7 tests)

- Tier #1 / #2 / #3 resolution
- `.dw_frozen_root()` legacy contract preserved
- Error envelope mentions resolution tier
- `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` fires at most once per session
- `.dw_frozen_root_notify_once()` silent when `dw_frozen_root` explicit

### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.4 candidate entry under Unreleased citing the DW-Production IM audit.

## No public-API changes

`dw_frozen_root` global semantics unchanged. Consumers who already explicitly set it (like DW-Production after the IM Run #2 patch) get no behaviour change. v0.4.3.1 callers of `.dw_frozen_root()` keep working.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs)
- [ ] `testthat::test_dir("r/tests/testthat")` adds 7 new tests + the legacy 285+ all pass
- [ ] Smoke test: re-run DW-Production IM `01_immunization.R` without the profile-side `dw_frozen_root` patch — verify the new error envelope surfaces the wrong-path tier on the first failure (instead of requiring grep diving)

## Cross-references

- Issue: #38
- DW-Production PR [#139](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/139) (IM audit; the empirical surface)
- Companion v0.4.4 work: #37 (allowlist helper), #36 (carry-forward bugs)

issues.body:
# [v0.4.4] Make `.dw_frozen_root()` resolution discoverable + warn on fallback

**Milestone**: `v0.4.4`
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `dw_use`, `url-freeze`, `discoverability`
**Surfaced by**: DW-Production IM reviewer-mode audit on 2026-05-28 (PR #139)

## Summary

`.dw_frozen_root()` in `r/R/dw_io.R` resolves the URL-freeze cache root via a silent 3-tier fallback:

1. `dw_frozen_root` global if set
2. `<githubFolder>/_frozen` if `githubFolder` is set
3. `<getwd()>/_frozen`

When a consumer's project structure doesn't match the fallback heuristic (#2 or #3), `dw_use("https://...")` errors with **Missing frozen copy: <wrong path>**, and the consumer has to grep `dw_io.R` to discover the resolution chain.

## Empirical hit (DW-Production IM, 2026-05-28)

DW-Production stores frozen snapshots at `<project>/01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/...` per its standing convention. The toolkit's fallback #2 resolved to `<githubFolder>/_frozen` instead → IM Run #2 (28.9 min) errored on `Missing frozen copy: C:/GitHub/mytasks/_frozen/raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata/.../AU.csv` even though the actual snapshot existed at `C:/GitHub/mytasks/DW-Production/01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata/.../AU.csv`. The profile was patched to set `dw_frozen_root` explicitly.

## Proposed v0.4.4 changes

### Change 1 — emit a session-load message when the fallback fires

At the first `dw_use("https://...")` call in a session:

- If `dw_frozen_root` global is set: silent (consumer is opted in)
- If fallback #2 fires (`<githubFolder>/_frozen`): `message("[dw_use:remote] dw_frozen_root not set; falling back to ", path)`
- If fallback #3 fires (`<getwd()>/_frozen`): same with `warning()` (much less reliable than #2)

Cheap discoverability win — surfaces the resolution choice without grepping.

### Change 2 — extend the error envelope when frozen copy is missing

Today:

```
[cso_toolkit.dw_use:remote] Reviewer mode forbids fetching from the network.
 Missing frozen copy: <path>
 URL: <url>
 Fix: a producer must call dw_use(...) once and commit the frozen file + sidecar.
```

Add a line surfacing WHICH resolution path picked the root:

```
 Frozen-root resolution: <dw_frozen_root global | githubFolder fallback | getwd() fallback>
 Try setting `dw_frozen_root <- "<your-canonical-path>"` in your profile if the fallback path is wrong.
```

### Change 3 (optional) — document the project-relative pattern in NEWS / vignette

DW-Production stores `_frozen/` under `01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/` so reviewers see the same vintages via git. Document this pattern as the recommended convention for repos that commit the cache to source control.

## Scope (in)

- 2 small touchpoints in `r/R/dw_io.R` (`.dw_frozen_root` + `.resolve_remote_url` envelope)
- 1 NEWS entry under v0.4.4
- 0 behaviour changes for consumers who already set `dw_frozen_root` explicitly

## Scope (out)

- Changing the resolution heuristic itself (would break consumers that depend on the existing fallback chain)
- Auto-pinning `dw_frozen_root` to a project-relative path (too project-specific)

## Cross-references

- DW-Production PR #139 (IM audit; captures the empirical failure + profile-side patch)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#37 (UNICEF default allowlist helper; same v0.4.4 milestone)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#36 (3 carry-forward bugs; same v0.4.4 milestone)

issues.body:
# [v0.4.4] `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` helper for consumers

**Milestone**: `v0.4.4`
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `dw_use`, `url-freeze`
**Surfaced by**: DW-Production reviewer-mode audit of IM 01_immunization.R on 2026-05-28 against v0.4.3.1 toolkit

## Summary

`dw_use("https://...")` reads from the URL-freeze cache once the URL has been ratified into `dw_url_allowlist`. Today every consumer / project profile (DW-Production's `profile_DW-Production.R`, the upcoming child-data clones, etc.) has to hand-write the regex patterns for the UNICEF-DRP organisation. That is:

- duplicative — every profile inlines the same `^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/` pattern
- brittle — drift in the canonical pattern goes silently (one profile gets it right, another doesn't)
- inconsistent — `OCHA-DAP`, `IATI-Secretariat`, and other common UNICEF-data-partner orgs face the same problem with no shared default

## Proposed addition (toolkit-side)

Add a documented constant / accessor to `r/R/dw_io.R`:

```r
#' Standard URL-allowlist patterns for UNICEF-owned reference data
#'
#' Returns a character vector of `^...`-anchored regex patterns covering
#' UNICEF DRP GitHub-raw URLs. Consumers seed `dw_url_allowlist` from this
#' constant instead of re-deriving the patterns per project. Extend with
#' project-specific patterns via `c(dw_default_unicef_allowlist(), ...)`.
#'
#' @return Character vector of regex patterns.
#' @family url-freeze
#' @export
dw_default_unicef_allowlist <- function() {
  c(
    "^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/unicef-drp/",
    "^https://github\.com/unicef-drp/"
  )
}
```

Consumers then write:

```r
# In profile_*.R
dw_url_allowlist <- c(
  dw_default_unicef_allowlist(),
  # project-specific extras:
  "^https://yourorg\.github\.io/"
)
```

## Scope (in)

- The helper itself (one function, ~10 LOC + docs + 1 unit test)
- NEWS entry documenting the pattern + the recommendation to seed `dw_url_allowlist` from it

## Scope (out)

- Setting the allowlist *automatically* (would defeat the URL-freeze safety contract — consumers must opt in)
- Adding allowlist patterns for non-UNICEF orgs (project-specific decision)

## Why this surfaced

Reviewer-mode run of IM on 2026-05-28 against v0.4.3.1 toolkit failed at the URL-freeze gate (`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata/.../AU.csv` was not in the empty `dw_url_allowlist`). Even though the frozen snapshot existed in `01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/`, the allowlist check refused the resolution. DW-Production patched its `profile_DW-Production.R` to seed the allowlist; this issue brings that fix upstream as a reusable helper.

## Test plan

- [ ] `dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` returns the expected character vector
- [ ] Seeding `dw_url_allowlist <- dw_default_unicef_allowlist()` and calling `dw_use("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata/refs/heads/main/output/AU.csv")` resolves to the frozen path (when present) without erroring on the allowlist gate
- [ ] Doc string + NEWS entry cross-reference this issue

## Cross-references

- DW-Production PR #139 (IM audit report — captures the empirical failure + the profile-side workaround)
- cso-toolkit #36 (separate v0.4.4 carry-forward bug issue)

issues.body:
# [v0.4.4] Three substantive bugs in toolkit helpers (carry-forward from PR #33 / v0.4.3.1)

**Milestone**: `v0.4.4`
**Labels**: `bug`, `dw_regions`, `aggregate_data_v2`, `create_sector_script`
**Surfaced by**: Copilot review of DW-Production PR #137 (HVA scaffold install of v0.4.3.1)

## Summary

Three substantive bugs in toolkit helpers that exist on v0.4.2 + v0.4.3 + v0.4.3.1 (unchanged across all three since v0.4.0). They were caught when Copilot reviewed an HVA scaffold-install PR — the first time these files landed on a sector branch that lacked them entirely, so the review wasn't filtered by "compared to the same file pre-edit". The install PRs (DW-Production #134/#135/#136/#137) are merging without blocking on these because the bugs predate the install; this issue tracks the upstream fix for v0.4.4.

## Bug 1 — `aggregate_data_v2.R:60` calls `.cso_require()` from `zzz.R`

```r
.cso_require("dplyr")  # in aggregate_data_v2.R
```

`.cso_require()` is defined in `zzz.R`. Sectors that source `aggregate_data_v2.R` directly (without sourcing `zzz.R` first) will fail with `could not find function ".cso_require"`. The internal cso-toolkit testthat suite passes because the package loader sources `zzz.R` first, but vendored consumers don't always do that.

**Fix options**:
- Make `aggregate_data_v2.R` self-contained (inline the `.cso_require()` definition or use `requireNamespace()` directly).
- OR: document that `zzz.R` must be sourced before `aggregate_data_v2.R` in the vendoring README.

Prefer option 1 (self-contained) since vendoring is the production model per the cso-toolkit strategy doc.

## Bug 2 — `dw_regions.R:180` does not propagate the `value` argument through to the merge cleanup

When the caller passes `value = "some_other_col"` to `dw_regions(...)`:

1. The population merge fetches a denominator column named `OBS_VALUE` (the toolkit's internal convention)
2. `weight_col` is set to that fallback `OBS_VALUE`
3. The cleanup at the end does not rename or drop the fallback `OBS_VALUE` column

**Effect**: `dw_regions(data, value = "some_other_col")` returns a tibble with a spurious `OBS_VALUE` column that contains population weights, not the requested value. Documented shape-compatibility contract is broken.

**Fix**: rename the fetched population column to a private weight name (e.g., `.__weight__`) before the merge, or extend the cleanup block to drop the fallback `OBS_VALUE` when `value != "OBS_VALUE"`.

## Bug 3 — `create_sector_script.R:112` checks for a `profile_DW_Production` sentinel that the profile never defines

The generated sector-runner template includes:

```r
if (!exists("profile_DW_Production")) {
  stop("Profile not loaded; source('profile_DW-Production.R') first.")
}
```

But the only occurrence of `profile_DW_Production` in `profile_DW-Production.R` is the header comment — no variable / function with that name is ever defined.

**Effect**: every `create_sector_script()` / `create_dw_sector_script()` output errors out immediately on source, even when the profile WAS sourced successfully.

**Fix**: use a variable the profile actually sets — `projectFolder`, `dwCodes`, or whatever the canonical sentinel is — and update the template. Or: have the profile set `profile_DW_Production <- TRUE` at the end as a sentinel and document this in the profile-templating skill.

## Cross-references

- cso-toolkit v0.4.3.1 release: https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/releases/tag/v0.4.3.1
- DW-Production install PRs: #134 (NT), #135 (WS), #136 (IM), #137 (HVA — where these were surfaced)
- Original DW-Production NT reviewer-mode audit: #133

## Priority

P2 — none of these bugs block the v0.4.3.1 install in DW-Production sector branches (the affected call sites are either gated behind not-yet-used adoption or behind specific argument shapes), but all three should land in v0.4.4 before the next round of sector-script adoption.

issues.title: chore(release): v0.4.3.1 — bump three version stamps inside dw_io.R
issues.body:
## Summary

Patch release. v0.4.3 (PR #34 earlier today) bumped `DESCRIPTION::Version` and renamed `NEWS.md` but **missed three version stamps inside `r/R/dw_io.R`**. Caught by Copilot review on DW-Production install PRs #134 + #136.

The drift would have polluted `dw_publish()` provenance sidecars with the wrong toolkit version — the whole point of carrying the stamp inside the file is to survive copy-out into a consumer's `00_functions/`.

## Changes

- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 3: `# Toolkit version: 0.4.2` → `0.4.3.1`
- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 254: docstring `Currently "0.4.2"` → `"0.4.3.1"`
- `r/R/dw_io.R` line 266: `dw_toolkit_version()` return `"0.4.2"` → `"0.4.3.1"`
- `r/DESCRIPTION` Version: `0.4.3` → `0.4.3.1`; Date unchanged
- `NEWS.md`: new `## v0.4.3.1` section between Unreleased and v0.4.3
- `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd`: regenerated by `roxygen2::roxygenise(".")` to pick up the docstring change

No behavioural changes. Drop-in replacement for v0.4.3.

## Release-cut checklist gap

For the next minor (v0.5.0), the release-PR template should require running `grep -rn '0\.[0-9]\.[0-9]' r/R/` before tagging. This patch documents the gap; the procedural fix lands separately.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs, same as v0.4.3 release)
- [ ] `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.3.1"` in a smoke session
- [ ] After merge: tag `v0.4.3.1` and re-sync the 4 DW-Production install PRs (#134/#135/#136/#137)

milestone.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

issues.body:
## Summary

Cuts v0.4.3 on develop.

- `NEWS.md`: "Unreleased" → "v0.4.3 (2026-05-28)"; three h4 subsections demoted to h3 (the dropped "candidate entries" h3 was their previous parent); duplicated lead paragraph trimmed; fresh "Unreleased" section added back at the top.
- `r/DESCRIPTION`: `Version: 0.4.2 → 0.4.3`, `Date: 2026-05-27 → 2026-05-28`.

## Contents of v0.4.3

| Issue | Title | PR |
|---|---|---|
| #30 | dw_use(): parquet / dta `col_select = NULL` conditional dispatch | #33 |
| #31 | dw_use(): `cols_lenient = FALSE` flag for `any_of()`-style schema intersect | #33 |
| #32 | Provenance sidecars (producer → reviewer → ingestor integrity chain) | **design-only at this stage; implementation deferred to a follow-up PR within the same milestone** |

Both code-shipping issues (#30, #31) merged via PR #33 (commit `1493751`). Empirical validation came from the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit on 2026-05-27 (DW-Production PR [#133](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/133)) — NT pipeline now runs end-to-end through 4b → 5b in reviewer mode; cell-level diff against canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced.

## After this PR merges

1. Tag `v0.4.3` on the resulting merge commit on `develop`.
2. Cut a GitHub Release pointing at the tag, pulling the v0.4.3 section of NEWS.md as the release body.
3. DW-Production runs `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.3")` — drops `dw_io.R` from `local_edits`, bumps `pulled_version` from `v0.4.2` to `v0.4.3`.

## Test plan

- [ ] R CMD check matrix passes (4 jobs — same as PR #33's post-fix run)
- [ ] DESCRIPTION + NEWS heading hierarchy renders cleanly on the GitHub Release page
- [ ] After merge: `git tag v0.4.3 <merge_sha>` succeeds and pushes
- [ ] `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.3")` on a DW-Production consumer recognises the new tag and refreshes hashes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: fix(r): port v0.4.3 candidates from DW-Production NT audit (closes #30, #31)

milestone.title: v0.4.3
milestone.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

issues.body:
## Summary

Ports the two `dw_use` fixes that landed first as DW-Production `local_edits` against v0.4.2 (DW-Production PR [#133](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/133)) into canonical cso-toolkit. Surfaced empirically by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit on 2026-05-27.

Closes #30. Closes #31.

(Issue #32 — provenance sidecars — is the design-foundation companion; implementation is a separate PR within the v0.4.3 milestone.)

### Why this PR exists

DW-Production's NT pipeline cannot reproduce its canonical warehouse output without these two fixes:

1. **dw_use() returns empty schemas on default parquet reads.** Run #6 (before fix): 10/25 NT stages OK with 13 downstream stages failing `object 'COLUMN' not found` because `dw_use(*.parquet)` was returning a zero-column tibble.
2. **dw_use(cols = dplyr::any_of(...)) errors fatally under tidyselect >= 1.2.0.** Sector scripts use `any_of()` for "select these IF present" semantics; that pattern no longer works at top level.

After both fixes + the cols_lenient feature on call sites: Run #8 (90.7 min) → 24/25 stages OK; 5b cell-level diff vs canonical `dw_nut.csv` produced.

### Changes

#### `r/R/dw_io.R`

- Parquet and dta branches now use conditional dispatch (`if is.null(cols) pass through ...`, `else pass col_select = cols`).
- New `cols_lenient = FALSE` parameter on `dw_use()`.
- New internal helper `.dw_schema_cols(path, fmt)` — cheap metadata-only schema introspection per format (parquet via `arrow::open_dataset()`, csv/tsv/txt via `data.table::fread(nrows = 0)`, dta via `haven::read_dta(n_max = 0)`, xlsx via `readxl::read_xlsx(n_max = 0)`).

#### `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-cols-lenient.R` (new)

Seven tests covering:
- parquet without cols → all columns
- dta without cols → all columns
- parquet with explicit cols → strict selection respected
- `cols_lenient = TRUE` intersects with parquet schema
- `cols_lenient = TRUE` intersects with csv schema
- empty intersection → warning + read all
- `cols_lenient = FALSE` default preserves strict semantics

#### `NEWS.md`

v0.4.3 candidate entries added under "Unreleased". At release cut, the section rename folds the entries up.

### Migration for sector scripts

```r
# Before (errors fatally under tidyselect >= 1.2.0):
dw_use(path, cols = dplyr::any_of(c("a", "b", "c")))

# After:
dw_use(path, cols = c("a", "b", "c"), cols_lenient = TRUE)
```

`dplyr::all_of()` at top level is deprecated in tidyselect 1.2.0 anyway — pass a bare character vector for strict semantics.

### Validation harness

DW-Production PR #133 was the empirical validation: the exact code in this PR ran the NT pipeline (`tests/test_nt_full_pipeline.R`) end-to-end through 4b → 5b in reviewer mode. Stage table from Run #8:

| Stage | Status | Time |
|---|---|---|
| 0_execute_conductor | OK | 854.2s |
| 1a–1f imports | all OK | <1m total |
| **2a_agg_prep** (was FATAL on `any_of()`) | **OK** | 794.9s |
| 2b–2j aggregations | all OK | ~55s total |
| 3a–3c targets | all OK | ~10s total |
| **4b_dw_format_merge** (was unreachable before fixes) | **OK; wrote dw_nut_tocopy.csv** | 1200.2s |
| 5a_compare_vs_lastrun | (separate sector script bug; fixed in DW-Prod) | n/a |
| 5b_compare_vs_warehouse | **OK; cell-level diff produced** | 565.2s |

### After this PR lands

- Tag `v0.4.3` on cso-toolkit (separate PR or `develop` → `main` workflow).
- DW-Production runs `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = "v0.4.3")` to drop `dw_io.R` from `local_edits` and bump `pulled_version` to `v0.4.3`.
- Provenance sidecars (#32) get a follow-up PR within the same milestone.

## Test plan

- [ ] `R CMD check r/` passes (or the equivalent CI invocation)
- [ ] `testthat::test_dir("r/tests/testthat")` passes; new `test-dw_io-cols-lenient.R` adds 7 tests
- [ ] Smoke-test on a real DW-Production sector script (NT 2a already validated via DW-Production PR #133 Run #8)
- [ ] Copilot review surfaces no NEW substantive findings (the in-PR comments already explain the rationale)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: [v0.4.3] Provenance sidecars: producer -> reviewer -> ingestor integrity chain

milestone.title: v0.4.3
milestone.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

issues.body:
# [v0.4.3] Provenance sidecars: producer → reviewer → ingestor integrity chain

**Milestone**: `v0.4.3` (Integrity release)
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `dw_save`, `dw_use`, `dw_publish`, `provenance`, `breaking-change-after-v0.5.0`
**Type**: New feature (foundational; extends to dw_publish in a follow-up)

## Summary

Today `dw_use` + `dw_save` carry no end-to-end integrity guarantee. A reviewer can read a stale local copy of a canonical file without noticing; a producer can write a canonical artifact that gets edited out-of-band; an ingestor (future `dw_publish`) can push a file whose bytes don't match what the producer wrote. The `.provenance.json` sidecar pattern (already shipped in v0.4.0 for URL-frozen reads, see `.write_remote_provenance` at `r/R/dw_io.R:1421`) is the right primitive to extend.

This issue proposes making sidecars first-class for every canonical write, with mode-aware default-on verification on every read.

## Three-role integrity chain

```
producer_inputs ──[sha256 in sidecar]──► producer writes output + sidecar
                                          │
                                          ├─► reviewer reads canonical
                                          │     verifies producer's sha256
                                          │     re-runs pipeline → reviewer_output + sidecar
                                          │     compares own output sha256 vs canonical
                                          │       match → byte-reproducible
                                          │       diff  → cell-level drift report (dw_compare)
                                          │
                                          └─► ingestor reads canonical
                                                verifies producer's sha256
                                                pushes to Helix via dw_publish
                                                records helix_submission_id in sidecar
```

## Sidecar schema (extends existing `.write_remote_provenance` pattern)

```json
{
  "path": "01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/dw_nut.csv",
  "sha256": "f8a3b…",
  "bytes": 487123456,
  "content_hash": "9e2c1…",
  "rows": 1165291,
  "cols": 26,
  "col_names": ["DATAFLOW", "REF_AREA", "INDICATOR", ...],
  "written_at": "2026-05-27T21:35:48Z",
  "written_by": "jpazvd",
  "writer_role": "producer | reviewer | ingestor",
  "dw_mode": "producer",
  "dw_toolkit_version": "0.4.3",
  "git_commit": "c4c2076",
  "git_branch": "fix/nt-reviewer-mode-blockers-v2",
  "sector": "nt",
  "vintage": "2026-04-10",
  "inputs": [
    {
      "path": ".../cmrs2_series_accepted.parquet",
      "sha256": "...",
      "bytes": 234567890,
      "rows": 594123
    }
  ],
  "helix_submission_id": null,
  "published_at": null
}
```

### Two hashes, intentional

- **`sha256`** — byte hash of the file. Catches tampering + serialization drift.
- **`content_hash`** — deterministic hash of canonically-sorted rows × values. Catches semantic drift independent of CSV formatting (`scipen`, CRLF vs LF, locale decimals, row order). Initial implementation: `digest::digest(dplyr::arrange(x, dplyr::across(dplyr::everything())))`. FP-tolerance (round-to-N or interval hashing) deferred to v0.5.x.

### Scope of `inputs`

Immediate parents only — the files that `dw_use`d to produce this output. Recursive DAG verification is the reviewer's responsibility (they can walk the chain), not the toolkit's. Keeps sidecars small and writes fast.

## Default-behaviour matrix (role × write/read)

### Write side — `dw_save(provenance = TRUE)` (default)

| | Producer mode | Reviewer mode | Ingestor mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| canonical writes | **default ON** (atomic with Teams + Z: copies) | (blocked; reviewer can't write canonical) | **default ON** (sidecar updated post-Helix-push) |
| `_local/` scratch writes | off (noise; opt-in) | **default ON** (defensible audit trail) | n/a |
| explicit opt-out | `provenance = FALSE` | `provenance = FALSE` (rare) | NOT ALLOWED (no publish without sidecar) |

### Read side — `dw_use(verify_provenance = TRUE)` (new param, default ON)

| | Producer mode | Reviewer mode | Ingestor mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| canonical reads | warn on missing; **hard-fail on sha256 mismatch** | **hard-fail on missing OR mismatch** | **hard-fail always** |
| `_local/` reads | silent (scratch) | silent (scratch) | n/a |
| explicit opt-out | `verify_provenance = FALSE` (DBM bootstrap on un-stamped legacy files) | `verify_provenance = FALSE` with stern warning (per user 2026-05-27: "hardfail unless this is specified [as] overwrite") | NOT ALLOWED |

The override flag `verify_provenance = FALSE` is the semantic mirror of `overwrite = TRUE` on the write side: both default to refuse-and-protect, both flip with an explicit caller flag.

## API surface

New internal helpers in `r/R/dw_io.R`:

```r
.write_provenance_sidecar(path, sidecar_data, atomic = TRUE)
.read_provenance_sidecar(path)  # NULL if missing
.dw_file_sha256(path)            # memoized by (path, mtime, size) in session env
.dw_content_hash(x)              # deterministic row-major canonical form
```

New public helper (composable with 5a/5b compare scripts):

```r
dw_verify_provenance(path, depth = c("self", "chain")) -> list(
  status = c("match", "drift", "missing", "sidecar_corrupt"),
  details = ...
)
```

Integration points:
- `dw_save` writes sidecar alongside file (atomic; if file write fails, sidecar doesn't get written)
- `dw_use` reads + verifies sidecar (per default-matrix above)
- `dw_publish` (cso-toolkit#15, follow-up): verifies before push, updates sidecar after

## Performance / cost

- **sha256 on 470 MB CSV ≈ 3 s**. Acceptable per write. Memoization on read makes the second read free in a session.
- **Sidecar storage**: ~500 B JSON per data file. Negligible.
- **Hot-loop reads** (NT 2a reads ~10 parquets per stage): memoization by `(path, mtime, size)` in session env prevents re-hashing.

## Migration plan

- **v0.4.3**: ships infrastructure (write + read + verify helpers + `verify_provenance` flag). Defaults: ON for new writes, **WARN-on-missing-then-pass** for reads (no break).
- **v0.4.4**: flip read defaults to hard-fail per the matrix above. Two minor versions warning lead-time is enough.
- **v0.5.0**: missing sidecars on canonical reads = hard error always.
- One-shot backfill: `dw_backfill_sidecars(root)` — walks `teamsWrkDataCanonical/...` once, computes hashes, writes sidecars. Producer-only operation; runs once per sector during the v0.4.3 → v0.4.4 transition window.

## Cross-references

- v0.4.0 precedent: `.write_remote_provenance` at `r/R/dw_io.R:1421` (URL-freeze sidecars)
- v0.4.0 precedent: `dw_verify_z` (Z: integrity check on every `dw_use`)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#30 (parquet col_select hotfix, same milestone)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#31 (cols_lenient feature, same milestone)
- Follow-up: cso-toolkit#15 (`dw_publish` — integrate sidecar update post-push)
- DW-Production design conversation: 2026-05-27 between @jpazvd and AI on PR #133 (NT reviewer-mode integrity)

## Open design decisions locked

| Question | Decision | Date |
|---|---|---|
| Default-on for producer writes? | Yes for canonical, off for `_local/` scratch | 2026-05-27 |
| Hard-fail vs soft-warn on sidecar-missing in reviewer mode? | Hard-fail unless caller passes `verify_provenance = FALSE` | 2026-05-27 |
| `content_hash` algorithm | Start simple: `digest::digest(arrange(x, across(everything())))`; FP-tolerance deferred to v0.5.x | 2026-05-27 |
| `inputs` chain scope | Immediate parents only; recursion is reviewer's job | 2026-05-27 |

issues.title: [v0.4.3] dw_use: add cols_lenient = FALSE flag for any_of()-style schema intersect

milestone.title: v0.4.3
milestone.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

issues.body:
# [v0.4.3] dw_use: add `cols_lenient = FALSE` flag for any_of()-style schema intersect

**Milestone**: `v0.4.3` (Integrity release)
**Labels**: `enhancement`, `dw_use`, `api`
**Type**: New feature
**Builds on**: cso-toolkit#30 (parquet/dta col_select hotfix, same milestone)

## Summary

Sector scripts in DW-Production (NT confirmed; HVA + IM exhibit the same pattern) want `dplyr::any_of()`-style semantics on `dw_use(cols = ...)`: "select these columns IF present in the schema, ignore the absent ones." Today this is unsupported because:

- `dplyr::any_of()` / `tidyselect::any_of()` **error fatally** when called outside a tidyselect selecting context (tidyselect >= 1.2.0). NT 2a_agg_prep.R failed for this reason in Run #7.
- `dplyr::all_of()` is **deprecated** at top level (tidyselect 1.2.0) with a warning; soft-fails today but will hard-fail in a future tidyselect.
- R evaluates `dw_use(cols = dplyr::any_of(c(...)))` BEFORE handing control to `dw_use`, so no lazy-eval trick inside the toolkit can intercept this.

## Proposed API

```r
dw_use(path, cols = c("col1", "col2", "missing_col"), cols_lenient = TRUE)
```

- `cols_lenient = FALSE` (default) → strict; if any requested col is missing from the schema, the underlying reader errors. Backwards-compatible.
- `cols_lenient = TRUE` → toolkit intersects `cols` with the file's schema BEFORE the data read. Empty intersection → warning + read all columns (forward-progress guarantee, never errors).

## Implementation

New internal helper for cheap, metadata-only schema introspection per format:

```r
.dw_schema_cols <- function(path, fmt) {
  tryCatch(
    switch(fmt,
      parquet = {
        .require("arrow")
        arrow::open_dataset(path)$schema$names  # metadata-only; no data read
      },
      csv =, tsv =, txt = {
        .require("data.table")
        sep <- if (identical(fmt, "csv")) "," else "\t"
        names(data.table::fread(input = path, sep = sep, nrows = 0,
                                showProgress = FALSE))
      },
      dta = { .require("haven"); names(haven::read_dta(path, n_max = 0)) },
      xlsx = { .require("readxl"); names(readxl::read_xlsx(path, n_max = 0)) },
      NULL
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      warning(sprintf("[cso_toolkit.dw_use] cols_lenient schema introspect failed for %s (%s): %s. Skipping lenient intersection.",
                      basename(path), fmt, conditionMessage(e)), call. = FALSE)
      NULL
    }
  )
}
```

Then in `dw_use()`, after format detection and before dispatch:

```r
if (isTRUE(cols_lenient) && !is.null(cols) && length(cols) > 0) {
  schema_names <- .dw_schema_cols(resolved, fmt)
  if (!is.null(schema_names)) {
    kept <- intersect(cols, schema_names)
    if (length(kept) == 0) {
      warning(sprintf("[cso_toolkit.dw_use] cols_lenient = TRUE: none of the requested cols matched the file schema for %s (requested %d; schema has %d). Reading all columns.",
                      basename(resolved), length(cols), length(schema_names)),
              call. = FALSE)
      cols <- NULL
    } else {
      cols <- kept
    }
  }
}
```

Already applied + validated in DW-Production at commit `b39cf62` (PR #133). Run #8: NT 2a_agg_prep.R goes from FATAL (any_of error) to OK 794.9s.

## Why this lives in the toolkit, not in sector scripts

The `dw_use(cols = dplyr::any_of(c(...)))` pattern recurs across DW-Production sectors. Making `cols_lenient` first-class means future sector adoption is one keyword arg, not a hand-rolled
`intersect(c(...), names(arrow::open_dataset(p)$schema))` per call site.

## Verification

- Parquet introspection uses `arrow::open_dataset()` metadata-only path (verified: no data payload load for 594K-row CMRS series file)
- CSV / TSV / TXT introspection uses `fread(nrows = 0)` header read
- DTA uses `haven::read_dta(n_max = 0)`
- XLSX uses `readxl::read_xlsx(n_max = 0)`
- Empty-intersection case warns + falls through to all-cols read (no hard fail)
- Schema introspection error case warns + skips lenient pre-filter (no hard fail)

## Cross-references

- DW-Production PR: unicef-drp/DW-Production#133 (`fix/nt-reviewer-mode-blockers-v2`, commit `b39cf62`)
- Predecessor: cso-toolkit#30 (parquet/dta col_select hotfix; must land first because lenient intersect produces a non-empty `cols` vector that the parquet branch must handle correctly)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#32 (provenance sidecars, same milestone)

issues.title: [v0.4.3] dw_use: conditional dispatch for parquet/dta col_select=NULL (NT pipeline blocker)

milestone.title: v0.4.3
milestone.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

issues.body:
# [v0.4.3] dw_use: conditional dispatch for parquet/dta col_select=NULL (NT pipeline blocker)

**Milestone**: `v0.4.3` (Integrity release)
**Labels**: `bug`, `dw_use`, `hotfix`, `nt`
**Type**: Bug fix

## Summary

`dw_use(parquet_path)` with no explicit `cols` returns a **zero-column tibble** instead of all columns. Same pattern affects the `dta` branch. Surfaced by DW-Production NT reviewer-mode pipeline (Run #6, 2026-05-27): 13+ stages failed downstream of 1b with "object 'COLUMN' not found" because the upstream `dw_use(out_dw_nut_country_series_for_agg.parquet)` returned an empty schema.

## Root cause

`r/R/dw_io.R::dw_use()` parquet branch (canonical v0.4.2):

```r
parquet = { .require("arrow"); arrow::read_parquet(resolved, col_select = cols, ...) },
dta     = { .require("haven"); haven::read_dta(resolved, col_select = cols, ...) },
```

When `dw_use(path)` is called without explicit column selection, `cols` defaults to `NULL`. `arrow::read_parquet(path, col_select = NULL)` returns a zero-column schema rather than all columns. `haven::read_dta(path, col_select = NULL)` exhibits the analogous behaviour.

Affected NT call sites (verified):
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2a_agg_prep.R:18` and many others in 2b–2j, 4b
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/00_nt_functions.R:259, 487, 751, 780, 816, 828`

## Fix

Conditional dispatch — only pass `col_select` when non-NULL:

```r
parquet = {
  .require("arrow")
  if (is.null(cols)) arrow::read_parquet(resolved, ...)
  else                arrow::read_parquet(resolved, col_select = cols, ...)
},
dta = {
  .require("haven")
  if (is.null(cols)) haven::read_dta(resolved, ...)
  else                haven::read_dta(resolved, col_select = cols, ...)
},
```

Already applied + validated in DW-Production at commit `8c923fa` (PR #133). Run #7 (after fix): 24/25 stages OK, vs Run #6 (before fix): 10/25 stages OK.

## Verification

- DW-Production NT reviewer-mode pipeline runs end-to-end through 4b → 5b
- Parquet-via-`dw_use` returns full schema by default
- DTA-via-`dw_use` returns full schema by default
- Explicit `cols = c("col1", "col2")` still works (existing behaviour preserved)

## Cross-references

- DW-Production PR: unicef-drp/DW-Production#133 (`fix/nt-reviewer-mode-blockers-v2`)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#TBD (cols_lenient feature, this milestone)
- Companion: cso-toolkit#TBD (provenance sidecars, this milestone)
- Manifest: DW-Production currently carries `dw_io.R` in `local_edits` (commit `8c923fa`); drop the local edit when this issue lands.

issues.title: v0.4.2: dialect="base" honours dispatched separator (fix silent-CSV bug)
issues.body:
## Summary

Patch release fixing a Copilot-flagged silent-CSV bug in v0.4.1's new `dialect = "base"` dispatch on `dw_save()`. Surfaced by Copilot review of [DW-Production#128](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/128) (the v0.4.1 cleanup pull) before any sector pipeline could write a corrupted `.tsv`.

## The bug

v0.4.1 restored a `dialect = c("fwrite", "base")` parameter on `dw_save()`. The `"base"` branch dispatched through `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)`, which **hardcodes a comma separator**. That meant:

```r
dw_save(x, "out.tsv", dialect = "base")
# Wrote comma-separated content into a .tsv file -- silent corruption.
```

Indistinguishable from a real TSV at the filename level, but with wrong delimiters inside.

## The fix

Switch the `dialect = "base"` branch in `.write_csv()` from:

```r
utils::write.csv(x, file = path)
```

…to the equivalent underlying call with the dispatched separator:

```r
utils::write.table(x, file = path,
                   sep = sep,
                   col.names = NA,
                   qmethod = "double")
```

`write.csv` is itself a wrapper around `write.table` with those exact args plus an enforced `sep = ","`. So:

- For `.csv` (sep = `,`) the byte output is **identical** to `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)`. The byte-parity guarantee v0.4.1 introduced for legacy callers (DW-Production NT `2[bcfg]_agg_*` scripts) is preserved.
- For `.tsv` / `.txt` (sep = `\t`) the file now contains actual **tab** separators with the same `row.names` / quoted-string defaults.

## Test coverage

New regression test file `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-dialect.R`:

| Test | Assertion |
|---|---|
| `dialect='base'` on `.csv` | byte-identical to `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)` |
| `dialect='base'` on `.tsv` | output contains tab chars + no commas in data rows (the fix) |
| `dialect='base'` with `compress = TRUE` | envelope-shaped error (gzip is fwrite-only) |
| `dialect='unknown'` | envelope-shaped error from `match.arg()` |

Plus version-stamp assertion bumps in `test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` and `test-dw_publish.R` (`"0.4.1"` → `"0.4.2"`).

## Files changed

- `r/R/dw_io.R` — `.write_csv()` dispatch + `@param dialect` roxygen + header + `dw_toolkit_version()` + `@return` roxygen
- `r/DESCRIPTION` — `Version: 0.4.1 → 0.4.2`
- `NEWS.md` — v0.4.2 entry
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-dialect.R` — NEW (4 regression tests)
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` — version-stamp assertion
- `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R` — version-stamp assertion
- `r/man/dw_save.Rd` — `@param dialect` description
- `r/man/dw_toolkit_version.Rd` — `Currently "0.4.1" → "0.4.2"`

## No public-API change

`dw_save()`'s signature is unchanged. Callers using `dialect = "base"` on `.csv` see no behavioural difference. Callers passing `.tsv` / `.txt` with `dialect = "base"` get correct tab output instead of silent comma output (which was the bug).

## Validation

Locally verified before backport: see DW-Production#128 commit `4a38b07` which applied the same fix and confirmed via focused Rscript checks:

- `.csv` byte-parity: `TRUE` (write.table with col.names=NA + qmethod="double" produces identical output to write.csv)
- `.tsv` contains tabs: `TRUE` (fix addresses the silent CSV-with-TSV-extension bug)

## Cross-references

- Triggering finding: Copilot review on [DW-Production#128](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/128) (commit `0e6d7eb`, line 675)
- Predecessor: [cso-toolkit#24](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/pull/24) (v0.4.1 release; introduced the `dialect` parameter)

issues.title: [v0.4.2] create_sector_script DW wrapper: wrong default paths (011_input/013_output) + nonexistent profile sentinel
issues.body:
## Summary

Two bugs in the DW-Production wrapper inside `create_sector_script.R`, surfaced by Copilot findings #8 and #9 on [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127).

### Bug A — Wrong default paths (`011_input` / `013_output` vs `011_rawdata` / `013_wrkdata`)

The DW-specific wrapper at line 217 generates default paths:

```r
input_dir  <- file.path(teamsFolder, "01_dw_prep", "011_input")
output_dir <- file.path(teamsFolder, "01_dw_prep", "013_output")
```

But DW-Production's repo layout (and the canonical Teams deposit at `060.DW-MASTER/01_dw_prep/`) uses:

```
01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/   <-- not 011_input
01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/   <-- not 013_output
```

Generated sector scripts will point at non-existent directories.

### Bug B — Profile sentinel `profile_DW_Production` doesn't exist

The wrapper at line 215 checks:

```r
if (!exists("profile_DW_Production")) source("profile_DW-Production.R")
```

…but `profile_DW-Production.R` doesn't define any `profile_DW_Production` global. Generated scripts re-source the profile every time even after it's been sourced.

## Suggested fix

### For Bug A — update wrapper defaults

```r
input_dir  <- file.path(teamsFolder, "01_dw_prep", "011_rawdata")
output_dir <- file.path(teamsFolder, "01_dw_prep", "013_wrkdata")
```

### For Bug B — use an existing real sentinel

`profile_DW-Production.R` defines `projectFolder` and `teamsFolder` near the top. Either:

```r
if (!exists("projectFolder")) source("profile_DW-Production.R")
```

…or add an explicit sentinel to the profile:

```r
profile_DW_Production <- TRUE   # at the top of profile_DW-Production.R
```

The former is less invasive (no profile change needed).

## Blast radius for DW-Production

**Zero on current pipelines.** The wrapper is for generating NEW sector scripts; existing sector scripts (NT, IM, WS, HVA, etc.) are not generated by this wrapper. Bug only fires when a sector lead uses the wrapper to scaffold a new sector — at which point they'd notice the broken paths immediately.

Still worth fixing in v0.4.2 so future scaffolding works out of the box.

## Cross-references

- Copilot findings #8 + #9 on DW-Production#127 (commit `12791e8`)
- File on canonical: `r/R/create_sector_script.R`

issues.title: [v0.4.2] dw_regions(): Aggregate column not renamed to caller's value arg; regional rows have NA in value
issues.body:
## Summary

`dw_regions()` appends regional aggregate rows to the caller's country-level tibble but **does not rename** the `Aggregate` column (produced by `aggregate_data_v2()`) back to the caller's requested `value` column. Result: regional rows have country values in `value` and regional values in `Aggregate`, leaving `value` as `NA` for regional rows.

Surfaced by Copilot finding #10 on [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127).

## Reproduction

```r
library(dplyr)
# Hypothetical sector pipeline (not yet used in DW-Production):
country_data <- tibble(
  REF_AREA    = c("AFG", "BGD", "IND"),
  TIME_PERIOD = 2024,
  pop_under5  = c(5e6, 15e6, 120e6)
)
out <- dw_regions(
  country_data,
  value  = "pop_under5",
  by     = "TIME_PERIOD",
  method = "sum"
)

tail(out, 5)
# REF_AREA TIME_PERIOD pop_under5  Aggregate
# AFG      2024        5000000     NA
# BGD      2024        15000000    NA
# IND      2024        120000000   NA
# SAR      2024        NA          140000000   <-- regional row: value NA, Aggregate has the number
# WLD      2024        NA          ...         <-- same
```

## Suggested fix

In `dw_regions()`, after `bind_rows()`-ing the regional output back to the country data, rename `Aggregate` → `value`:

```r
regional <- aggregate_data_v2(country_data, value = value, by = by, method = method, ...)
# Currently:
out <- bind_rows(country_data, regional)
# Should be:
regional <- regional %>% dplyr::rename(!!value := Aggregate)
out <- bind_rows(country_data, regional)
```

## Blast radius for DW-Production

**Zero on current sector scripts.** Per policy confirmed 2026-05-27 (jpazvd), no DW-Production sector currently calls `dw_regions()` / `dw_publish()` / `dw_pop()`. These v0.4.0-shipped helpers are inert until sectors adopt them.

The bug should still be fixed in v0.4.2 BEFORE any sector adopts `dw_regions()`, otherwise the silent NA-fill will corrupt regional aggregates the first time it gets used.

## Cross-references

- Copilot finding #10 on DW-Production#127 (commit `12791e8`)
- Related: cso-toolkit#18 (the issue that landed `dw_regions()` in v0.4.0)

issues.title: [v0.4.2] dw_save: producer-mode self-mirror when teamsWrkData == teamsFolderCanonical emits false Teams-mirror-failed warning
issues.body:
## Summary

Producer-mode `dw_save()` emits a false "Teams mirror failed" warning when the profile resolves `teamsWrkData` (or `teamsRawData` / `teamsMetaData`) directly to `teamsFolderCanonical`. Surfaced by Copilot finding #5 on [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127).

## Reproduction

Default producer-mode profile typically sets:

```r
teamsWrkData         <- teamsFolderCanonical  # same path
teamsWrkDataCanonical <- teamsFolderCanonical
```

When a producer calls:

```r
dw_save(x, file.path(teamsWrkData, "sector", "2025", "out.csv"))
```

…the primary write lands directly at the canonical Teams location. Then:

```r
mirrors <- .dw_remote_mirrors(path)
# teams_mirror == primary_path (because teamsWrkData == teamsFolderCanonical)
# z_mirror      == Z: equivalent
...
# Mirror block:
if (!is.na(teams_mirror)) {
  ok <- file.copy(path, teams_mirror, overwrite = TRUE)
  if (!ok) warning("[cso_toolkit.dw_save] Teams mirror FAILED")
}
```

`file.copy(path, path)` returns `FALSE` (same source + destination), triggering the false-positive warning despite the primary write having succeeded.

## Suggested fix

Treat the canonical primary as already-written-to-Teams:

```r
# In .dw_remote_mirrors:
if (normalizePath(teams_mirror) == normalizePath(primary_path)) {
  teams_mirror <- NA   # primary IS the Teams write; no mirror step needed
}
```

…OR skip the mirror step in the dispatch block when `teams_mirror` normalizes to `path`.

## Blast radius for DW-Production

- Fires on every producer-mode write under the canonical profile. Currently no producer runs are happening from jpazvd (reviewer); will affect Database Managers (@karavan88 / @jmunkhbadar) the moment they run a sector pipeline in producer mode.
- Symptom is cosmetic (warning, not error) — primary write succeeds, only the redundant mirror emits the false-failure message.
- Real failures (e.g., Teams disconnected mid-write) get masked because the false-positive desensitises readers.

## Cross-references

- Copilot finding #5 on DW-Production#127 (commit `12791e8`)
- Related canonical issues: see "dw_save flow ordering" (separate issue)

issues.title: [v0.4.2] dw_save flow ordering: mirror destinations computed before .gz suffix; mirror_to_z dots leak
issues.body:
## Summary

Two interacting flow-ordering bugs in `dw_save()` surfaced by Copilot review of [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127) (findings #4, #6, #7).

### Bug A — Mirror destinations computed before `.gz` suffix

```r
mirrors <- .dw_remote_mirrors(path)     # <-- uses original path
...
# later in the function:
if (isTRUE(compress)) path <- paste0(path, ".gz")
```

When `compress = TRUE`, the primary file is written as `path.gz` but `teams_mirror` / `z_mirror` paths still use the uncompressed `path`. Result:

- Compressed bytes get copied to a misleading filename (no `.gz` extension on the mirror destinations)
- The overwrite check looks for the uncompressed mirror name and misses any existing `.gz` mirror

### Bug B — `mirror_to_z` stripped from `dots`, but writer dispatch still forwards original `...`

```r
if ("mirror_to_z" %in% names(dots)) {
  warning("[cso_toolkit.dw_save] `mirror_to_z` argument is deprecated...")
  dots$mirror_to_z <- NULL
}
...
switch(fmt_for_dispatch,
  csv = .write_csv(x, tmp_path, sep = ",", compress = compress, ...),   # <-- still forwards mirror_to_z if caller passed it
  ...
)
```

Internal callers (e.g. `dw_api_fetch(... mirror_to_z = TRUE)`) pass `mirror_to_z = TRUE` into the format writer, which doesn't accept it → unused-argument error.

## Reproduction

```r
# Bug A
dw_save(x, "out.csv", compress = TRUE)
# Primary lands at out.csv.gz; mirror copies look for out.csv (no .gz)

# Bug B
dw_save(x, "out.csv", mirror_to_z = TRUE)
# warning() fires for the deprecation; then fwrite(...) chokes on mirror_to_z
```

## Suggested fix

1. **Bug A**: finalize `path` (append `.gz`) BEFORE computing `mirrors` and running the overwrite check.

   ```r
   if (isTRUE(compress)) path <- paste0(path, ".gz")
   mirrors <- .dw_remote_mirrors(path)
   ...
   ```

2. **Bug B**: forward sanitized `dots` (not raw `...`) to the format writers. Switch the dispatch to `do.call`:

   ```r
   switch(fmt_for_dispatch,
     csv = do.call(.write_csv,
                   c(list(x = x, path = tmp_path, sep = ",", compress = compress, dialect = dialect), dots)),
     ...
   )
   ```

## Blast radius for DW-Production

Zero on current pipelines:

- No sector currently calls `dw_save(..., compress = TRUE)` (audit pending; if any do, please cross-reference here).
- No sector script passes `mirror_to_z`; the internal `dw_api_fetch` use is the only known caller and gets a deprecation warning today, not an error (because deprecated args are removed from `dots` before they reach the dispatch — except the `...` forwarding leaks them in v0.4.0).

Filed pre-emptively so v0.4.2 catches both.

## Cross-references

- Surfaced via Copilot on DW-Production#127 (commit `12791e8`)
- Related to (but separate from) the dialect/overwrite patches landing in v0.4.1

issues.title: v0.4.1: restore dialect param + lenient overwrite default in dw_save
issues.body:
## Summary

Patch two regressions identified by Copilot review of DW-Production PR [unicef-drp/DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127) (the v0.4.0 pull into NT review). Both patches were validated against the NT branch in DW-Production before this backport — see PR #127 commit `12791e8` and the NT regression test log in PR #127's comment thread.

## Patch A — Restore `dialect` parameter on `dw_save()` / `.write_csv()`

**Regression**: v0.4.0 dropped the `dialect` parameter that v0.3.x exposed for byte-parity with `utils::write.csv()`. DW-Production NT scripts depend on `dialect = "base"` for legacy CSV format preservation:

- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2b_agg_iod.R:52`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2c_agg_vas_series.R:47`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2f_agg_bw.R:52`
- `01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/2g_agg_iycf.R:240`

The NT post-pilot writeup ([`nt/reviews/2026-05-25-jpazvd-pilot/01_post_pilot_writeup.md`](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/blob/review/sector-nt-2026-05-18/01_dw_prep/012_codes/nt/reviews/2026-05-25-jpazvd-pilot/01_post_pilot_writeup.md)) explicitly relies on byte-identical output to `utils::write.csv()`. Under v0.4.0, the `dialect` argument is silently forwarded via `...` into `data.table::fwrite`, which doesn't accept it — sector scripts error or silently lose byte parity.

**Fix**:

- Add `dialect = c("fwrite", "base")` as an explicit parameter on `dw_save()`. Validates via `match.arg()`.
- Plumb `dialect` through the CSV/TSV/TXT dispatch lines to `.write_csv()`.
- In `.write_csv()`, branch on dialect:
  - `"fwrite"` (default) — existing `data.table::fwrite` path
  - `"base"` — `utils::write.csv(x, file = path)` (preserves `row.names = TRUE` + default-quoted strings)
- `dialect = "base"` with `compress = TRUE` raises an explanatory error (gzip is fwrite-only).

## Patch B — Lenient overwrite default for reviewer mode

**Design mismatch**: v0.4.0 shipped `overwrite = FALSE` uniformly across both modes (strict). The closed issue #14 design discussion (jpazvd, 2026-05-27) preferred lenient: producer-mode requires explicit `overwrite = TRUE`; reviewer-mode keeps default-TRUE for scratch writes to local sandbox under `01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/_local/`.

**Rationale**: reviewer sandbox writes are already gitignored and easy to nuke + re-run. Forcing explicit `overwrite = TRUE` there is friction without safety benefit. Producer writes target the canonical Teams + Z: deposit where accidental overwrite is unsafe — explicit acknowledgement remains required.

**Fix**:

- Signature: `overwrite = FALSE` → `overwrite = NULL` (sentinel).
- After `is_reviewer` / `is_producer` detection in `dw_save` body, resolve:
  ```r
  if (is.null(overwrite)) {
    overwrite <- isTRUE(is_reviewer)
    # reviewer  -> TRUE  (scratch is safe to re-run)
    # producer  -> FALSE (must be explicit)
    # mode unset -> FALSE (safe default)
  }
  ```
- Existing `if (!isTRUE(overwrite))` gate at the post-mirror-check unchanged — the NULL resolution above coerces to TRUE/FALSE before it fires.

## Verification

**On DW-Production PR #127** (NT branch with these patches applied):

- `Rscript -e "parse('00_functions/dw_io.R')"` → SYNTAX OK
- Focused unit tests in `tests/test_v041_nt.R`:
  - `.cso_require()` defined after zzz.R vendor (DW-Production-side, not relevant here)
  - `dw_save(x, p, dialect = "base")` produces byte-identical output to `utils::write.csv(x, p)` ✓
  - `dw_save(x, p)` reviewer mode → default overwrite resolves to TRUE; re-run against existing file succeeds silently ✓
  - `dw_toolkit_version()` returns `"0.4.0"` (will be `"0.4.1"` after this PR merges + tag) ✓
- NT active conductor end-to-end run (`0_execute_conductor.R`) with patched toolkit: [log to be posted on PR #127 once the background run completes; will cross-reference here]

## What's NOT in scope for this PR

Three additional Copilot findings on DW-Production#127 are real canonical bugs but deferred to separate issues — they have zero blast radius on current DW-Production sector scripts because no sector currently calls `dw_regions()` / `dw_publish()` / `dw_pop()` (the wrappers are inert).

Filed as separate issues:

- `dw_save` flow ordering: mirror destinations computed before `.gz` suffix; `mirror_to_z` stripped from `dots` but `...` still forwards original.
- `dw_save` producer self-mirror when `teamsWrkData == teamsFolderCanonical`.
- `dw_regions` `Aggregate` column not renamed to caller's `value` arg.
- `create_sector_script` DW wrapper: wrong default paths + missing profile sentinel.

These would land in a v0.4.2 cleanup release.

## Migration notes for v0.4.1 changelog

- **`dw_save(..., dialect = ...)` parameter restored.** Sector scripts that depend on byte-parity with `utils::write.csv()` (e.g., DW-Production NT 2[bcfg]_agg_*) can pass `dialect = "base"` again. The default remains `"fwrite"` (`data.table::fwrite`).
- **`dw_save()` overwrite default is now mode-aware.** Reviewer mode keeps default-TRUE (scratch repo writes are safe to re-run). Producer mode keeps default-FALSE introduced in v0.4.0 (explicit `overwrite = TRUE` required). Pass `overwrite = NULL` to use the mode default; pass `overwrite = TRUE` / `FALSE` explicitly to override.

## Cross-references

- Triggering review: [DW-Production#127](https://github.com/unicef-drp/DW-Production/pull/127) (Copilot findings #2 + #3)
- Design discussion: cso-toolkit#14 (closed; mode contract)
- NT regression test: `tests/test_v041_nt.R` in DW-Production PR #127

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: release: v0.4.0 — mode contract + Stata parity + demographics + dw_publish STUB
issues.body:
## v0.4.0 release

Brings every PR merged into `develop` since v0.3.0 (2026-05-25) to `main` and tags `v0.4.0`. Five housekeeping commits flip the version stamps + roadmap.

## Substantive PRs landed since v0.3.0

| PR | Issue | Title |
|---|---|---|
| #11 | — | DW-Production alignment audit (B1–B4 backports) |
| #12 | — | testthat regression suite + GitHub Actions CI |
| #16 | — | README overhaul + bidirectional cross-linking |
| #19 | #14 | feat(r+py): producer/reviewer mode contract tightening (**BREAKING**) |
| #20 | #5 | feat(stata): `dw_use` + `dw_require_no_api` + `dw_load_config` |
| #21 | #17 + #18 | feat(r): `dw_pop()` + `dw_regions()` |
| #22 | #15 | feat(r): `dw_publish()` STUB (dry-run only) |

## Release-housekeeping commits (this PR)

1. `17d9728` chore(r): bump csotoolkit Version to 0.4.0 (2026-05-26)
2. `d8ce404` chore(py): bump cso-toolkit Python package to 0.4.0
3. `0393219` chore(templates): bump .toolkit_manifest.yml to v0.4.0
4. `a31c775` docs(news): promote Unreleased → v0.4.0 (2026-05-26)
5. `66e0615` docs(readme): roadmap table — v0.4.0 in flight → released

## Breaking change (one)

`dw_save(overwrite = ...)` default flipped **TRUE → FALSE** in v0.4.0. The overwrite check now examines ALL destinations that will be written (primary, Teams mirror, Z: mirror) and refuses if any of them already exists. Pass `overwrite = TRUE` to restore v0.3.0 behaviour. See migration notes in `NEWS.md` under issue #14.

## Verification

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → **245 / 0**
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes** (banner: `csotoolkit 0.4.0`)
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` → **all 34 invariants pass**
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` → **30 / 0**
- [x] `dw_toolkit_version()` (R) and `cso_toolkit.dw_toolkit_version()` + `cso_toolkit.__version__` (Python) all return `"0.4.0"`.

## Post-merge actions

1. Sync develop with main (back-merge of the version-bump commits).
2. `gh release create v0.4.0 --target main --title "v0.4.0 ..." --notes-file NEWS-v0.4.0-extract.md` (avoiding local `git push --tags` OOM per repo convention).

## Plan position

- ✅ **PR-B** (#19) — `feat(r+py): #14`
- ✅ **PR-C** (#20) — `feat(stata): #5`
- ✅ **PR-D** (#21) — `feat(r): #17 + #18`
- ✅ **PR-E** (#22) — `feat(r): #15 STUB`
- ⏭ **Release PR** (this PR) — version bumps + tag v0.4.0

What's *not* in v0.4.0 (deferred to v0.5.0):

- Live `dw_publish()` submission branch (issue #15 stays open).
- Python + Stata siblings of `dw_pop()` / `dw_regions()` (issue #18 stays open per user decision).

issues.title: feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub — dry-run only, live submission deferred to v0.5.0
issues.body:
## Summary

Lands `dw_publish()` as a **deliberate STUB** (issue #15). Ships the public signature + dry-run validation now so DW-Production sector scripts can wire the canonical Helix-submission call site today, then have the live branch light up automatically when v0.5.0 lands.

This PR is **independent of every other v0.4.0 PR** — pure R addition, no changes to existing helpers.

## Scope boundary (folded into the helper's roxygen + body)

The long-running DW-Production confusion is finally explicit:

| Helper | Surface | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_save()` | Filesystem | Teams folder + Z: drive mirror |
| `dw_publish()` (this PR) | API | Helix submission only |

## What's in (v0.4.0)

- Public signature matching the v0.5.0 contract:
  ```r
  dw_publish(path, indicator, vintage, sector,
             endpoint = "helix", dry_run = TRUE, ...)
  ```
  Note `dry_run = TRUE` is the v0.4.0 default; live branch is gated.
- **Producer-only mode contract** — reviewer-mode calls raise BEFORE any I/O via the same envelope shape as `dw_api_fetch()`.
- **Argument validation** — empty / missing `path` / `indicator` / `vintage` / `sector` raises the envelope; `path` must exist on disk and not be a directory; `endpoint` must be `"helix"`.
- **`dry_run = TRUE` returns a validated payload** with `sha256`, `bytes`, `built_at`, `built_by`, and the toolkit-version stamp. Caller scripts can assert the payload shape today without ever hitting the network.

## What's deferred to v0.5.0

- Live submission (`dry_run = FALSE`) raises with the envelope-shaped *"Live submission not yet implemented"* message and a pointer to GitHub issue #15. Real Helix endpoint integration ships when sector leads (@karavan88, @sbrar29, @laurenfrancis1202) finalise the submission contract.

## Atomic commits

1. `<feat1>` feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub
2. `<test1>` test(r): #15 dw_publish() regression suite
3. `<docs1>` docs(news): #15 v0.4.0 dw_publish() STUB entry

## Test plan

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → **235 pass / 0 fail** (was 196 + 6 new asserts that exercise mode lockout, validation, dry-run payload, and the live-submission stop)
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` → clean regeneration with new `dw_publish.Rd`
- [x] Tests assert: reviewer-mode raises BEFORE any path-existence check; dry-run payload has every documented key; `dry_run = FALSE` envelope includes the `issues/15` pointer.

## Files added

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| R source | `r/R/dw_publish.R` |
| R generated | `r/NAMESPACE` (+ `dw_publish` export), `r/man/dw_publish.Rd` (+ trivial `@seealso` updates in 5 sibling Rd files) |
| R tests | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_publish.R` |
| Release notes | `NEWS.md` |

## Plan position

- ✅ **PR-B** (#19) — `feat(r+py): #14`
- ✅ **PR-C** (#20) — `feat(stata): #5`
- ✅ **PR-D** (#21) — `feat(r): #17 + #18`
- ⏭ **PR-E** (this PR) — `feat(r): #15 dw_publish() stub`
- ⏭ **Release PR** — version bumps + tag `v0.4.0`

issues.title: feat(r): #17 + #18 dw_pop() + dw_regions() — population denominators + UNICEF regional aggregates
issues.body:
## Summary

Closes #17 and #18. Adds two convenience wrappers that almost every sector pipeline needs but that v0.3.0 made users write themselves. **R-only in v0.4.0**; Python + Stata parity tracked at the same issues.

This PR is **independent of PR-B (#19) and PR-C (#20)** — pure R additions, no source changes to existing helpers.

## What's new

| Helper | Wraps | Default behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_pop(year, indicator, countries, refresh, cache_key)` | `dw_api_fetch(api = "wb", indicator = "SP.POP.TOTL")` | Latest year per country; tidy `(REF_AREA, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE)` tibble |
| `dw_regions(data, value, weight, method, taxonomy, by, refresh_metadata)` | `dw_api_fetch(api = "github_raw", owner_repo = "unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata", path = "<taxonomy>.csv")` + `aggregate_data_v2()` | Population-weighted means (via `dw_pop()`); appends regional rows to input |

issues.title: feat(stata): #5 v0.4.0 — dw_use + dw_require_no_api + dw_load_config
issues.body:
## Summary

Closes #5. Ships the three Stata helpers that complete the v0.4.0 producer / reviewer mode contract on the Stata side. The Stata sibling now has the full read + mode-contract surface for `.dta` / `.csv` / `.xlsx`; Parquet / RDS / `dw_api_fetch` remain R + Python only by design.

This PR is **independent of PR-B (#19)** — pure Stata + docs, no R / Python source changes. Mergeable in either order.

## What's new

| Helper | Purpose | Mirrors |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_use.ado` + `.sthlp` | Stata read with v0.4.0 mode-branched resolver (producer = local-first; reviewer = network-first Teams → Z: → local + warning → stop) + `.provenance.json` parse + non-blocking Z: integrity check (`size` default, `sha256` opt-in) | `r/R/dw_io.R::dw_use`, `python/src/dw_io.py::dw_use` |
| `dw_require_no_api.ado` + `.sthlp` | Reviewer-mode no-API gate. `$dw_mode == "reviewer"` → `error 459` with envelope-shaped message; any other mode is a silent pass-through | `r/R/profile_helpers.R::dw_require_no_api` |
| `dw_load_config.ado` + `.sthlp` | Hand-rolled YAML reader for `~/.config/user_config.yml` (no external dep → AppLocker-safe). Sets `$dw_mode` + the `teams*` and `sandboxRoot` globals; hard-stops if `dw_mode` is missing or invalid | R `profile_helpers.R` validation |

## Atomic commits

1. `0ec11bf` feat(stata): #5 dw_require_no_api.ado
2. `7efe5d3` feat(stata): #5 dw_load_config.ado
3. `eb2ba19` feat(stata): #5 dw_use.ado (the big one)
4. `fb40cbe` docs(stata): #5 update READMEs for v0.4.0 helper set
5. `b3a9086` docs(news): #5 closure entry under v0.4.0

## Out of scope (by design)

- **No Stata `dw_api_fetch`.** Reviewer sessions are blocked by `dw_require_no_api`; producer pipelines wanting Stata-native HTTP should call R / Python and hand back the cache via `dw_use`.
- **Stata-side `.parquet` / `.rds` reads.** Auto-dispatch covers `.dta` / `.csv` / `.xlsx` only — route through R or Python and `dw_save()` to `.dta` for Stata consumption.
- **`datasignature` content hash, not file-level SHA-256.** Same as v0.3.0 — Stata-native and AppLocker-safe; cross-tool integrity checks compare content hashes, not file hashes.

- [x] No CI changes — the existing R-CMD-check workflow continues to gate the R package; Stata has no CI step.
