issues.title: feat(r+py): #14 v0.4.0 producer/reviewer mode contract tightening
issues.body:
## Summary

Closes #14. Tightens the producer / reviewer mode contract so producer outputs are provably redundant and reviewer reads are provably canonical. R + Python siblings ship in lock-step.

**BREAKING:** `dw_save(overwrite = ...)` default flipped TRUE → FALSE. See "Migration" below.

## Behaviour matrix (v0.4.0)

| Aspect | Producer session | Reviewer session |
|--------|------------------|------------------|
| `dw_save` write | Fans out primary + Teams mirror + Z: mirror; hard-stops if neither mirror is configured | `PermissionError` on writes under canonical **OR** Z: drive paths (was canonical-only) |
| `dw_use` read | Local-first; falls back to canonical when missing (v0.3.0 preserved) | Network-first: Teams → Z: → repo-local (with envelope-shaped provenance warning) → hard-stop |
| Overwrite gate | `overwrite = FALSE` is the new default; checks all destinations that will be written | Same — but reviewer writes are sandbox-only so only the primary destination counts |
| `mirror_to_z` keyword | Silently dropped with a deprecation warning (Z: mirror is now automatic) | Same |
| `dw_toolkit_version()` | Returns `"0.4.0"` | Returns `"0.4.0"` |

## Atomic commits

1. `5a9e660` feat(r): #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io.R
2. `221becc` test(r): regression suite for #14 v0.4.0 mode contract
3. `8e89c9a` feat(py): #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io.py
4. `bd8ed61` test(py): #14 smoke + envelope updates for v0.4.0
5. `cb82384` docs: #14 v0.4.0 mode contract in dw_io references
6. `5d8c0be` docs(news): #14 v0.4.0 BREAKING migration notes

## Migration (breaking change)

Existing producer callers that relied on the v0.3.0 silent re-write semantics must either:

1. **Pass `overwrite = TRUE` explicitly** when overwriting an existing deposit. Common path for daily re-runs of the same vintage.
2. **Or sequence the write under a fresh vintage subfolder** so no prior deposit collides. Recommended pattern for archival work.

Reviewer-mode callers do not need changes — the new network-first read order is transparent when Teams/Z: are reachable; the new warning surfaces only when they are not (which used to be a silent provenance gap).

## Test plan

- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::test('.')"` from `r/` → 161 pass / 0 fail (was 125 + 36 new mode-contract assertions)
- [x] `Rscript -e "devtools::check('.', args=c('--no-manual','--no-build-vignettes'))"` → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` → **All smoke checks passed** (34 invariants; +9 for #14)
- [x] `python python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` → **30 passed, 0 failed** (envelope shape preserved on every new raise)
- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` → clean regeneration
- [x] R + Python paths exercise the same 9 verification cases from issue #14's body

## Files touched

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| R source | `r/R/dw_io.R` |
| R generated | `r/NAMESPACE`, `r/man/*.Rd` (9 modified + `dw_toolkit_version.Rd` new) |
| R tests | `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_io-mode-contract.R` (new) |
| Python source | `python/src/__init__.py`, `python/src/dw_io.py` |
| Python tests | `python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py`, `python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` |
| Docs | `docs/dw_io_reference.md`, `docs/dw_io_python_reference.md` |
| Release notes | `NEWS.md` |

## Out of scope

- **Issue #5** (Stata helpers `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do`) → PR-C
- **Issues #17 + #18** (R `dw_pop()` + `dw_regions()`) → PR-D

issues.title: [v0.4.0] dw_regions() — UNICEF regional aggregates
issues.body:
## Summary

Add `dw_regions()` to `r/R/dw_regions.R` — joins a country-level
tibble against the UNICEF region taxonomy
(`unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata`,
`UNICEF_REP_REG_GLOBAL.csv` by default) and computes regional
aggregates via `aggregate_data_v2()`.

## Scope (in)

- R helper at `r/R/dw_regions.R` with Roxygen + `@family demographics`.
- Default weight: population from `dw_pop()` companion (#17).
- testthat regression tests at `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_regions.R`.
- NEWS.md `Unreleased` entry.

## Scope (out — TODO follow-ups)

- Python sibling at `python/src/dw_regions.py` (post-v0.4.0).
- Stata sibling at `stata/src/dw_regions.ado` (post-v0.4.0).

## Signature draft

```r
dw_regions(data,                                 # country-level tibble with REF_AREA
           value,                                # value column name
           weight = "population",                # "population" pulls from dw_pop() automatically
           method = "weighted_mean",
           taxonomy = "UNICEF_REP_REG_GLOBAL",   # which UNICEF region grouping
           by = c("INDICATOR", "TIME_PERIOD"),   # additional grouping (besides region)
           refresh_metadata = FALSE)
# returns: input tibble + regional aggregate rows appended
#          (regional aggregate REF_AREA = region code, e.g. "SSA")
```

## Implementation outline

1. Fetch region taxonomy via
   `dw_api_fetch(api = "github_raw", owner_repo =
   "unicef-drp/Country-and-Region-Metadata", path = "<taxonomy>.csv",
   cache_key = paste0("regions_", tolower(taxonomy)))`.
2. If `weight = "population"`, call `dw_pop()` (#17) to fetch
   denominators; join on `REF_AREA` + `TIME_PERIOD`.
3. Inner-join `data` to the region taxonomy on `REF_AREA`.
4. Call `aggregate_data_v2()` per region; concatenate result rows
   to the input.

## Verification

- Country-level input + regions taxonomy → output has additional
  rows for each region (UNICEF Global has SSA, WCA, LAC, MENA, ESA,
  EAP, EECA + Global = at least 7 regional rows per `(by, region)`
  combo).
- Coverage stats (countries reporting / countries in region) carried
  through via `aggregate_data_v2()` metadata columns.
- Reviewer-mode call with empty region-metadata cache raises the
  standard envelope.
- Default-weight path uses `dw_pop()`; explicit-weight path bypasses
  the dw_pop call.

## Companion

- Builds on #17 (`dw_pop()`).

## Milestone

`v0.4.0` (R only this release; Python + Stata parity tracked as
follow-ups).

issues.title: [v0.4.0] dw_pop() — convenience wrapper for latest population
issues.body:
## Summary

Add `dw_pop()` to `r/R/dw_pop.R` as a thin wrapper around
`dw_api_fetch(api = "wb", ...)` for the canonical population
indicator (`SP.POP.TOTL`).  Used by `dw_regions()` (companion issue)
and any sector that needs population denominators for weighted
aggregation.

## Scope (in)

- R helper at `r/R/dw_pop.R` with Roxygen + `@family demographics`.
- testthat regression tests at `r/tests/testthat/test-dw_pop.R`.
- NEWS.md `Unreleased` entry.

## Scope (out — TODO follow-ups)

- Python sibling at `python/src/dw_pop.py` (post-v0.4.0).
- Stata sibling at `stata/src/dw_pop.ado` (post-v0.4.0).

## Signature draft

```r
dw_pop(year = NULL,                          # NULL = latest per country
       indicator = "SP.POP.TOTL",
       countries = NULL,                     # NULL = all UNICEF M49
       refresh = FALSE,
       cache_key = "wb_population_sp_pop_totl")
# returns: tibble(REF_AREA, TIME_PERIOD, OBS_VALUE)
```

## Verification

- Default call returns ≥ 195 countries × ≥ 5 years.
- `year = 2023` returns exactly one year per country.
- `refresh = TRUE` re-hits the World Bank API (producer mode only).
- Reviewer-mode call with empty cache raises the standard envelope
  (`[cso_toolkit.dw_api] Reviewer mode forbids live API calls.`).

## Companion

- Companion issue for the regional-aggregate consumer that uses this
  helper as the default weight column.

## Milestone

`v0.4.0` (R only this release; Python + Stata parity tracked as
follow-ups).

issues.title: docs: overhaul top-level README + bidirectional README + NEWS cross-linking
issues.body:
## Summary

Two doc improvements bundled into one PR.

### Part A — Top-level `README.md` overhaul

Replaces the prior README's flat 28-row file table with a richer
structure designed for first-time readers:

- **Elevator pitch + badges above the fold** (R CMD check status,
  license, tag).
- **TL;DR** (4-bullet pitch) + **Table of contents**.
- **`Architecture at a glance`** with an embedded **Mermaid flowchart**:
  external sources → toolkit families → language siblings → consumer
  repos → canonical deposit. Renders inline on GitHub.
- **`Three roles, one contract`** with a second **Mermaid diagram** of
  the producer/reviewer/ingestor workflow + comparison table.
- **`What's inside — by capability`** replacing the 28-row file table
  with **seven function-family groupings** (IO contract, Cached
  external APIs, Aggregation + survey weights, Project scaffolding,
  Contract auditing, Vintage sync, Graceful error envelopes). Each
  group has an R / Python / Stata coverage matrix.
- **`Quick start`** with side-by-side R / Python / Stata hello-world
  snippets, each linking to the language-root README.
- **`Versioning and roadmap`** table refreshed (`v0.1.0-rc1` →
  `v0.2.0` → `v0.3.0` → `v0.4.0` in-flight → `v1.0.0` commitment).
- **`Testing and CI`** section listing the layered test surface
  (testthat 125 PASS, R CMD check, Python smoke + envelope, GH Actions
  matrix).
- **Citation** block updated from `v0.1.0-rc1` to `v0.3.0`.
- **`See also`** footer linking to every language README + per-helper
  catalogue + docs.

### Part B — Bidirectional README + Changelog cross-linking

Every README in the repo now reaches `NEWS.md` and every other README
in at most one hop:

| README | Now also links to |
|---|---|
| `r/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `python/README.md`, `stata/README.md` |
| `python/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `r/README.md`, `stata/README.md` |
| `stata/README.md` | `NEWS.md`, `r/README.md`, `python/README.md` |
| `r/R/README.md` | top-level, `r/README.md`, `NEWS.md`, sibling per-helper catalogues |
| `python/src/README.md` | same shape as above |
| `stata/src/README.md` | same shape + links its v0.2 known-limitations to issue #5 |
| Top-level `README.md` | already had every link; added a prominent **"Changelog" paragraph** clarifying that `NEWS.md` IS the changelog (R-ecosystem convention; no separate `CHANGELOG.md`) |

### Why this matters

The previous top-level README was a "what's in the box" inventory.
This version is a **README for a first-time reader**: it answers
"what is this", "who is it for", "how do the pieces fit together",
"what does it look like to use", and "where do I read more"
before any inventory table appears.

### Test plan

- [x] Mermaid syntax validates (`flowchart LR` + `flowchart LR` blocks
  render on github.com preview)
- [x] No code changes
- [x] R CMD check, Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged
  (0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes; 20/20; 30/30)
- [x] Every README → NEWS.md path verified manually

### Out of scope (for follow-ups)

- Issue #14 (v0.4.0 producer/reviewer mode-contract tightening on
  `dw_save` + `dw_use`) — separate PR.
- Issue #5 (Stata `dw_use.ado` + `dw_require_no_api.do` +
  `dw_load_config.do`) — separate PR.
- Issue #15 (`dw_publish` — Helix API submission) — marked `[future]`
  in the issue body; may slip to v0.5.0.

issues.title: [future] Create dw_publish — API-only submission to data warehouse / Helix platform

milestone.title: v0.5.0
milestone.description: Live dw_publish() submission branch (#15) + dw_regions() API redesign (#40)

issues.body:
## Summary

The toolkit currently has no first-class function for promoting a warehouse artifact from "deposited on Teams/Z:" to "submitted to the Helix data platform via API". Several DW-Production sector scripts (and outdated comments referenced in the v4 `dw_save` plan) describe `dw_publish` as if it existed, but at canonical `v0.3.0` it does not. This issue creates `dw_publish` cleanly, with explicit scope boundaries against `dw_save` (companion Issue A).

**Milestone**: TBD (post-`v0.4.0`)
**Labels suggested**: `enhancement`, `api`, `helix`, `producer-mode`

## Scope (in)

- HTTP / SDMX submission to UNICEF Helix endpoints
- Authentication handling (token sourcing from `dw_apis_allowed`-gated config)
- Mode contract: **producer-only** (reviewer mode forbids API submission, same logic as `dw_api_fetch`)
- Pre-flight validation: target file exists locally; schema matches the indicator-pack contract; vintage stamp present
- Post-submission: provenance sidecar gets `published_at`, `published_by`, `helix_submission_id` fields
- Idempotency: re-submitting the same `(indicator, vintage, sha256)` triple returns the existing submission ID, not a new one

## Scope (out)

- **Filesystem copies to Teams folder or Z: drive** — those belong to `dw_save` (see Issue A)
- Helix-side ingestion / staging logic — out of toolkit scope
- Reviewer-mode access — explicit `stop()` with the same rationale as `dw_api_fetch`

## Proposed signature (draft, for discussion)

```r
dw_publish(path,                    # local path to the artifact to publish
           indicator,               # indicator code (e.g., "U5MR")
           vintage,                 # vintage tag (e.g., "2025")
           sector,                  # sector code (e.g., "hva")
           endpoint = "helix",      # one of: "helix" (default) | future endpoints
           dry_run = FALSE,         # if TRUE, validates + returns submission payload without sending
           ...                      # passed to underlying httr2 request
           ) -> list(submission_id, status, response_body, idempotent)
```

## Verification

1. **Dry-run mode** validates schema + auth + endpoint reachability without submitting.
2. **Live submission** returns a Helix submission ID; provenance sidecar updated.
3. **Idempotency**: re-submission with same `(indicator, vintage, sha256)` returns the existing ID.
4. **Reviewer-mode call** `stop()`s before any network attempt.
5. **Wrong schema** → `stop()` with diff against expected.

## Documentation tasks

- Update sector-script header comments (DW-Production) that currently describe `dw_publish` as a "promote local to canonical Teams" step — that's `dw_save` (Issue A), NOT `dw_publish`.
- Add a section to `r/vignettes/dw_publish.Rmd` explaining the scope boundary vs `dw_save`.
- Update `r/vignettes/dw_api.Rmd` to cross-reference `dw_publish` for the submission direction.

## Cross-references

- **Predecessor**: Issue A (`dw_save` producer-mode contract — must land first; `dw_publish` builds on the same mode-contract scaffolding `dw_save` uses)
- Full plan: `~/.claude/plans/fluffy-greeting-pretzel.md` (v4 section, "Scope boundary" table)
- Inspired by: user clarification 2026-05-26 — *"dw_publish should be only to submit data via API not in network paths such as Teams folder or Z drive"*

cc @karavan88 @sbrar29 @laurenfrancis1202 — sector input needed on the producer-side promotion workflow you each use today, so the `dw_publish` signature/idempotency model matches your real-world Helix submission flow.

issues.title: [v0.4.0] producer/reviewer mode contract — dw_save + dw_use (network-first reads, local-only reviewer writes, redundant producer writes, explicit overwrite)
issues.body:
## Summary

The current `dw_save` and `dw_use` (canonical `v0.3.0`) do not enforce a fully-symmetric producer/reviewer mode contract:

- `dw_save` writes to ONE primary destination per call; Z: mirroring only fires when the primary write IS canonical Teams.
- `dw_use` reads with a "local-first, canonical-fallback" pattern — but for **reviewers**, this lets a stale repo-local cache mask the canonical artifact, which silently breaks provenance.

This issue tightens BOTH sides of the contract for `v0.4.0`. The full unified rule:

| Reviewer-mode operation | Allowed | Forbidden behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| Read from Teams/Z: (network) | Preferred (network-first resolution) | — |
| Read from repo-local (cached) | Fallback only, with `warning()` flagging provenance gap | — |
| Read via API call | NO | `stop()` with mode-lock message |
| Write to repo-local | YES | — |
| Write to Teams/Z: (network) | NO | `stop()` with explanatory warning |
| Write via API call (`dw_publish` — Issue B) | NO | `stop()` with mode-lock message |

| Producer-mode operation | Allowed | Forbidden / Required |
|---|---|---|
| Write to local (repo) | Always (primary destination) | — |
| Mirror to Z: drive | If mounted + writable | — |
| Mirror to Teams folder | If mounted + writable | — |
| Both Z: + Teams unavailable | NO | `stop()` — DBM must map at least one |
| Overwrite existing artifact | NO default | Requires explicit `overwrite = TRUE` |

**Milestone**: `v0.4.0` (to be created)
**Labels suggested**: `enhancement`, `breaking-change`, `producer-mode`, `reviewer-mode`, `safety`

## Target producer-mode behaviour — `dw_save`

When `dw_mode == "producer"` and a sector script calls `dw_save(x, primary_path, ...)`:

1. **Always write to local** — `primary_path` interpreted as canonical local path (under `dwWrkData` / `dwRawData` / `dwMetaData`, repo-rooted).
2. **Mirror to all available remote destinations** — write to BOTH Z: and Teams when both are available ("preferably both but at least one of the two" per user spec).
3. **Hard-stop if neither remote available** — `stop()` with:
   > "Producer-mode `dw_save` requires at least one of Z: (preferred) or Teams to be mounted/writable. Currently neither is available on this machine. Please map at least one — preferably both. See profile_DW-Production.R Z: mount instructions."
4. **Explicit overwrite required** — if any of the resolved destinations already has a file at the target path AND `overwrite` was NOT explicitly passed as `TRUE`, `stop()` with:
   > "Producer-mode dw_save: file exists at <path(s)>; pass `overwrite = TRUE` to confirm intentional overwrite."

## Target reviewer-mode behaviour — `dw_save`

- **Local-only writes** (repo path).
- Mode lock refuses any path resolving under `teamsFolderCanonical/...`, Z: mirror, or any other network destination.
- Refusal message is a `stop()` with explanatory warning:
  > "Reviewer-mode dw_save: refused to write to '<path>' — reviewers can only write to the repo-local drive. Network destinations (Teams folder, Z: drive) are reserved for producer mode. If you intended to publish this artifact, switch to producer mode (`dw_mode: 'producer'` in user_config.yml) and re-run, or hand off to a Database Manager."
- `overwrite = TRUE` remains the default for local repo writes (scratch — safe to re-run).

## Target reviewer-mode behaviour — `dw_use` (NEW in this issue)

Per user spec 2026-05-26: "in reviewer files should be pulled from network first; and can only be saved in the local drive (repo folder); anything else should give an error message with a warning message to explain it".

1. **Network-first resolution** — when a reviewer calls `dw_use(path)`, the resolver tries the canonical Teams path FIRST (then Z: if mounted as a sibling location), not the repo-local copy. Rationale: reviewers must test against the producer deposited artifact, not a stale local cache.
2. **Local fallback only with explicit warning** — if neither Teams nor Z: is mounted, fall back to the repo-local copy with a non-fatal `warning()`:
   > "Reviewer-mode dw_use: canonical network paths unavailable; reading from repo-local copy at <path>. This breaks provenance — re-mount Teams/Z: before relying on this output."
3. **Hard-stop if the file is nowhere** — if the file is not on Teams, not on Z:, AND not in the repo, `stop()` with:
   > "Reviewer-mode dw_use: file '<name>' not found on Teams, Z:, or repo. Producer has not deposited this artifact yet, or your network mount is missing. Contact the sector producer."
4. **API calls remain forbidden** — `dw_use` MUST NOT fall through to a `dw_api_fetch()` in reviewer mode. Unchanged from the pre-existing contract: API access is producer-only (per `dw_apis_allowed` gate). A reviewer-mode call that reaches the API path is a contract violation — `stop()` with the existing API-mode-lock message.

## Target producer-mode behaviour — `dw_use`

- **Network-first resolution** (same as reviewer mode for symmetry of provenance).
- Local fallback allowed without warning (producer is the one who deposited it; reading from local cache while preparing the next deposit is normal).
- `dw_api_fetch()` fall-through allowed (producer can refresh from API when `dw_apis_allowed == TRUE`).

## Scope boundary — `dw_save` vs `dw_publish`

| Function | Responsibility | Destinations |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_save` (this issue) | Filesystem writes, mode-aware | Local repo + Teams folder + Z: drive |
| `dw_publish` (companion Issue B) | API submissions to data warehouse / Helix platform | HTTP / SDMX endpoints — **NOT** for filesystem copies |

## Key design decision — primary path is ALWAYS local

The producer-mode `.dw_root_for` mode-aware refactor (DW-Production local edit that resolves `dw_root("wrk")` to `teamsWrkDataCanonical` in producer mode) is **rolled back** as part of this work: `dw_root("wrk")` returns `dwWrkData` (repo-local) in BOTH modes. The producer-mode network mirroring happens INSIDE `dw_save`, not via path resolution.

This decouples concerns:

- Sector script: "write this artifact to the canonical local position for this sector x vintage" — one path, no mode awareness needed.
- Toolkit: "given the producer mode contract, also write the same artifact to Z: and Teams as redundant copies".

## Critical files (against canonical `v0.3.0` base — line numbers may shift)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `.dw_root_for` | Drop mode-aware path rewriting if present in 0.3.0; always return repo-rooted globals |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `.dw_z_mirror_path` | Generalise: given a primary repo path, derive the equivalent Z: path |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` new `.dw_teams_mirror_path` | New helper (repo->Teams suffix swap), analogous to `.dw_z_mirror_path` |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `dw_save` signature | Adjust `mirror_to_z` semantics (likely rename to `mirror_to_remote` or remove and make it automatic in producer mode) |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `dw_save` mode-lock block | Keep reviewer-mode refusal of canonical paths; add producer-mode "at least one remote available" pre-flight |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `dw_save` overwrite check | Refactor to check ALL resolved destinations (local + Teams + Z:); require explicit `overwrite = TRUE` in producer mode |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `dw_save` Z: mirror block | Replace with producer-mode mirror block writing to BOTH Teams and Z: (whichever available) |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` `dw_use` resolver | **NEW**: flip from local-first to network-first in reviewer mode; add `warning()` on local-cache fallback; add `stop()` on file-nowhere |
| `r/R/dw_io.R` header | Add version stamp `Toolkit version: 0.4.0` |
| New `dw_toolkit_version()` constant | Returns `"0.4.0"`; lets sector scripts assert minimum version |

## Verification (canonical CI + DW-Production validation harness)

**`dw_save`:**

1. **Reviewer-mode regression** (DW-Production HVA + IM scripts) — output unchanged vs v0.3.0 baseline; canonical Teams mtime untouched.
2. **Reviewer-mode write refusal** — direct call `dw_save(x, teamsFolderCanonical/foo.csv)` from reviewer session -> `stop()` with the explanatory warning verbatim.
3. **Producer-mode happy path** (Z: + Teams both available) — output in 3 places, byte-identical.
4. **Producer-mode degraded** (Z: unmounted) — output in 2 places + non-blocking warning.
5. **Producer-mode hard-stop** (neither available) — `stop()` with actionable error.
6. **Overwrite safety** — second producer-mode run `stop()`s without `overwrite = TRUE`; succeeds with it.

**`dw_use`:**

7. **Reviewer-mode network-first** — with both Teams and repo-local copies present (and differing), `dw_use(name)` returns the Teams contents (verified by sha256), not the local. Local-cache divergence is the test surface.
8. **Reviewer-mode local-fallback warning** — unmount Teams/Z:, call `dw_use(name)` with file in repo only -> returns content + emits `warning()` containing "provenance" verbatim.
9. **Reviewer-mode file-nowhere stop** — unmount Teams/Z:, file not in repo -> `stop()` with "Contact the sector producer".
10. **Reviewer-mode API fall-through blocked** — temporarily flip `dw_apis_allowed = TRUE` in a reviewer session and verify `dw_use` STILL refuses to fall through to `dw_api_fetch`; the API path is producer-gated regardless.

**Version stamp:**

11. **Version-stamp triangulation** — `head -n 10 dw_io.R` shows `Toolkit version: 0.4.0`; `.toolkit_manifest.yml::pulled_version` agrees; `dw_toolkit_version()` returns the same string.

## Breaking-change notes

- **Producer mode**: `overwrite` default flips from `TRUE` to `FALSE`. Existing producer workflows that re-run pipelines against pre-existing files will need to add `overwrite = TRUE` to call sites.
- **Reviewer mode**: `dw_use` resolution order flips from local-first to network-first. Reviewers on a fast network see no functional change; reviewers on a slow network or with stale local caches will notice their reads now hit the canonical mount (slower but provenance-correct). The local-cache fallback path now emits a `warning()` it did not before.

Surface both in the `v0.4.0` changelog with migration notes.

## Cross-references

- Companion: Issue B (`dw_publish` creation) — must land after this one (`dw_publish` builds on the same mode-contract scaffolding)
- DW-Production consumer: vendored copy at `00_functions/dw_io.R`, manifest pin currently `v0.1.0-rc1` -> bumping to `v0.3.0` (Step 0 sync) -> `v0.4.0` after this issue lands
- Inspired by: HVA empirical run 2026-05-26 in DW-Production; user clarification 2026-05-26 on reviewer-mode read+write rules; full plan at `~/.claude/plans/fluffy-greeting-pretzel.md` (v4 section)

cc @karavan88 @sbrar29 @laurenfrancis1202 — sector-lead co-review request (your sectors are the validation harness for the mode contract, both read and write sides).

issues.title: test(r): add testthat regression suite + GitHub Actions CI (125 PASS)
issues.body:
## Summary

Adds the **R regression-test infrastructure** flagged as missing in the
follow-up to #11 / #12.  Stacked on #12 so the B1–B4 backports can
actually be tested.

## Layer 1 — `testthat` suite

`r/tests/testthat/` with **47 test blocks, 125 assertions**, all
passing locally.

| File | Tests | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| `test-dw_io-paths.R` | 7 | `dw_resolve_path` — structured + literal + vintage + error envelopes |
| `test-dw_io-canonical.R` | 5 | `dw_is_canonical` incl. **sibling-prefix regression** |
| `test-dw_io-isid.R` | 5 | `dw_isid` — pass / dup detection / missing keys / composite keys |
| `test-dw_io-save-roundtrip.R` | 4 | `dw_save → dw_use` + isid contract + reviewer-mode guard |
| `test-dw_io-gzip.R` | 3 | **B2 regression** — gzip auto-detect, magic-byte check |
| `test-dw_io-provenance-failure.R` | 1 | **B3 regression** — sidecar failure warns, primary file preserved |
| `test-dw_io-remote-freeze.R` | 4 | **B1 regression** — empty allowlist refuses, reviewer mode lockout, frozen read |
| `test-dw_api.R` | 4 | **B4 regression** — `http`/`github_raw` default to `rds`; unsupported api + cached + reviewer lockout |
| `test-dw_nestweight.R` | 3 | Stratum totals preserved + zero-weight on missing + missing-column envelope |
| `test-test_scripts.R` | 6 | Clean dir / catches `read_csv` / `httr::GET` / `# cso-allow:` escape / `error_on_violation` |
| `test-cso_toolkit_sync.R` | 5 | check/diff/pull error envelopes + reviewer-mode no-API rule |

Shared fixtures in `r/tests/testthat/helper-state.R`:

- `local_state(...)` — snapshot `.GlobalEnv`, set test-time bindings,
  restore on test exit (works with any of the 15 toolkit state keys)
- `local_tempdir()` — auto-cleanup
- `expect_envelope(err, function_name = "...")` — asserts
  `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT.\n  Why: ...\n  Fix: ...` shape on every
  raised error or warning

## Layer 2 — GitHub Actions CI

`.github/workflows/r-check.yml` runs `R CMD check` (which invokes the
testthat suite automatically because testthat is in Suggests) across
**4 OS/R matrix combinations**:

- ubuntu-latest · R release
- ubuntu-latest · R devel
- macos-latest · R release
- windows-latest · R release  (catches UNICEF-laptop short-name issues)

Trigger: push/PR to `main` or `develop`. Concurrent runs cancelled on
the same branch.

## Two real bugs the suite caught while being written

1. **`dw_is_canonical` Windows short-name asymmetry.**
   `normalizePath()` resolves 8.3 short names (`JPAZEV~1`) to long
   names (`jpazevedo`) only for existing paths; non-existent paths
   keep the short form. This made the reviewer-mode canonical-write
   guard silently broken for sector scripts targeting a non-existent
   output file in a short-named user profile. Fix:
   `.normalize_for_comparison()` walks up to the nearest existing
   ancestor before normalising.

2. **`.is_allowlisted_url()` subscript-out-of-bounds.**  Dereferenced
   `allow[[1]]` *before* checking `length(allow) == 0`, so a consumer
   profile with `dw_url_allowlist <- character(0)` threw the wrong
   error. Same bug in the error-message branch of `_resolve_remote_url`.
   Fix: `length() == 0` short-circuit first.

Both bugs now have regression tests that pin the correct behaviour.

## DESCRIPTION updates

- `Suggests:` gains `testthat (>= 3.0.0)` and `withr`.
- `Config/testthat/edition: 3`.

## Test plan

- [x] `devtools::test(".")` → **125 PASS / 0 FAIL / 0 SKIP / 0 WARN**
- [x] `devtools::check(".", args = c("--no-manual", "--no-build-vignettes"))`
      → **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged (20/20 + 30/30)

## Why this lands on top of #12

PR #12 introduces the B1–B4 backports. The new regression tests
*exercise* B1–B4. Stacking ensures the tests have something real to
test against.

## After merge

When #12 merges into develop, this PR's base auto-rebases to develop.
Then merge this one too.

issues.title: feat: DW-Production backports — B1 remote-URL freeze + B2/B3/B4 fixes
issues.body:
## Summary

Implements **Stage 2** of the alignment audit from #11. Adopts the four
DW-Production-side improvements that surfaced when comparing
`DW-Production/00_functions/` against cso-toolkit `v0.3.0`. R + Python
parity throughout.

## Backports

### B1 — `dw_use("https://...")` remote-URL freeze (new feature)

Whole pattern lifted from DW-Production: producer downloads the URL once
into a frozen cache + writes a `.provenance.json` sidecar; reviewer mode
refuses to fetch unfrozen URLs.

- **R side** (`r/R/dw_io.R`): seven new internal helpers
  (`.is_allowlisted_url`, `.dw_frozen_root`, `.url_to_frozen_path`,
  `.write_remote_provenance`, `.download_and_freeze`,
  `.resolve_remote_url`) wired into `.resolve_for_read()` via an
  `^https?://` dispatch.
- **Python side** (`python/src/dw_io.py`): same shape, same names
  (snake_case).
- **`_state` additions** (`python/src/_state.py`): `dw_url_allowlist`,
  `dw_frozen_root`, `githubFolder`.

Allowlist is **empty by default** so the toolkit ships consumer-neutral
(per maintainer decision from the audit). Consumers configure via:

```r
# R consumer's profile_<repo>.R
dw_url_allowlist <- c("^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/your-org/")
```

```python
# Python consumer's profile_<repo>.py
from cso_toolkit import _state
_state.configure(dw_url_allowlist=[
    r"^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/your-org/",
])
```

### B2 — gzip auto-detect (QoL fix)

`dw_save` now auto-enables compression when the path already ends in
`.gz`. Previously passing `compress=FALSE` with a `.gz` path silently
wrote the file uncompressed under the misleading name.

### B3 — `tryCatch` around `.provenance.json` (robustness fix)

Sidecar write failure now emits a `warning()` / `warnings.warn()`
rather than rolling back the primary file write. Sidecars are metadata;
the asset is what matters.

### B4 — UIS URL encoding + `http`/`github_raw` default extension (bug fix)

- R-side `.api_fetch_uis` now uses `utils::URLencode(reserved=TRUE)` on
  query keys + values. Previously param values containing `&` / `=` /
  spaces / non-ASCII corrupted the query.
  (Python's `requests` already URL-encodes via `params=dict`.)
- `.dw_api_default_ext` for `http` and `github_raw` bumped from `csv` to
  `rds` (R) / `pkl` (Python). Text and binary payloads now round-trip
  correctly.

## Open questions resolved (with audit defaults)

The three questions from #11's body resolved with the proposed defaults:

1. **Allowlist scope** → empty by default; consumers configure via
   `_state`. ✅
2. **`aggregate_data_v2` back-compat wrapper** → preserve toolkit's
   removal decision. No change in this PR. ✅
3. **Version cadence** → `v0.4.0` (minor). B1 is a net-new feature. ✅

You can override any of these in review.

## Test plan

- [x] R CMD check: **0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
- [x] Python smoke test: **20/20** invariants pass (was 15; +5 new for
  B1–B4)
- [x] Python error-envelope test: **30/30** unchanged
- [x] Roxygen + NAMESPACE regenerated cleanly (26 Rd files all parse)
- [x] Manual: `dw_use("https://...")` with empty allowlist refuses
- [x] Manual: reviewer mode refuses unfrozen URL with three-part
  envelope (verified in smoke test)

## Next step (after merge)

Cut `release/v0.4.0` branch, bump versions
(`r/DESCRIPTION` → `0.4.0`, `python/pyproject.toml` → `0.4.0`,
`python/src/__init__.py` → `__version__ = "0.4.0"`,
`templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml.pulled_version` → `v0.4.0`), open
release PR `develop → main`, tag `v0.4.0`. Same workflow as `v0.3.0`.

issues.title: audit: align DW-Production 00_functions/ with cso-toolkit v0.3.0
issues.body:
## Summary

Read-only alignment audit (report-only — no code changes).

Compares `C:\GitHub\mytasks\DW-Production\00_functions\` against
cso-toolkit `r/R/` at HEAD (post-`v0.3.0`) AND at the version
DW-Production currently pins (`v0.1.0-rc1`, per its
`.toolkit_manifest.yml`).

The report ([`docs/dw-production-alignment-2026-05-25.md`](docs/dw-production-alignment-2026-05-25.md))
documents what each direction's drift looks like and proposes a
four-stage workflow to reconcile.

## Headline findings

### DW-Production has 4 undeclared local edits that should flow INTO cso-toolkit

`local_edits: []` in DW-Production's manifest is **incorrect** —
DW-Production has substantive on-top-of-`v0.1.0-rc1` changes:

| ID | File | What | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| **B1** | `dw_io.R` | Entire **remote-URL freeze pattern** for `dw_use("https://...")` (allowlist + one-time download + frozen-file provenance + reviewer lockout) — ~70 lines | 🟢 Net-new feature |
| **B2** | `dw_io.R` | Gzip auto-detect on `dw_save(compress=...)` when path already ends `.gz` | 🟡 Quality-of-life |
| **B3** | `dw_io.R` | `tryCatch` around `.provenance.json` write so sidecar failure warns rather than rolling back primary | 🟡 Robustness |
| **B4** | `dw_api.R` | `utils::URLencode()` on UIS params + `rds` default ext for `http` / `github_raw` (was silently mis-shaping payloads) | 🔴 Bug fix |

### Toolkit-side improvements DW-Production needs to pull (v0.1.0-rc1 → v0.3.0)

- Roxygen-complete reference (NAMESPACE + 26 Rd files + pkgdown config)
- Graceful three-part error envelope `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT / Why / Fix`
- 4 new toolkit-only helpers: `dw_nestweight.R`, `profile_helpers.R`, `test_scripts.R`, `zzz.R`
- `create_dw_sector_script()` wrapper that preserves DW-Production's hardcoded layout
- Python siblings of every helper at `python/src/`

### Split work (both sides made deliberate divergent choices)

`aggregate_data_v2.R`: DW kept the `aggregate_data()` back-compat
wrapper inside the v2 file; toolkit moved it out citing source-order
fragility.  Recommendation in the report: add a renamed exported
function so both behaviours are reachable.

## Recommended workflow (4 stages)

1. **Stage 1 (30 min, DW side, no code)** — populate
   `DW-Production/.toolkit_manifest.yml`'s `local_edits:` list so the
   next `cso_toolkit_pull()` doesn't silently clobber B1–B4.
2. **Stage 2 (~1.5 days, cso-toolkit side, 1 PR)** — backport B1–B4
   with R + Python parity + tests + Roxygen.  Tag `v0.4.0`.
3. **Stage 3 (~1 hour, DW side)** — `cso_toolkit_pull("v0.4.0")`;
   vendor the 4 new toolkit-only helpers; switch sector-script call
   sites to `create_dw_sector_script()`.
4. **Stage 4 (incremental, DW side)** — wire `test_scripts(error_on_violation=TRUE)`
   into DW-Production CI; run `review_profile()`; adopt
   `dw_nestweight()` in MICS-FLS / DHS pipelines.

## Open questions for the maintainer

The report's "Open questions" section needs your call:

1. **Remote-URL freeze allowlist** — ship the toolkit with
   `raw.githubusercontent.com/unicef-drp/*` baked in, or empty by
   default + consumers populate via `_state`?
2. **`aggregate_data_v2` back-compat wrapper** — preserve toolkit's
   removal decision, or land DW-Production's wrapper under a renamed
   export?
3. **Tag cadence** — backports as `v0.3.1` (patch) or `v0.4.0`
   (minor)?  The remote-URL freeze argues for minor; report
   recommends `v0.4.0`.

## Test plan

- [x] Branch `chore/compare-dw-production-2026-05-25` is read-only
      (no R or Python source modified)
- [x] R CMD check on this branch identical to develop's last result
      (0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes)
- [x] Python smoke + envelope tests unchanged
- [x] Report renders cleanly in GitHub Markdown

## Next steps

This PR is intended for **review-and-discussion**, not merge.  After
you weigh in on the three open questions, I'll either:

- close this branch and open a focused implementation PR for Stage 2 (with the four backports), OR
- merge this audit into develop as a record + open the implementation PR separately.

issues.title: chore: bump develop to next dev version (post-v0.3.0)
issues.body:
## Summary

Post-v0.3.0 dev-version bump on `develop`.  Standard gitflow hygiene
so future commits don't carry the released version number in their
metadata.

### Changes

- `r/DESCRIPTION` — `Version: 0.3.0` → `0.3.0.9000` (R dev convention
  for "post-0.3.0, pre-next").
- `python/pyproject.toml` — `version = "0.3.0"` → `"0.4.0.dev0"` (PEP
  440 pre-release marker).
- `python/src/__init__.py` — `__version__ = "0.3.0"` → `"0.4.0.dev0"`.
- `NEWS.md` — new `## Unreleased` section added at the top, with a
  one-line note documenting the convention (entries land here as PRs
  merge; at release time the header is renamed and a fresh Unreleased
  section is added back).

`templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml.pulled_version` deliberately stays at
`v0.3.0` — consumers pin to released tags, not dev versions.

### Test plan

- [x] `R CMD check` → 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes
- [x] `cso_toolkit.__version__` resolves to `"0.4.0.dev0"` via the
      bootstrap loader
- [x] No behaviour changes; metadata only

issues.title: release: v0.3.0 — Python parity + Roxygen-complete R + graceful errors
issues.body:
## Release: v0.3.0 (2026-05-25)

First release with full **Python parity** for every R helper, plus the
**R Roxygen-complete reference** (NAMESPACE + 26 Rd files + pkgdown
config), and a **three-part error envelope**
(`[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT / Why / Fix`) standardised across R and
Python.

## Summary of changes since v0.2.0

### Python helpers — new

- `python/src/` — full Python port of every R helper. Same behaviour
  contract; mode-aware path routing, Z: drive mirror, provenance
  sidecars, version-drift detection. 10 modules + foundation
  (`_state.py`, `__init__.py`); 26 public entries.
- `python/pyproject.toml` — optional `pip install -e python/` for local
  development. Optional-dep groups: `excel`, `parquet`, `yaml`, `http`,
  `sdmx`, `worldbank`, `test`, `all`.
- `python/src/py.typed` — PEP 561 marker.
- `python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` — 15-invariant end-to-end test.
- `python/tests/manual/error_envelope_test.py` — 30-path contract test
  asserting every public raise emits the `[cso_toolkit.<func>]` prefix
  + `Fix:` guidance.
- `docs/dw_io_python_reference.md` + `docs/dw_api_python_reference.md`.

### R helpers — documentation hygiene

- Roxygen pass across `dw_io.R`, `dw_api.R`, `cso_toolkit_sync.R`,
  `generate_markdown_report.R`, and `aggregate_data.R`. Every exported
  function carries `@param` / `@return` / `@export`; every internal
  helper carries `@keywords internal` + `@noRd`.
- `@seealso` + `@family` Roxygen tags on every export, grouped into
  eight families: `io`, `api`, `sync`, `aggregate`, `survey-weights`,
  `reporting`, `scaffolding`, `audit`.
- Generated `r/NAMESPACE` (26 exports) + `r/man/` (26 `.Rd` files).
- `r/_pkgdown.yml` — pkgdown reference site config with the eight
  grouped sections, UNICEF cyan/navy theme.
- `r/LICENSE` — CRAN-style 2-line MIT pointer.
- `r/R/zzz.R` — single shared `.cso_require()` helper +
  `utils::globalVariables()` declarations.

### Graceful error envelopes (R + Python)

- Every R `stop()` / `warning()` and every Python `raise` follows the
  three-part `[cso_toolkit.<func>] WHAT.\n  Why: ...\n  Fix: ...`
  shape with `call. = FALSE` (R) or typed exceptions (Python).
- Python: HTTP / JSON-parse / CSV-parse / YAML failures wrapped with
  URL + status + body snippet; no bare `requests.HTTPError` /
  `KeyError` / `pd.errors.ParserError` bubbles up uncaught.
- Python: sensitive keys (`token`, `headers`, `api_key`, `password`,
  …) in `dw_api_fetch` kwargs redacted before they reach the
  `.provenance.json` sidecar.

### Sync / vendoring infrastructure

- `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — header expanded with a
  field-by-field reference; `pulled_version` bumped to `v0.3.0`.
- `r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` — release-eve smoke test that
  simulates a consumer repo end-to-end against api.github.com.

### Documentation

- New `r/README.md`, `python/README.md`, `stata/README.md` —
  per-language top-level READMEs with install paths, layout,
  quick-start, mode contract, error envelope, testing.
- Top-level `README.md` Status + Versioning bullets refreshed
  (previously frozen on `v0.1.0-rc1`).

## Version bumps

- `r/DESCRIPTION` — `Version: 0.3.0`, `Date: 2026-05-25`
- `python/pyproject.toml` — `version = "0.3.0"`
- `python/src/__init__.py` — `__version__ = "0.3.0"`
- `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — `pulled_version: "v0.3.0"`

## Verification
