issues.title: feat(python): port all 10 R helpers to python/src/
issues.body:
## Summary

Python parity for the R helpers in `r/R/`.  Companion to #6 (Roxygen +
sync); together they fulfil the "review all R functions + generate the
Python equivalent" ask.  No R-side or Stata-side files touched —
this PR is purely additive under `python/`.

### Architecture

Vendoring mirrors the R side: drop `python/src/` into a consumer repo's
`00_functions/` (renamed `cso_toolkit/`), then:

```python
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "00_functions")
from cso_toolkit import dw_save, dw_use, dw_api_fetch
from cso_toolkit import _state as cso_state
```

Session-level globals (mode, paths, Z: drive availability) configured
once at profile load via `cso_state.configure(...)`.

### Module catalogue (10 modules + foundation)

| R file                          | Python file                      | Public entries |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | -------------- |
| `dw_io.R`                       | `dw_io.py`                       | `dw_save`, `dw_use`, `dw_compare`, `dw_merge`, `dw_isid`, `dw_verify_z`, `dw_resolve_path`, `dw_is_canonical` |
| `dw_api.R`                      | `dw_api.py`                      | `dw_api_fetch`, `dw_api_cached`, `dw_api_inventory` |
| `cso_toolkit_sync.R`            | `cso_toolkit_sync.py`            | `cso_toolkit_check`, `cso_toolkit_diff`, `cso_toolkit_pull` |
| `aggregate_data.R` + `aggregate_data_v2.R` | `aggregate_data.py`   | `aggregate_data`, `aggregate_data_v2`, `generate_agg_footnote`, `apply_time_window` |
| `dw_nestweight.R`               | `dw_nestweight.py`               | `dw_nestweight` |
| `generate_markdown_report.R`    | `generate_markdown_report.py`    | `generate_markdown_report`, `process_all_csv_files` |
| `create_sector_script.R`        | `create_sector_script.py`        | `create_sector_script`, `create_dw_sector_script` |
| `profile_helpers.R`             | `profile_helpers.py`             | `create_profile`, `review_profile` |
| `test_scripts.R`                | `test_scripts.py`                | `test_scripts` (now scans `.py` for raw IO/HTTP) |
| —                               | `_state.py`                      | `configure`, `_get` |
| —                               | `__init__.py`                    | re-exports all 26 public entries |

### Behaviour-parity highlights

- **Path resolution** — same `kind = "wrk" | "raw" | "meta"` matrix,
  same `name` / `sector` / `vintage` arguments.
- **Mode contract** — `dw_save` raises `PermissionError` (Python
  analogue of R's `stop()`) on reviewer-mode canonical writes without
  `allow_canonical_write=True`.
- **Z: drive mirror** — automatic carbon-copy on canonical writes;
  non-blocking size or sha256 integrity check on canonical reads.
- **Provenance sidecar** — identical JSON schema to R (`path`,
  `format`, `written_at`, `user`, `dw_mode`, `vintage`, `sha256`,
  `isid`, `schema`, optional `metadata`).
- **Auto-dispatch on extension** — `.csv` / `.tsv` / `.txt` / `.xlsx` /
  `.parquet` / `.dta` / `.json` / `.yaml`, plus Python additions
  `.pkl` / `.pickle` (analogue of R's `.rds`).

### Scaffolding adaptations

The three scaffolding helpers generate Python (not R):

- `create_sector_script.py` writes `00_run_<code>.py` with profile
  import, `log_message` usage, `start_time` tracking, and a
  `try / except` block.
- `create_profile.py` writes `profile_<repo>.py` that wires into the
  Python toolkit via `cso_toolkit._state.configure(dw_mode=..., dw_apis_allowed=...)`.
- `test_scripts.py` registry detects Python-flavoured violations
  (`pd.read_csv`, `df.to_excel`, `requests.get`, `sdmx.Client`,
  `wbgapi.data.*`, etc.) instead of the R-flavoured ones.

### Dependency layering

- **Core** (always required): `pandas`, `numpy`.
- **Format-specific** (lazy-imported): `openpyxl` (xlsx), `pyarrow`
  (parquet), `PyYAML` (yaml).
- **API-specific** (lazy-imported by `dw_api_fetch` only): `requests`,
  `sdmx1`, `wbgapi`.

Consumers running purely reviewer-mode pipelines never touch the
API libs.

## Test plan

- [x] `python python/tests/manual/smoke_test.py` — 12 in-process checks
  pass end-to-end (see commit `9758f50` body for the full list)
- [x] `cso_toolkit.__version__` resolves to `"0.2.1.dev0"`
- [x] `len(cso_toolkit.__all__) == 27`
- [x] `dw_save` → `dw_use` CSV roundtrip + `.provenance.json` sidecar
- [x] `dw_isid` rejects duplicates with sample rows in the error
- [x] `aggregate_data_v2(method="weighted_mean", coverage_threshold=0.5)`
- [x] `dw_nestweight` preserves stratum totals to 1e-9 tolerance
- [x] `create_profile` → `review_profile` reports 7-pass / 0-fail
- [x] `test_scripts` 0 violations on clean file; 1 violation
  (`io-read-csv`) on file with raw `pd.read_csv`
- [x] `cso_toolkit_check` with missing manifest returns `None`

## Sequencing note

PR #6 (Roxygen + sync) and this PR are **independent** code-wise.
Either can merge first.  Reading them as a pair tells the full story
(R docs polished → Python port lands), but the Python port doesn't
depend on the R Roxygen artefacts to compile or run.

issues.title: docs(r) + chore(r): Roxygen pass + sync infrastructure
issues.body:
## Summary

Two atomic phases of pre-Python-port hygiene on the R helpers.

### docs(r): Roxygen pass on 5 R files + regenerate NAMESPACE/man (66f2dec)

Brings the 5 partially-documented R files up to the Roxygen-complete bar
already set by `aggregate_data_v2.R`, `dw_nestweight.R`,
`create_sector_script.R`, `profile_helpers.R`, and `test_scripts.R`.

| File                          | Functions touched |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `dw_io.R`                     | 9 exported + ~10 internal |
| `dw_api.R`                    | 3 exported + 9 internal fetchers |
| `cso_toolkit_sync.R`          | 3 exported + 3 internal |
| `generate_markdown_report.R`  | 2 exported |
| `aggregate_data.R`            | added missing `@param country.coverage`, cleaned `@export` whitespace |

Every exported function now carries `@param` / `@return` / `@export`;
every internal helper carries `@keywords internal` + `@noRd`. Also
fixed a Roxygen-block bug in `aggregate_data_v2.R` where the top-of-file
banner used `#'` and got attached to `.cso_require`, generating a spurious
`dot-cso_require.Rd`.

Regenerated artefacts via `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)`:

- `r/NAMESPACE` — first-time generation; exports 26 functions.
- `r/man/*.Rd` — 26 files, all parse cleanly via `tools::parse_Rd()`.

**No behaviour change.** Pure documentation.

### chore(r): sync infrastructure + bump to 0.2.1.9000 (20b195e)

Three small additions that move the vendoring contract forward.

- **(a)** `r/DESCRIPTION` → `0.2.1.9000` + `NEWS.md` Unreleased entries.
- **(b)** `r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` — manual smoke test that
  simulates a consumer repo, drops the three core helpers + manifest into
  it, sources them, and calls `cso_toolkit_check()` end-to-end against
  the live GitHub API. Verifies the returned list shape (`source`,
  `pinned_version`, `upstream_version`, `updates_available`,
  `updated_files`). "Manual" because the test hits the GitHub API; not
  part of any automated suite.
- **(c)** `templates/.toolkit_manifest.yml` — header expanded with a
  field-by-field reference; `pulled_version` bumped `v0.1.0-rc1 → v0.2.0`;
  the seven optional helpers (`aggregate_data`, `dw_nestweight`, etc.)
  listed as commented-out entries so consumers can opt in without
  re-deriving the file list.

Smoke-test output as of this PR:

```
[cso-toolkit] unicef-drp/cso-toolkit: pinned v0.2.0 == upstream v0.2.0 (up to date)
OK: shape matches expected (source, pinned_version, upstream_version, updates_available, updated_files).
```

## Why this lands before the Python port

The Python port will derive its docstrings from the R Roxygen blocks
(one source of truth per function). Doing the Roxygen pass first means
the Python port lands with docstrings that match the R reference exactly
— no later sync churn.

## Test plan

- [x] `roxygen2::roxygenise('.', clean = TRUE)` writes 26 Rd files, 0 errors
- [x] `tools::parse_Rd()` on every Rd file → all clean
- [x] `devtools::load_all('.', export_all = FALSE)` → loads cleanly
- [x] `Rscript r/tests/manual/check_consumer_side.R` → end-to-end pass against api.github.com

issues.title: v0.3: ship remaining Stata-side helpers (dw_use, dw_require_no_api, dw_load_config)
issues.body:
## Context

[v0.2.0](https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/releases/tag/v0.2.0)
shipped the first three Stata helpers (`dw_save.ado`, `dw_compare.ado`,
`dw_mkdir.ado`) so the `stata/src/` placeholder is no longer empty.
This issue tracks the **three remaining Stata-side gaps** that v0.3
should close. Each gap mirrors a contract piece that the R side
(`r/R/dw_io.R` / `r/R/dw_api.R`) already enforces.

## Gap 1 — `stata/src/dw_use.ado` (reads, currently unconstrained)

**Why it's missing today.** v0.2 shipped `dw_save` (writes) but no
matching `dw_use` (reads). Sector `.do` files can therefore use any
read command (`use`, `import delimited`, `insheet`, `import excel`,
etc.) against any path without the mode contract noticing. Reviewer
mode does not get the Z: integrity check on canonical reads, and
producer mode does not get the auto-fallback to canonical when the
sandbox copy is missing.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- [ ] `dw_use, filename(...) path(...) [as(...) cols(...) verify_z(...) fallback_canonical(...)]`
  matching the call style of R `dw_use()`.
- [ ] Auto-dispatch on extension (`.dta`, `.csv`, `.xlsx`, `.parquet`,
  `.rds` via `R` if installed) — same list as R `dw_use()` insofar as
  Stata can read each format natively or via a documented add-on.
- [ ] Producer mode + canonical read: verifies Teams ↔ Z: by file
  size (default) or content hash via `datasignature`; mismatch fires
  a warning, read completes.
- [ ] Reviewer mode: reads canonical fine; if the sandbox path
  doesn't exist, falls back to canonical with a warning (matches
  R-side `fallback_canonical = TRUE`).
- [ ] Reads the sibling `.provenance.json` when present and stashes
  the schema / datasignature into `r()` for downstream caller checks.

## Gap 2 — `stata/src/dw_require_no_api.do` (reviewer-mode hard-stop)

**Why it's missing today.** On the R side, every `dw_api_fetch()`
call invokes `dw_require_no_api()` which aborts with a clear error
in reviewer mode. On the Stata side, there's no equivalent — a
reviewer-mode session that loads a `.do` file containing
`copy https://... using ...` or a `datalibweb` call will silently
hit the network.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- [ ] Stata `.do` (or `.ado`) helper that reads `$dw_mode`; in
  reviewer mode, displays a clear error message and `error 459`.
- [ ] Optional `context()` parameter so the error message can name
  the call site.
- [ ] Documented invocation pattern (top of any sector `.do` that
  would otherwise hit a remote: `dw_require_no_api, context("ws/01_fetch.do")`).
- [ ] Help file `dw_require_no_api.sthlp`.

## Gap 3 — `stata/src/dw_load_config.do` (YAML config loader)

**Why it's missing today.** Each Stata project currently sets
`$dw_mode`, `$teamsWrkDataCanonical`, `$teamsRawDataCanonical` etc.
by hand in its profile `.do`. The R side reads
`~/.config/user_config.yml` via `yaml::read_yaml()`. There's no
Stata equivalent, which means the source of truth for the mode
contract diverges across the two languages.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- [ ] `dw_load_config.do` (or `.ado`) reads `~/.config/user_config.yml`
  (or a path passed in via option) and populates the standard globals:
  `$dw_mode`, `$teamsWrkData`, `$teamsRawData`,
  `$teamsWrkDataCanonical`, `$teamsRawDataCanonical`,
  `$sandboxRoot`.
- [ ] Hard-fail when `dw_mode` is missing or not in
  `{"producer", "reviewer"}` (matches the R-side `profile_helpers`
  behaviour).
- [ ] YAML parsing in Stata without an external package — minimal
  hand-rolled key:value parser keyed to the documented `user_config.yml`
  schema. Avoids AppLocker / package-install friction.
- [ ] Help file `dw_load_config.sthlp` documenting the expected YAML
  schema and the globals populated.

## Suggested ordering

1. `dw_require_no_api` first — cheapest, smallest, and unblocks
   reviewer-mode discipline on Stata sectors as soon as it lands.
2. `dw_load_config` second — once installed, every Stata profile
   collapses to one line (`do dw_load_config.do`) and the mode
   contract is uniform across R and Stata.
3. `dw_use` last — biggest scope, depends on the prior two for the
   mode-contract checks it needs to enforce.

## Related

- v0.2.0 release: https://github.com/unicef-drp/cso-toolkit/releases/tag/v0.2.0
- `stata/src/README.md` "Known limitations (v0.2)" section
- Reference R helpers: `r/R/dw_io.R`, `r/R/dw_api.R`, `r/R/profile_helpers.R`

issues.title: release: develop -> main (Stata helpers, R toolkit expansion, CSO rebrand, workflow diagrams)
issues.body:
## Summary

Promotes `develop` to `main` after the three feature PRs (#1, #2, #3) landed on develop earlier today (2026-05-24). This is the **first substantive release** beyond the initial scaffold.

### What this release adds

**R helpers (`r/R/`)** — 7 new files:
- `aggregate_data.R` / `aggregate_data_v2.R` — `aggregate_data()` (legacy) + `aggregate_data_v2()` with `weighted_mean` / `mean` / `sum` / `proportion`, coverage threshold, metadata columns; plus `generate_agg_footnote()` and `apply_time_window()`
- `generate_markdown_report.R` — descriptive-stats Markdown report from a CSV file or a folder of CSVs
- `create_sector_script.R` — `create_sector_script(sector_name, sector_code, base_dir, ...)` + DW-Production convenience wrapper `create_dw_sector_script()`
- `profile_helpers.R` — `create_profile(repo_name, ...)` scaffolds a `profile_<repo>.R`; `review_profile(path, ...)` audits an existing one against the toolkit contract
- `test_scripts.R` — `test_scripts(path, ...)` recursively scans `.R` scripts and flags direct calls to file-IO or external-API commands wrapped by `dw_io.R` / `dw_api.R` (16 built-in rules; per-line `# cso-allow: <rule-id>` escape hatch; CI mode via `error_on_violation = TRUE`)
- `dw_nestweight.R` — `dw_nestweight()` redistributes survey weights from missing nested observations (R port of `edukit_nestweight`)

**Stata helpers (`stata/src/`)** — first three Stata helpers fill the v0.2 placeholder:
- `dw_save.ado` — Stata sibling of R `dw_save()`. `isid` + `compress` + `save` + sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar (JSON-escaped); enforces producer / reviewer mode via `$dw_mode`
- `dw_compare.ado` — Stata sibling of R `dw_compare()`. Merges on `idvars` and classifies per value column; optional Markdown report
- `dw_mkdir.ado` — recursive `mkdir`
- Companion `.sthlp` help files for all three

**Docs (`docs/`)**:
- `dw_io_reference.md` / `dw_api_reference.md` — per-function references
- `git_workflow.md` — gitflow + branch-protection contract reference
- `roles_and_workflow.md` — extended with Mermaid data-flow + role-vs-action diagrams

**Branding + meta**:
- Top-level `README.md` rebranded as **UNICEF Chief Statistician Office toolkit**, with a new **Objective and motivation** section spelling out the reproducibility-and-scalability mission for the D&A Section in OSE
- `NEWS.md` Unreleased section enumerating every addition
- `r/R/README.md` and `stata/src/README.md` updated to the live helper inventory

### Lineage credits

Three Stata helpers and the R `dw_nestweight` are ports from the World Bank [EduAnalyticsToolkit](https://github.com/worldbank/EduAnalyticsToolkit). Each ported file credits the original author (Diana Goldemberg for `dw_save` / `dw_nestweight`, Kristoffer Bjärkefur for `dw_compare` / `dw_mkdir`).

### Review fixes folded in

Every PR landed with all Copilot review comments addressed: 11 fixes on PR #1 (multi-col `by` correctness, `n_distinct` NA-counting, namespace hygiene, removed source-time side effects, `%||%` collision, generated-template defaults), 2 on PR #2 (Mermaid paths matching documented layout, pushed-commit recovery recipe), 5 on PR #3 (`capture isid` vs `quietly isid`, JSON-escaped sidecar values, DRY compare logic, non-numeric guard).

## Suggested next steps after merge

- [ ] Tag `v0.2.0` on `main` per the versioning policy (v0.1.0-rc1 was scaffold; v0.2 was scoped to ship the Stata side, which now exists)
- [ ] Update the GitHub release notes from this PR body
- [ ] Open a tracking issue for the three remaining Stata gaps: `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do`

## Commit log

23 commits from main → develop (atomic per logical change). See the Files Changed tab for the full diff (+3,047 / −30 across 21 files).

## Test plan

- [x] All R files parse (`Rscript -e parse(...)`); test_scripts self-audit returns 0 violations
- [x] R smoke tests: `aggregate_data` multi-col `by`; `create_profile` + `review_profile` round-trip; `dw_nestweight` numeric + non-numeric
- [ ] Stata files: smoke-tested manually post-merge against a real Stata install (Stata not on CI / sandbox)
- [ ] Visual confirm: Mermaid diagrams render on GitHub for `roles_and_workflow.md` + `git_workflow.md`

issues.title: feat: port edukit_save / comparefiles / rmkdir to Stata as dw_* helpers, and edukit_nestweight to R as dw_nestweight
issues.body:
## Summary

Lifts four commands from the World Bank
[EduAnalyticsToolkit](https://github.com/worldbank/EduAnalyticsToolkit)
into cso-toolkit. Three become **Stata helpers** (filling the v0.2
placeholder); one is **converted to R**.

### New files

**R (`r/R/`):**
- `dw_nestweight.R` — port of `edukit_nestweight` (Diana Goldemberg). Redistributes survey weights from missing nested observations. Pure analytics helper; sits alongside `aggregate_data*`. The `dw_` prefix is kept for namespace consistency though it's not contract-bound.

**Stata (`stata/src/`):**
- `dw_save.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `edukit_save` / `savemetadata` (Diana Goldemberg). isid + compress + save + sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar matching the R `dw_io` shape. Enforces producer / reviewer mode via `$dw_mode`; canonical writes blocked in reviewer mode unless `allow_canonical_write` is passed. Content hash via Stata-native `datasignature` (no shell-out / AppLocker issue).
- `dw_compare.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `comparefiles` / `edukit_comparefiles` (Kristoffer Bjärkefur). Merges two `.dta` files on idvars and classifies each value column as identical / numerically-equivalent (within `tol()`) / different; optional Markdown report.
- `dw_mkdir.ado` (+ `.sthlp`) — port of `rmkdir` / `edukit_rmkdir` (Kristoffer Bjärkefur). Recursive `mkdir` (Stata's built-in is non-recursive).

### Modified files

- `stata/src/README.md` — replaces the v0.1.0-rc1 "scaffolded, empty" placeholder with live helper docs: lineage table, install via adopath, mode-contract wiring, and three explicit known limitations.

### Deliberately deferred

- **Top-level `README.md` table updates** and **`NEWS.md` Unreleased entries** — these would conflict with PR #1's bigger rebrand rewrite, which already adds an Unreleased section. Cleaner to add them in a follow-up after #1 merges, into the section #1 created.
- **`edukit` (version meta)** — covered by `cso_toolkit_sync.R` + `.toolkit_manifest.yml`.
- **`edukit_datalibweb`** — WB-internal `datalibweb` retry wrapper; not applicable.
- **`edukit_dlwcheck`** — hardcoded education-assessment vocabularies (PISA / TIMSS / PIRLS / SACMEQ / EGRA); not applicable to UNICEF CSO indicator scope.

## Lineage credits

| cso-toolkit | EduAnalyticsToolkit ancestor | Original author |
|---|---|---|
| `dw_save.ado` | `edukit_save` / `savemetadata` | Diana Goldemberg |
| `dw_compare.ado` | `comparefiles` / `edukit_comparefiles` | Kristoffer Bjärkefur |
| `dw_mkdir.ado` | `rmkdir` / `edukit_rmkdir` | Kristoffer Bjärkefur |
| `dw_nestweight.R` | `nestweight` / `edukit_nestweight` | Diana Goldemberg |

Each helper credits the original author in its header.

## Deliberate divergences from the upstream lineage

1. **Naming**: renamed `dw_*` to align with the cso-toolkit family and avoid clashing if both toolkits are installed.
2. **Metadata model (Stata side)**: upstream `edukit_save` embeds metadata as `char _dta[...]` inside the `.dta`; `dw_save` writes a sibling `.provenance.json` sidecar matching the R-side shape, so producer / reviewer integrity checks work across both languages.
3. **Compare scope**: `dw_compare` is sized for the IO contract (publication-gate counts, per-column n_diff), not full editorial diffs. The upstream `comparefiles` remains the right tool for rich Markdown row-level diff reports — `dw_compare`'s `.sthlp` points users there.

## Test plan

- [x] R `dw_nestweight()` smoke test: 200-row tibble across two strata, 60 randomly NA'd — per-stratum totals byte-identical (diff = 0 in both strata).
- [ ] Stata `.ado` files: **no automated syntax check available** in this environment (Stata not on PATH). Manual review done; needs `do dw_save.ado` etc. against a real Stata install before tagging.
- [ ] End-to-end smoke: `dw_mkdir` → `dw_save` → `dw_compare` round-trip in producer mode, confirm `.provenance.json` sidecar shape matches what the R `dw_io` integrity check expects.
- [ ] Reviewer-mode hard-fail test: with `global dw_mode = "reviewer"` and a canonical path, confirm `dw_save` aborts with Stata error 459 unless `allow_canonical_write` is passed.

## Out-of-PR follow-ups (after this and #1 land)

- Add `dw_nestweight`, `dw_save`, `dw_compare`, `dw_mkdir` rows to PR #1's "What's in the box" table.
- Add four bullets to PR #1's NEWS Unreleased section.
- Open a tracking issue for the three remaining Stata-side gaps: `dw_use.ado`, `dw_require_no_api.do`, `dw_load_config.do` (read `dw_mode` from `~/.config/user_config.yml`).

issues.title: docs: add Mermaid diagrams for role + data-flow and git workflow
issues.body:
## Summary

Adds two Mermaid-based workflow diagrams so the contract this repo encodes
is visible at a glance, not buried in prose.

### `docs/roles_and_workflow.md` (extended)
- **Data flow at a glance** — flowchart of producer (APIs → cache →
  pipeline → canonical → Z: mirror), reviewer (canonical → sandbox →
  compare report → issues), and ingestor (canonical → Helix / SDMX /
  data.unicef.org / SoWC). Coloured by role boundary.
- **Role-vs-action matrix** — five allowed actions vs four forbidden
  actions, side-by-side.

### `docs/git_workflow.md` (new)
- Branches-at-a-glance table summarising the protection state of `main`
  and `develop` (default, PR required, force push, deletion, admin bypass).
- Mermaid flowchart of the trunks: `feat/*` → `develop` via PR, `develop`
  → `main` via release PR, hotfix admin push → `develop`.
- "The contract these rules enforce" prose explaining each setting.
- Standard recipes for the three workflows (task work, release, hotfix)
  with copy-able `git` / `gh` commands.
- Recovery recipe for an accidental commit on `develop` (force-push
  blocked, so recovery is local-only).

## Format choice

Mermaid embedded in Markdown rather than standalone SVG so:
- GitHub renders both diagrams inline (no binary checked in).
- Future contract changes diff cleanly in PR review.
- No external tool needed to update — change the source, push, done.

## Out of scope (deliberate)

- Linking these diagrams from `README.md` would conflict with the
  pending #1 rebrand PR; cleaner to add the cross-link in a follow-up
  after #1 merges.

## Test plan

- [x] Both `.md` files render Mermaid blocks (`mermaid` fenced code)
  — will display on GitHub once the PR opens.
- [ ] Open the PR on GitHub.com and visually confirm both diagrams
  render before merging (Mermaid syntax errors are silent in local
  preview).

issues.title: feat: lift aggregate/reporting/scaffolding helpers from DW-Production, add profile + contract-audit helpers, rebrand as UNICEF CSO toolkit
issues.body:
## Summary

Lifts the cross-sector, reusable helpers from `DW-Production/00_functions/`
into the canonical upstream (`cso-toolkit`), rebrands the repo as the
**UNICEF Chief Statistician Office (CSO) toolkit**, adds two new helper
modules (`profile_helpers.R`, `test_scripts.R`), and spells out the
objective: facilitate reproducibility and scalability of analytics developed
by the UNICEF Data and Analytics Section in OSE.

### New R helpers (`r/R/`)
- `aggregate_data.R` — legacy `aggregate_data()` (lifted verbatim)
- `aggregate_data_v2.R` — `aggregate_data_v2()` with `weighted_mean` /
  `mean` / `sum` / `proportion`, coverage threshold, metadata columns;
  ships `generate_agg_footnote()`, `apply_time_window()`, and a
  v1-compatible wrapper
- `generate_markdown_report.R` — descriptive-stats Markdown from CSV
  (annotated with `# cso-allow: io-read-csv` because it accepts arbitrary
  paths)
- `create_sector_script.R` — generalized over the DW-Production original;
  adds `base_dir`, `profile_name`, `profile_file`, `input_subpath`,
  `output_subpath`, `overwrite` parameters. Ships a
  `create_dw_sector_script()` convenience wrapper that pins the
  DW-Production conventions.
- `profile_helpers.R` — `create_profile(repo_name, ...)` scaffolds a
  `profile_<repo>.R` template with the standard CSO building blocks
  (cross-platform user identification, YAML config load, optional
  producer/reviewer `dw_mode`, optional Z: drive advisory, packages,
  sentinel). `review_profile(path, ...)` audits an existing profile for
  the same blocks with pass/warn/fail reporting.
- `test_scripts.R` — `test_scripts(path, ...)` recursively scans `.R`
  scripts and flags any direct call to a raw file-IO or external-API
  command that `dw_io.R` / `dw_api.R` is meant to wrap (16 built-in
  rules across two families). Per-line escape hatch via
  `# cso-allow: <rule-id>`; CI mode via `error_on_violation = TRUE`.

### New docs (`docs/`)
- `dw_io_reference.md` — per-function reference for `dw_io.R` (lifted
  from `00_functions/README.md`)
- `dw_api_reference.md` — per-function reference for `dw_api.R` (lifted
  from `00_functions/README.md`)

### Updated docs
- `README.md` — rebrand + **Objective and motivation** section + updated
  table + updated citation
- `r/R/README.md` — split into "core (v0.1.0-rc1)" vs "added
  post-v0.1.0-rc1" sections
- `NEWS.md` — Unreleased section itemising every new file

## Compatibility with existing vendoring

Filenames and function names match `DW-Production/00_functions/` 1:1 so
the next `cso_toolkit_pull()` is a clean mechanical copy. The
DW-Production `.toolkit_manifest.yml` will need to add the four new
filenames to the `vendored:` list when the consumer chooses to refresh.

## Test plan

- [x] All new R files parse cleanly (`parse(file = ...)`)
- [x] `create_profile()` + `review_profile()` round-trip end-to-end on a
  tempdir target — 7 pass / 1 expected warn (Z: omitted)
- [x] `test_scripts()` self-audit on `r/R/` returns 0 violations (the
  toolkit's own wrapped commands are correctly ignored via the default
  `ignore_files` list; the `generate_markdown_report.R` raw `read_csv`
  is exempted via `# cso-allow: io-read-csv`)
- [ ] Vendoring smoke test: `cso_toolkit_pull(target_version = ...)` in
  DW-Production pulls the four new files without conflict (deferred to a
  follow-up consumer-side PR)

milestones.title: v0.4.3
milestones.description: Integrity release: parquet col_select hotfix (#30) + cols_lenient feature (#31) + provenance sidecars (#32). Surfaced by the DW-Production NT reviewer-mode reproducibility audit (PR #133, May 2026).

milestones.title: v0.4.4
milestones.description: Carry-forward bugs from v0.4.3 audit + URL-allowlist helper

milestones.title: v0.5.0
milestones.description: Live dw_publish() submission branch (#15) + dw_regions() API redesign (#40)

milestones.title: v0.4.5
milestones.description: Naming cleanup (#42) + standalone-source pipe binding fix — RELEASED 2026-05-29 as v0.4.5

milestones.title: v0.4.6
milestones.description: Manifest + standalone-source UX cleanups (issues #51 .cso_require envelope on %>% gate; #52 manifest sha256 CRLF/LF normalisation)

_schema.purpose: Operator-committed snapshot of Teams deposit state per sector. Overwritten by the operator's snapshot script (not by collect.R).

fields.snapshot_taken_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the operator's last refresh
fields.snapshot_source: How the operator generated this snapshot (e.g. 'manual', 'teams_inventory.ps1')

<code>.deposited: boolean — whether the canonical file exists at the expected Teams path
<code>.rows: integer — observation count in the deposited file (null if not deposited)
<code>.indicators: integer — distinct indicator count (null if not deposited)
<code>.size_mb: numeric — file size on Teams (null if not deposited)
<code>.path_teams: string — canonical Teams path (relative to the OneDrive root)
<code>.deposit_date: YYYY-MM-DD — date producer last deposited (null if not deposited)

nt.notes: 1.16M rows x 40 indicators on Teams (operator-confirmed today)

hva.notes: 214k rows x 23 indicators (producer v6 baseline; reviewer-mode overwrite incident filed as cso-toolkit#54)

im.notes: not deposited to Teams (reviewer ran locally to repo)

ws.notes: not deposited to Teams (reviewer ran locally to repo; 3 outputs hhs/sch/hcf)

mnch.notes: 15k rows x 27 indicators on Teams; CRITICAL: producer writes to 011_rawdata (raw input dir) via write.csv, bypasses dw_save

cme.notes: no Teams deposit expected (sector publishes via separate cme regional-aggregate path)

ed.notes: 107k rows x 16 indicators on Teams (step 2/5 only; 3/5 blocked on WB API proxy)

wt.notes: 6 indicators in scope; pipeline BLOCKED on Java/xlsx + schema mismatch + missing population.rds

ecd.notes: 8 indicators in scope; pipeline BLOCKED on hardcoded mjugder paths + missing ECD.xlsx on Teams

fixes_applied: activated 19 commented-out stages (1a-1f imports + 2b-2j aggregations + 3a/3b targets + 4b format-merge)
openxlsx + countrycode + glue libraries added to lean driver (these are loaded by the profile in production, not present in lean-driver test mode)
toolkit_findings: dw_url_allowlist correctly blocked an unallowlisted URL (cmrs_series_indicators directory) with non-fatal warning

fixes_applied: dw_root() shim (cso-toolkit#53; toolkit gap to be fixed in v0.4.6)
dwMetaData path corrected to 01_dw_prep/010_metadata (per profile convention, not 012_codes/00_metadata)
toolkit_findings: CRITICAL: dw_save reviewer-mode mode-lock did NOT engage on OneDrive-mounted Teams Documents path. Producer's 2026-05-26 v6 output (67.09 MB, 214,674 rows) was OVERWRITTEN by this reviewer-mode run. Filed as cso-toolkit#54 (HIGH severity, v0.4.6)
Reviewer-mode mode-lock honored (output went to _local/im/output/ correctly because sector writes to a repo-relative path explicitly)
Reviewer-mode mode-lock honored (sector writes to _local/ws/output/ via dwWrkData)
ws.scope_note: 01214_ws_reagagg_hhs.R (regional aggregation) is Phase-2 work per the orchestrator comments and is NOT wired into the current conductor; not part of this replication scope

toolkit_findings: CRITICAL: sector bypasses cso-toolkit entirely. The script uses raw write.csv() to write directly into Teams RAW data directory at 011_rawdata/dw_mnch_aggregates.csv — this mixes derived output with canonical raw inputs. Reviewer-mode mode-lock cannot engage when sector code does not call dw_save.
mnch.blockers_for_dbm: Refactor 01_aggregate.R:128 from `write.csv(aggregate_export, file.path(teamsRawData, 'dw_mnch_aggregates.csv'), ...)` to `dw_save(aggregate_export, file.path(dwWrkData, '_local', 'mnch', 'output', 'dw_mnch_aggregates.csv'), sector = 'mnch')`. Sector lead must adopt the dw_io contract.

fixes_applied: Installed shinyregionalaggregate from unicef-drp/Shiny_regional_aggregate via remotes::install_github
toolkit_findings: CME bypasses cso-toolkit; uses data.table::fread + shinyregionalaggregate package directly. No mode-lock applies because no dw_save call. Output written to repo-local 013_wrkdata/ correctly via direct file path.
cme.blockers_for_dbm: If reviewer-mode contract should engage, sector lead must refactor 00_run_cme.R / cme_regional_aggregate.R to use dw_save and dw_use. Currently bypasses toolkit entirely. NOT a regression but a forward-investment for v0.4.x adoption.

ed.halt_reason: World Bank API requests dropped by UNICEF corporate proxy after 7 of 9 indicators successfully fetched. SE.LPV.PRIM, .FE, .MA, .LD, .LD.FE, .LD.MA, .SD all OK. .SD.FE failed even with 5 retries + 180s timeout.
fixes_applied: PR #151 merged (resolves merge-conflict parse blockers from prior smoke test)
Sector typo year_results -> edu_uis_year_results (02_aggregate_uis_sdg.R L155 + L282)
Uncommented WB API block at L59-71 of 03 (was completely commented out, leaving downstream lp_data reference undefined)
Staged pop_school_age.csv + ed_fls.csv + ed_webpage_templ_oos.xlsx from Teams to repo-local dwRawData/ed/
Direct httr fetch replaced wbstats call (wbstats default timeout too short)
toolkit_findings: STEP 2 wrote to Teams canonical path /060.DW-MASTER/01_dw_prep/013_wrkdata/ed/dw_ed_edu.csv — same mode-lock issue as HVA (cso-toolkit#54)
ed.blockers_for_dbm:
Three DBM-actionable items:
  1. World Bank API access: UNICEF corporate proxy drops api.worldbank.org requests after a threshold (7-8 requests). Options: (a) have IT allowlist api.worldbank.org; OR (b) cache WB data ONCE on a non-corporate network and commit as a frozen URL under 01_dw_prep/011_rawdata/_frozen/api.worldbank.org/; OR (c) run the ED pipeline outside the corporate network for STEP 3-5.
  2. Sector code bug — typo at 02_aggregate_uis_sdg.R L155 + L282 referencing undefined 'year_results' instead of 'edu_uis_year_results' (defined at L77).
  3. Sector code bug — 03_lp_wb_nat_aggr.R has the WB API fetch block (L59-71) commented out but references the resulting 'lp_data' variable downstream -> script crashes immediately if profile-side WB fetch isn't done.

wt.halt_reason_1: library(xlsx) requires Java runtime; Java not available in the reviewer environment
wt.halt_reason_2: After patching to library(openxlsx), the script's column selection (Region.Name, M49.Code.region., etc.) does not match the actual Teams xlsx structure (M49, Name, Ref_Area_Type, RC_Region, RC_UNSDCode, RC_RegionName, RC_Name across 7 cols x 1331 rows). xlsx::read.xlsx2 and openxlsx::read.xlsx have different default column-name handling AND the producer's working version of the xlsx may have a different schema
wt.halt_reason_3: Even with package + column-name issues fixed, the regional_classification.R script calls assert_required_files() listing population.rds at <teamsWrkData>/CPD/wt/ as required — that producer-side cached output does not exist on Teams. Producer must run a prior cme/cpd cache step first.
fixes_attempted: Installed xlsx + summarytools + xlsxjars + matrixStats + pander + rapportools (xlsx still fails on Java load)
Patched library(xlsx) -> library(openxlsx) in wt_dw.R + population.R
Wrote read.xlsx2 -> openxlsx::read.xlsx shim in driver
Removed rm(list=ls(all=TRUE)) in wt_dw.R that wipes driver-set globals
Set required_vars globals (teamsRawData, teamsWrkData, dwCodes, githubFolder) explicitly
wt.blockers_for_dbm:
Three DBM-actionable items:
  1. Java dependency — sector lead must port wt_dw.R + sub-scripts from xlsx to openxlsx (recommended path; openxlsx is pure-R, no Java dependency) so the pipeline runs in environments without a Java runtime.
  2. xlsx file schema mismatch — sector code expects columns Region.Name, M49.Code.region., X2025.Submission..if.data.submission.is.required. but the canonical Teams xlsx '9. CompositionOfRegions_RCs_20241219.xlsx' has columns M49, Name, Ref_Area_Type, RC_Region, RC_UNSDCode, RC_RegionName, RC_Name. Either the sector code is reading the wrong sheet/file, OR the producer's working xlsx differs from the Teams-deposited copy. Sector lead must reconcile.
  3. Upstream cache dependency — wt regional_classification.R requires <teamsWrkData>/CPD/wt/population.rds, a producer-side cached artifact not present on Teams. Producer must commit this artifact OR document how the reviewer regenerates it before wt can run.

ecd.halt_reason: ecd_dw.R hardcodes four directories to 'C:/Users/<producer>/OneDrive - UNICEF/Documents/Work materials NYHQ/...' (a producer-side personal OneDrive). None of the four paths resolve on the reviewer's machine. Additionally, library(xlsx) requires Java same as wt.
fixes_attempted: Identified hardcoded paths at L20-23 of ecd_dw.R (db_dir, wrk_dir, out_dir, reg_group)
Searched Teams for ECD.xlsx (referenced by the indicator scripts) — file does not exist on Teams (only ECD.csv variants exist under 011_rawdata/cpd/Old/ and 013_wrkdata/CPD/Old_*)
Searched for ecd-specific raw inputs — none located at the path the script expects
ecd.blockers_for_dbm:
Four DBM-actionable items:
  1. CRITICAL: Replace 4 hardcoded paths at ecd_dw.R L20-23 with profile-driven globals (dwRawData, dwCodes, dwWrkData, dwMetaData). The current code is portable only on one producer's machine.
  2. Deposit the producer-side 'ECD.xlsx' file (referenced by all 6 ECD_*_dw.R indicator scripts, sheet='early stimulation', startRow=15) onto Teams at a canonical path so reviewers can read it. The file is currently only on the producer-side OneDrive.
  3. Java install OR xlsx -> openxlsx port (same as wt).
  4. The script bypasses cso-toolkit entirely (no dw_use, dw_save). For v0.4.x compliance the sector lead must adopt dw_io contract.

actions.title: CME: adopt dw_io contract (forward-investment for v0.4.x)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: CME currently bypasses cso-toolkit entirely (data.table::fread + direct file paths); no mode-lock applies
actions.description:
Refactor 00_run_cme.R + cme_regional_aggregate.R to use dw_save / dw_use
so reviewer-mode mode-lock and dw_use provenance engage. Not a regression —
forward-investment for v0.4.x adoption.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#51: cso_require envelope
actions.description:
Add cso_require() envelope to standardise required-package loading across
sector drivers. Tracked at cso-toolkit#51.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#52: CRLF normalisation
actions.description:
Cross-platform CRLF normalisation hardening across cso-toolkit IO helpers.
Tracked at cso-toolkit#52.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#53: dw_root() wrapper / shim
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Reviewer had to write a dw_root() shim to replicate HVA; toolkit gap to be fixed in v0.4.6
actions.description:
HVA replication required a hand-written dw_root() shim. cso-toolkit should
expose this as a first-class API in v0.4.6.

actions.title: cso-toolkit#54: dw_save reviewer-mode mode-lock fails on OneDrive-mounted Teams paths
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Reviewer-mode runs OVERWROTE producer's v6 HVA output (67.09 MB, 214,674 rows); same issue affects ED step 2/5
actions.description:
HVA reviewer-mode replay overwrote the producer's 2026-05-26 v6 output on Teams.
dw_save mode-lock did not engage because the OneDrive-mounted Teams path
bypasses the path classifier. Affects hva and ed today; any sector that writes
to a Teams-canonical OneDrive path is at risk.

Tracked at cso-toolkit#54 (HIGH severity, v0.4.6 milestone).

actions.title: ECD: adopt cso-toolkit dw_io contract (currently bypasses entirely)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: ECD code uses neither dw_use nor dw_save; for v0.4.x compliance the sector lead must adopt dw_io contract
actions.description:
ECD bypasses cso-toolkit entirely (no dw_use, no dw_save). For v0.4.x
compliance the sector lead must refactor to use the dw_io contract.

actions.title: ECD: deposit producer-side ECD.xlsx onto Teams at a canonical path
actions.blocker_for_dbm: All 6 ECD_*_dw.R indicator scripts reference ECD.xlsx (sheet='early stimulation', startRow=15) which only exists on @mjugder's OneDrive
actions.description:
All 6 ECD_*_dw.R indicator scripts read ECD.xlsx, sheet='early stimulation',
startRow=15. That file is currently only on the producer's OneDrive — not on
Teams. Reviewer searched Teams; only ECD.csv variants exist under
011_rawdata/cpd/Old/ and 013_wrkdata/CPD/Old_*.

Deposit ECD.xlsx onto Teams at a canonical path so reviewers can read it.

actions.title: ECD: replace 4 hardcoded mjugder paths with profile globals
actions.blocker_for_dbm: ecd_dw.R L20-23 hardcode C:/Users/mjugder/... paths; portable only on the producer's machine
actions.description:
ecd_dw.R lines 20-23 hardcode four directories to the producer's personal
OneDrive ('C:/Users/mjugder/OneDrive - UNICEF/Documents/Work materials NYHQ/...').
Replace with profile-driven globals: dwRawData, dwCodes, dwWrkData, dwMetaData.

actions.title: ECD: install Java OR port xlsx -> openxlsx
actions.owner: UNICEF IT (Java) OR ECD sector lead (port)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: library(xlsx) requires Java; same problem as wt
actions.description:
Same blocker as wt-java-or-openxlsx. Either IT installs Java/JRE on the
reviewer machine, or sector lead ports from xlsx to openxlsx.

actions.title: ED: World Bank API blocked by UNICEF corporate proxy
actions.blocker_for_dbm: ED pipeline halts at step 3/5 (Learning Poverty + FLS) because proxy drops api.worldbank.org after ~7 requests

actions.title: ED: 03_lp_wb_nat_aggr.R has WB fetch block commented out (L59-71)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Downstream code references undefined lp_data; script crashes without profile-side WB fetch
actions.description:
03_lp_wb_nat_aggr.R has the WB API fetch block at L59-71 commented out, but
later code references the resulting 'lp_data' variable. Either uncomment the
block or guarantee profile-side fetch populates lp_data before this script runs.

actions.title: ED: typo year_results -> edu_uis_year_results (02_aggregate_uis_sdg.R)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Sector code references undefined 'year_results' (defined as edu_uis_year_results at L77)
actions.description:
02_aggregate_uis_sdg.R lines 155 and 282 reference `year_results` instead of
`edu_uis_year_results` (the actual variable defined at L77). Fix in sector code.

actions.title: MNCH: refactor 01_aggregate.R:128 raw write.csv to dw_save
actions.blocker_for_dbm: MNCH bypasses cso-toolkit entirely; reviewer-mode mode-lock cannot engage when sector code does not call dw_save
actions.description:
01_aggregate.R:128 currently writes:
  write.csv(aggregate_export, file.path(teamsRawData, 'dw_mnch_aggregates.csv'), ...)
This (a) bypasses dw_save (no mode-lock) and (b) writes a derived output
directly into the canonical RAW data directory (mixes raw + derived).
Should become:
  dw_save(aggregate_export,
          file.path(dwWrkData, '_local', 'mnch', 'output', 'dw_mnch_aggregates.csv'),
          sector = 'mnch')

actions.title: WT: install Java OR port xlsx -> openxlsx
actions.owner: UNICEF IT (Java) OR WT sector lead (port)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: library(xlsx) requires Java; not installed on reviewer laptop (AppLocker likely blocks)
actions.description:
Two paths:
  (1) IT installs Java/JRE (or AdoptOpenJDK) on the reviewer machine; OR
  (2) Sector lead ports wt_dw.R + sub-scripts from xlsx to openxlsx (pure-R,
      no Java dependency). Recommended path.

actions.title: WT: deposit upstream population.rds cache or document regeneration
actions.blocker_for_dbm: wt regional_classification.R requires <teamsWrkData>/CPD/wt/population.rds (producer-side artifact, not on Teams)
actions.description:
regional_classification.R calls assert_required_files() listing
population.rds at <teamsWrkData>/CPD/wt/ as required. That cached artifact
does not exist on Teams. Producer must either commit population.rds OR
document how a reviewer regenerates it before wt can run.

actions.title: WT: reconcile xlsx schema mismatch (sector code vs Teams file)
actions.blocker_for_dbm: Sector code expects different columns than the Teams xlsx file provides
actions.description:
Sector code expects columns: Region.Name, M49.Code.region.,
  X2025.Submission..if.data.submission.is.required.
Canonical Teams xlsx (9. CompositionOfRegions_RCs_20241219.xlsx) has columns:
  M49, Name, Ref_Area_Type, RC_Region, RC_UNSDCode, RC_RegionName, RC_Name
Either the sector code is reading the wrong sheet/file, OR the producer's
working xlsx differs from the Teams-deposited copy. Sector lead must reconcile.
